verses.ar: وقالَ: «أيُّها الرَّبُّ إلهُ سَيِّدي إبراهيمَ، أوقِعْ أمامي أرجوكَ اليَومَ واصنَعْ وَفاءً مَعَ سَيِّدي إبراهيمَ.

verses.he: הִנֵּ֛ה אָנֹכִ֥י נִצָּ֖ב עַל עֵ֣ין הַ/מָּ֑יִם וּ/בְנוֹת֙ אַנְשֵׁ֣י הָ/עִ֔יר יֹצְאֹ֖ת לִ/שְׁאֹ֥ב מָֽיִם
verses.en: Behold, I am standing by the spring of water, and the daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water.
verses.ar: ها أنا واقِفٌ عِندَ عَينِ الماءِ وبَناتُ أهلِ المَدينَةِ خارِجاتٌ لِيَستَقينَ ماءً.

verses.he: וְ/הָיָ֣ה הַֽ/נַּעֲרָ֗ אֲשֶׁ֨ר אֹמַ֤ר אֵלֶ֨י/הָ֙ הַטִּי נָ֤א כַדֵּ/ךְ֙ וְ/אֶשְׁתֶּ֔ה וְ/אָמְרָ֣ה שְׁתֵ֔ה וְ/גַם גְּמַלֶּ֖י/ךָ אַשְׁקֶ֑ה אֹתָ֤/הּ הֹכַ֨חְתָּ֙ לְ/עַבְדְּ/ךָ֣ לְ/יִצְחָ֔ק וּ/בָ֣/הּ אֵדַ֔ע כִּי עָשִׂ֥יתָ חֶ֖סֶד עִם אֲדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: And let it be that the young woman to whom I shall say, 'Incline, I pray, your jar that I may drink,' and she says, 'Drink, and also your camels I shall water' — her You have appointed for Your servant, for Yitschak; and by her I shall know that You have done loyal-kindness with my master."
verses.ar: ولتَكُنِ الفَتاةُ الَّتي أقولُ لَها: 'أميلي أرجوكِ جَرَّتَكِ لأشرَبَ' فتَقولُ: 'اشرَبْ وجِمالَكَ أيضًا أسقي' — هِيَ الَّتي عَيَّنتَها لِعَبدِكَ لإسحاقَ؛ وبِها أعلَمُ أنَّكَ صَنَعتَ وَفاءً مَعَ سَيِّدي.»

verses.he: וַֽ/יְהִי ה֗וּא טֶרֶם֮ כִּלָּ֣ה לְ/דַבֵּר֒ וְ/הִנֵּ֧ה רִבְקָ֣ה יֹצֵ֗את אֲשֶׁ֤ר יֻלְּדָה֙ לִ/בְתוּאֵ֣ל בֶּן מִלְכָּ֔ה אֵ֥שֶׁת נָח֖וֹר אֲחִ֣י אַבְרָהָ֑ם וְ/כַדָּ֖/הּ עַל שִׁכְמָֽ/הּ
verses.en: And it was: before he had finished speaking — and behold, Rivqah was coming out, who was born to Betu'el son of Milkah, the woman of Nachor, brother of Avraham; and her jar was upon her shoulder.
verses.ar: وكانَ قَبلَ أن يَفرَغَ مِنَ الكَلامِ — وإذا رِفقَةُ خارِجَةٌ، الَّتي وُلِدَت لِبَتوئيلَ بنِ مِلكَةَ امرَأةِ ناحورَ أخي إبراهيمَ؛ وجَرَّتُها على كَتِفِها.

verses.he: וְ/הַֽ/נַּעֲרָ֗ טֹבַ֤ת מַרְאֶה֙ מְאֹ֔ד בְּתוּלָ֕ה וְ/אִ֖ישׁ לֹ֣א יְדָעָ֑/הּ וַ/תֵּ֣רֶד הָ/עַ֔יְנָ/ה וַ/תְּמַלֵּ֥א כַדָּ֖/הּ וַ/תָּֽעַל
verses.en: And the young woman was very good of appearance, a maiden, and no man had known her; and she went down to the spring and filled her jar and came up.
verses.ar: والفَتاةُ كانَت حَسَنَةَ المَنظَرِ جِدًّا، عَذراءُ لَم يَعرِفها رَجُلٌ؛ فنَزَلَت إلى العَينِ ومَلَأت جَرَّتَها وصَعِدَت.

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֥רָץ הָ/עֶ֖בֶד לִ/קְרָאתָ֑/הּ וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר הַגְמִיאִ֥י/נִי נָ֛א מְעַט מַ֖יִם מִ/כַּדֵּֽ/ךְ
verses.en: And the servant ran to meet her and said: "Let me sip, I pray, a little water from your jar."
verses.ar: فرَكَضَ العَبدُ لِلِقائِها وقالَ: «أسقيني أرجوكِ قَليلَ ماءٍ مِن جَرَّتِكِ.»

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֖אמֶר שְׁתֵ֣ה אֲדֹנִ֑/י וַ/תְּמַהֵ֗ר וַ/תֹּ֧רֶד כַּדָּ֛/הּ עַל יָדָ֖/הּ וַ/תַּשְׁקֵֽ/הוּ
verses.en: And she said: "Drink, my lord." And she hastened and lowered her jar upon her hand and gave him drink.
verses.ar: فقالَت: «اشرَبْ يا سَيِّدي.» وأسرَعَت وأنزَلَت جَرَّتَها على يَدِها وسَقَتهُ.

verses.he: וַ/תְּכַ֖ל לְ/הַשְׁקֹת֑/וֹ וַ/תֹּ֗אמֶר גַּ֤ם לִ/גְמַלֶּ֨י/ךָ֙ אֶשְׁאָ֔ב עַ֥ד אִם כִּלּ֖וּ לִ/שְׁתֹּֽת
verses.en: And she finished giving him drink and said: "Also for your camels I shall draw, until they have finished drinking."
verses.ar: وفَرَغَت مِن سَقيِهِ فقالَت: «ولِجِمالِكَ أيضًا أستَقي حَتّى يَفرَغوا مِنَ الشُّربِ.»

verses.he: וַ/תְּמַהֵ֗ר וַ/תְּעַ֤ר כַּדָּ/הּ֙ אֶל הַ/שֹּׁ֔קֶת וַ/תָּ֥רָץ ע֛וֹד אֶֽל הַ/בְּאֵ֖ר לִ/שְׁאֹ֑ב וַ/תִּשְׁאַ֖ב לְ/כָל גְּמַלָּֽי/ו
verses.en: And she hastened and emptied her jar into the trough and ran again to the well to draw, and drew for all his camels.
verses.ar: فأسرَعَت وأفرَغَت جَرَّتَها في الحَوضِ ورَكَضَت أيضًا إلى البِئرِ لِتَستَقيَ، واستَقَت لِكُلِّ جِمالِهِ.

verses.he: וְ/הָ/אִ֥ישׁ מִשְׁתָּאֵ֖ה לָ֑/הּ מַחֲרִ֕ישׁ לָ/דַ֗עַת הַֽ/הִצְלִ֧יחַ יְהוָ֛ה דַּרְכּ֖/וֹ אִם לֹֽא
verses.en: And the man was gazing at her in silence, waiting to know whether YHWH had made his way prosper or not.
verses.ar: والرَّجُلُ يَتَأمَّلُها صامِتًا لِيَعلَمَ هَل أنجَحَ الرَّبُّ طَريقَهُ أم لا.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֗י כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֨ר כִּלּ֤וּ הַ/גְּמַלִּים֙ לִ/שְׁתּ֔וֹת וַ/יִּקַּ֤ח הָ/אִישׁ֙ נֶ֣זֶם זָהָ֔ב בֶּ֖קַע מִשְׁקָל֑/וֹ וּ/שְׁנֵ֤י צְמִידִים֙ עַל יָדֶ֔י/הָ עֲשָׂרָ֥ה זָהָ֖ב מִשְׁקָלָֽ/ם
verses.en: And it was, when the camels had finished drinking, that the man took a golden ring, a half-shekel its weight, and two bracelets for her hands, ten of gold their weight.
verses.ar: وكانَ حينَ فَرَغَتِ الجِمالُ مِنَ الشُّربِ أنَّ الرَّجُلَ أخَذَ حَلقَةَ ذَهَبٍ نِصفَ شاقِلٍ وَزنُها، وسِوارَينِ لِيَدَيها عَشَرَةً مِن ذَهَبٍ وَزنُهُما.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר֙ בַּת מִ֣י אַ֔תְּ הַגִּ֥ידִי נָ֖א לִ֑/י הֲ/יֵ֧שׁ בֵּית אָבִ֛י/ךְ מָק֥וֹם לָ֖/נוּ לָ/לִֽין
verses.en: And he said: "Whose daughter are you? Tell me, I pray: is there room in the house of your father for us to lodge?"
verses.ar: وقالَ: «بِنتُ مَن أنتِ؟ أخبِريني أرجوكِ: أفي بَيتِ أبيكِ مَكانٌ لَنا لِنَبيتَ؟»

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֔י/ו בַּת בְּתוּאֵ֖ל אָנֹ֑כִי בֶּן מִלְכָּ֕ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָלְדָ֖ה לְ/נָחֽוֹר
verses.en: And she said to him: "I am the daughter of Betu'el, son of Milkah, whom she bore to Nachor."
verses.ar: فقالَت لَهُ: «أنا بِنتُ بَتوئيلَ بنِ مِلكَةَ الَّتي وَلَدَتهُ لِناحورَ.»

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֔י/ו גַּם תֶּ֥בֶן גַּם מִסְפּ֖וֹא רַ֣ב עִמָּ֑/נוּ גַּם מָק֖וֹם לָ/לֽוּן
verses.en: And she said to him: "Both straw and fodder are plentiful with us, and also a place to lodge."
verses.ar: وقالَت لَهُ: «تِبنٌ وعَلَفٌ كَثيرٌ عِندَنا، وأيضًا مَكانٌ لِلمَبيتِ.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּקֹּ֣ד הָ/אִ֔ישׁ וַ/יִּשְׁתַּ֖חוּ לַֽ/יהוָֽה
verses.en: And the man bowed his head and bowed down to YHWH.
verses.ar: فانحَنى الرَّجُلُ وسَجَدَ لِلرَّبِّ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר בָּר֤וּךְ יְהוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵי֙ אֲדֹנִ֣/י אַבְרָהָ֔ם אֲ֠שֶׁר לֹֽא עָזַ֥ב חַסְדּ֛/וֹ וַ/אֲמִתּ֖/וֹ מֵ/עִ֣ם אֲדֹנִ֑/י אָנֹכִ֗י בַּ/דֶּ֨רֶךְ֙ נָחַ֣/נִי יְהוָ֔ה בֵּ֖ית אֲחֵ֥י אֲדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: And he said: "Blessed be YHWH, God of my master Avraham, who has not forsaken His loyal-kindness and His faithfulness from my master. As for me — on the way YHWH has led me to the house of my master's brothers."
verses.ar: وقالَ: «مُبارَكٌ الرَّبُّ إلهُ سَيِّدي إبراهيمَ الَّذي لَم يَترُكْ وَفاءَهُ وأمانَتَهُ عَن سَيِّدي. أمّا أنا — فَفي الطَّريقِ هَداني الرَّبُّ إلى بَيتِ إخوَةِ سَيِّدي.»

verses.en: And the young woman ran and told her mother's household these things.
verses.ar: فرَكَضَتِ الفَتاةُ وأخبَرَت بَيتَ أُمِّها بِهذِهِ الأُمورِ.

verses.he: וּ/לְ/רִבְקָ֥ה אָ֖ח וּ/שְׁמ֣/וֹ לָבָ֑ן וַ/יָּ֨רָץ לָבָ֧ן אֶל הָ/אִ֛ישׁ הַ/ח֖וּצָ/ה אֶל הָ/עָֽיִן
verses.en: And Rivqah had a brother, and his name was Lavan; and Lavan ran out unto the man, to the spring.
verses.ar: ولِرِفقَةَ أخٌ اسمُهُ لابانُ؛ فرَكَضَ لابانُ إلى الرَّجُلِ خارِجًا إلى العَينِ.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֣י כִּ/רְאֹ֣ת אֶת הַ/נֶּ֗זֶם וְֽ/אֶת הַ/צְּמִדִים֮ עַל יְדֵ֣י אֲחֹת/וֹ֒ וּ/כְ/שָׁמְע֗/וֹ אֶת דִּבְרֵ֞י רִבְקָ֤ה אֲחֹת/וֹ֙ לֵ/אמֹ֔ר כֹּֽה דִבֶּ֥ר אֵלַ֖/י הָ/אִ֑ישׁ וַ/יָּבֹא֙ אֶל הָ/אִ֔ישׁ וְ/הִנֵּ֛ה עֹמֵ֥ד עַל הַ/גְּמַלִּ֖ים עַל הָ/עָֽיִן
verses.en: And it was, when he saw the ring and the bracelets upon the hands of his sister, and when he heard the words of Rivqah his sister, saying, "Thus the man spoke to me" — that he came unto the man; and behold, he was standing by the camels at the spring.
verses.ar: وكانَ حينَ رَأى الحَلقَةَ والسِّوارَينِ على يَدَي أُختِهِ، وحينَ سَمِعَ كَلامَ رِفقَةَ أُختِهِ قائِلَةً: «هكَذا كَلَّمَني الرَّجُلُ» — أنَّهُ جاءَ إلى الرَّجُلِ؛ وها هُوَ واقِفٌ عِندَ الجِمالِ عِندَ العَينِ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר בּ֖וֹא בְּר֣וּךְ יְהוָ֑ה לָ֤/מָּה תַעֲמֹד֙ בַּ/ח֔וּץ וְ/אָנֹכִי֙ פִּנִּ֣יתִי הַ/בַּ֔יִת וּ/מָק֖וֹם לַ/גְּמַלִּֽים
verses.en: And he said: "Come, blessed of YHWH! Why do you stand outside? For I have cleared the house, and a place for the camels."
verses.ar: فقالَ: «ادخُلْ يا مُبارَكَ الرَّبِّ! لِمَ تَقِفُ خارِجًا وأنا قَد هَيَّأتُ البَيتَ ومَكانًا لِلجِمالِ؟»

verses.he: וַ/יָּבֹ֤א הָ/אִישׁ֙ הַ/בַּ֔יְתָ/ה וַ/יְפַתַּ֖ח הַ/גְּמַלִּ֑ים וַ/יִּתֵּ֨ן תֶּ֤בֶן וּ/מִסְפּוֹא֙ לַ/גְּמַלִּ֔ים וּ/מַ֨יִם֙ לִ/רְחֹ֣ץ רַגְלָ֔י/ו וְ/רַגְלֵ֥י הָ/אֲנָשִׁ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר אִתּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And the man came into the house; and he unloaded the camels and gave straw and fodder to the camels, and water to wash his feet and the feet of the men who were with him.
verses.ar: فدَخَلَ الرَّجُلُ البَيتَ وحَلَّ عَنِ الجِمالِ؛ وأعطى تِبنًا وعَلَفًا لِلجِمالِ وماءً لِغَسلِ رِجلَيهِ وأرجُلِ الرِّجالِ الَّذينَ مَعَهُ.

verses.he: ו/יישם וַ/יּוּשַׂ֤ם לְ/פָנָי/ו֙ לֶ/אֱכֹ֔ל וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר֙ לֹ֣א אֹכַ֔ל עַ֥ד אִם דִּבַּ֖רְתִּי דְּבָרָ֑/י וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר דַּבֵּֽר
verses.en: And food was set before him to eat; but he said: "I shall not eat until I have spoken my words." And he said: "Speak."
verses.ar: ووُضِعَ أمامَهُ طَعامٌ لِيَأكُلَ؛ فقالَ: «لا آكُلُ حَتّى أتَكَلَّمَ كَلامي.» فقالَ: «تَكَلَّمْ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאמַ֑ר עֶ֥בֶד אַבְרָהָ֖ם אָנֹֽכִי
verses.en: And he said: "I am the servant of Avraham.
verses.ar: فقالَ: «أنا عَبدُ إبراهيمَ.

verses.he: וַ/יהוָ֞ה בֵּרַ֧ךְ אֶת אֲדֹנִ֛/י מְאֹ֖ד וַ/יִּגְדָּ֑ל וַ/יִּתֶּן ל֞/וֹ צֹ֤אן וּ/בָקָר֙ וְ/כֶ֣סֶף וְ/זָהָ֔ב וַ/עֲבָדִם֙ וּ/שְׁפָחֹ֔ת וּ/גְמַלִּ֖ים וַ/חֲמֹרִֽים
verses.en: And YHWH has blessed my master greatly, and he has become great; and He has given him flocks and herds, and silver and gold, and servants and maidservants, and camels and donkeys.
verses.ar: والرَّبُّ بارَكَ سَيِّدي جِدًّا فعَظُمَ؛ وأعطاهُ غَنَمًا وبَقَرًا وفِضَّةً وذَهَبًا وعَبيدًا وإماءً وجِمالًا وحَميرًا.

verses.he: וַ/תֵּ֡לֶד שָׂרָה֩ אֵ֨שֶׁת אֲדֹנִ֥/י בֵן֙ לַֽ/אדֹנִ֔/י אַחֲרֵ֖י זִקְנָתָ֑/הּ וַ/יִּתֶּן לּ֖/וֹ אֶת כָּל אֲשֶׁר לֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Sarah, the woman of my master, bore a son to my master after her old age; and he has given him all that is his.
verses.ar: ووَلَدَت سارَةُ امرَأةُ سَيِّدي لِسَيِّدي ابنًا بَعدَ شَيخوخَتِها؛ فأعطاهُ كُلَّ ما لَهُ.

verses.he: וַ/יַּשְׁבִּעֵ֥/נִי אֲדֹנִ֖/י לֵ/אמֹ֑ר לֹא תִקַּ֤ח אִשָּׁה֙ לִ/בְנִ֔/י מִ/בְּנוֹת֙ הַֽ/כְּנַעֲנִ֔י אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָנֹכִ֖י יֹשֵׁ֥ב בְּ/אַרְצֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And my master made me swear, saying: 'You shall not take a woman for my son from the daughters of the Kena'ani, in whose land I dwell.
verses.ar: وحَلَّفَني سَيِّدي قائِلًا: 'لا تَأخُذْ امرَأةً لِابني مِن بَناتِ الكَنعانِيّينَ الَّذينَ أسكُنُ في أرضِهِم.

verses.he: אִם לֹ֧א אֶל בֵּית אָבִ֛/י תֵּלֵ֖ךְ וְ/אֶל מִשְׁפַּחְתִּ֑/י וְ/לָקַחְתָּ֥ אִשָּׁ֖ה לִ/בְנִֽ/י
verses.en: Rather, to the house of my father you shall go, and to my family, and take a woman for my son.'
verses.ar: بَل إلى بَيتِ أبي تَذهَبُ وإلى عَشيرَتي وتَأخُذُ امرَأةً لِابني.'

verses.he: וָ/אֹמַ֖ר אֶל אֲדֹנִ֑/י אֻלַ֛י לֹא תֵלֵ֥ךְ הָ/אִשָּׁ֖ה אַחֲרָֽ/י
verses.en: And I said to my master: 'Perhaps the woman will not go after me.'
verses.ar: فقُلتُ لِسَيِّدي: 'رُبَّما لا تَسيرُ المَرأةُ وَرائي.'

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר אֵלָ֑/י יְהוָ֞ה אֲשֶׁר הִתְהַלַּ֣כְתִּי לְ/פָנָ֗י/ו יִשְׁלַ֨ח מַלְאָכ֤/וֹ אִתָּ/ךְ֙ וְ/הִצְלִ֣יחַ דַּרְכֶּ֔/ךָ וְ/לָקַחְתָּ֤ אִשָּׁה֙ לִ/בְנִ֔/י מִ/מִּשְׁפַּחְתִּ֖/י וּ/מִ/בֵּ֥ית אָבִֽ/י
verses.en: And he said to me: 'The YHWH, before whom I have walked, shall send His messenger with you and make your way prosper; and you shall take a woman for my son from my family and from the house of my father.
verses.ar: فقالَ لي: 'الرَّبُّ الَّذي سِرتُ أمامَهُ يُرسِلُ مَلاكَهُ مَعَكَ ويُنجِحُ طَريقَكَ؛ وتَأخُذُ امرَأةً لِابني مِن عَشيرَتي ومِن بَيتِ أبي.

verses.he: אָ֤ז תִּנָּקֶה֙ מֵ/אָ֣לָתִ֔/י כִּ֥י תָב֖וֹא אֶל מִשְׁפַּחְתִּ֑/י וְ/אִם לֹ֤א יִתְּנוּ֙ לָ֔/ךְ וְ/הָיִ֥יתָ נָקִ֖י מֵ/אָלָתִֽ/י
verses.en: Then you shall be clear of my oath: when you come to my family, and if they do not give her to you, then you shall be clear of my oath.'
verses.ar: حينَئذٍ تَكونُ بَريئًا مِن قَسَمي: حينَ تَأتي إلى عَشيرَتي، وإن لَم يُعطوها لَكَ تَكونُ بَريئًا مِن قَسَمي.'

verses.he: וָ/אָבֹ֥א הַ/יּ֖וֹם אֶל הָ/עָ֑יִן וָ/אֹמַ֗ר יְהוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵי֙ אֲדֹנִ֣/י אַבְרָהָ֔ם אִם יֶשְׁ/ךָ נָּא֙ מַצְלִ֣יחַ דַּרְכִּ֔/י אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָנֹכִ֖י הֹלֵ֥ךְ עָלֶֽי/הָ
verses.en: And I came today to the spring and said: 'O YHWH, God of my master Avraham, if You are making my way prosper, upon which I walk —
verses.ar: فجِئتُ اليَومَ إلى العَينِ وقُلتُ: 'أيُّها الرَّبُّ إلهُ سَيِّدي إبراهيمَ، إن كُنتَ مُنجِحًا طَريقي الَّذي أسيرُ فيهِ —

verses.he: הִנֵּ֛ה אָנֹכִ֥י נִצָּ֖ב עַל עֵ֣ין הַ/מָּ֑יִם וְ/הָיָ֤ה הָֽ/עַלְמָה֙ הַ/יֹּצֵ֣את לִ/שְׁאֹ֔ב וְ/אָמַרְתִּ֣י אֵלֶ֔י/הָ הַשְׁקִֽי/נִי נָ֥א מְעַט מַ֖יִם מִ/כַּדֵּֽ/ךְ
verses.en: behold, I am standing by the spring of water; and let it be that the maiden who comes out to draw, and I say to her, "Give me, I pray, a little water to drink from your jar,"
verses.ar: ها أنا واقِفٌ عِندَ عَينِ الماءِ؛ ولتَكُنِ الفَتاةُ الَّتي تَخرُجُ لِتَستَقيَ فأقولُ لَها: أسقيني أرجوكِ قَليلَ ماءٍ مِن جَرَّتِكِ،

verses.he: וְ/אָמְרָ֤ה אֵלַ/י֙ גַּם אַתָּ֣ה שְׁתֵ֔ה וְ/גַ֥ם לִ/גְמַלֶּ֖י/ךָ אֶשְׁאָ֑ב הִ֣וא הָֽ/אִשָּׁ֔ה אֲשֶׁר הֹכִ֥יחַ יְהוָ֖ה לְ/בֶן אֲדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: and she says to me, "Both you drink, and also for your camels I shall draw" — she is the woman whom YHWH has appointed for the son of my master.'
verses.ar: فتَقولُ لي: اشرَبْ أنتَ ولِجِمالِكَ أيضًا أستَقي — هِيَ المَرأةُ الَّتي عَيَّنَها الرَّبُّ لِابنِ سَيِّدي.'

verses.he: אֲנִי֩ טֶ֨רֶם אֲכַלֶּ֜ה לְ/דַבֵּ֣ר אֶל לִבִּ֗/י וְ/הִנֵּ֨ה רִבְקָ֤ה יֹצֵאת֙ וְ/כַדָּ֣/הּ עַל שִׁכְמָ֔/הּ וַ/תֵּ֥רֶד הָ/עַ֖יְנָ/ה וַ/תִּשְׁאָ֑ב וָ/אֹמַ֥ר אֵלֶ֖י/הָ הַשְׁקִ֥י/נִי נָֽא
verses.en: Before I had finished speaking to my heart — and behold, Rivqah was coming out, and her jar upon her shoulder; and she went down to the spring and drew. And I said to her: 'Give me drink, I pray.'
verses.ar: قَبلَ أن أفرَغَ مِنَ الكَلامِ في قَلبي — وإذا رِفقَةُ خارِجَةٌ وجَرَّتُها على كَتِفِها؛ فنَزَلَت إلى العَينِ واستَقَت. فقُلتُ لَها: 'أسقيني أرجوكِ.'

verses.he: וַ/תְּמַהֵ֗ר וַ/תּ֤וֹרֶד כַּדָּ/הּ֙ מֵֽ/עָלֶ֔י/הָ וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר שְׁתֵ֔ה וְ/גַם גְּמַלֶּ֖י/ךָ אַשְׁקֶ֑ה וָ/אֵ֕שְׁתְּ וְ/גַ֥ם הַ/גְּמַלִּ֖ים הִשְׁקָֽתָה
verses.en: And she hastened and lowered her jar from upon her and said: 'Drink, and also your camels I shall water.' And I drank, and also the camels she watered.
verses.ar: فأسرَعَت وأنزَلَت جَرَّتَها عَنها وقالَت: 'اشرَبْ ولِجِمالِكَ أيضًا أسقي.' فشَرِبتُ والجِمالَ أيضًا سَقَت.

verses.he: וָ/אֶשְׁאַ֣ל אֹתָ֗/הּ וָ/אֹמַר֮ בַּת מִ֣י אַתְּ֒ וַ/תֹּ֗אמֶר בַּת בְּתוּאֵל֙ בֶּן נָח֔וֹר אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָֽלְדָה לּ֖/וֹ מִלְכָּ֑ה וָ/אָשִׂ֤ם הַ/נֶּ֨זֶם֙ עַל אַפָּ֔/הּ וְ/הַ/צְּמִידִ֖ים עַל יָדֶֽי/הָ
verses.en: And I asked her and said: 'Whose daughter are you?' And she said: 'The daughter of Betu'el, son of Nachor, whom Milkah bore to him.' And I placed the ring upon her nose and the bracelets upon her hands.
verses.ar: فسَألتُها وقُلتُ: 'بِنتُ مَن أنتِ؟' فقالَت: 'بِنتُ بَتوئيلَ بنِ ناحورَ الَّذي وَلَدَتهُ لَهُ مِلكَةُ.' فوَضَعتُ الحَلقَةَ على أنفِها والسِّوارَينِ على يَدَيها.

verses.he: וָ/אֶקֹּ֥ד וָֽ/אֶשְׁתַּחֲוֶ֖ה לַ/יהוָ֑ה וָ/אֲבָרֵ֗ךְ אֶת יְהוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵי֙ אֲדֹנִ֣/י אַבְרָהָ֔ם אֲשֶׁ֤ר הִנְחַ֨/נִי֙ בְּ/דֶ֣רֶךְ אֱמֶ֔ת לָ/קַ֛חַת אֶת בַּת אֲחִ֥י אֲדֹנִ֖/י לִ/בְנֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And I bowed my head and bowed down to YHWH, and I blessed YHWH, God of my master Avraham, who had led me on the way of truth to take the daughter of the brother of my master for his son.
verses.ar: وانحَنَيتُ وسَجَدتُ لِلرَّبِّ، وبارَكتُ الرَّبَّ إلهَ سَيِّدي إبراهيمَ الَّذي هَداني في طَريقِ الحَقِّ لآخُذَ بِنتَ أخي سَيِّدي لِابنِهِ.

verses.he: וְ֠/עַתָּה אִם יֶשְׁ/כֶ֨ם עֹשִׂ֜ים חֶ֧סֶד וֶֽ/אֱמֶ֛ת אֶת אֲדֹנִ֖/י הַגִּ֣ידוּ לִ֑/י וְ/אִם לֹ֕א הַגִּ֣ידוּ לִ֔/י וְ/אֶפְנֶ֥ה עַל יָמִ֖ין א֥וֹ עַל שְׂמֹֽאל
verses.en: And now, if you will do loyal-kindness and faithfulness with my master, tell me; and if not, tell me, that I may turn to the right or to the left."
verses.ar: والآنَ إن كُنتُم صانِعينَ وَفاءً وأمانَةً مَعَ سَيِّدي فأخبِروني؛ وإلّا فأخبِروني لأتَوَجَّهَ يَمينًا أو شَمالًا.»

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֨עַן לָבָ֤ן וּ/בְתוּאֵל֙ וַ/יֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ מֵ/יְהוָ֖ה יָצָ֣א הַ/דָּבָ֑ר לֹ֥א נוּכַ֛ל דַּבֵּ֥ר אֵלֶ֖י/ךָ רַ֥ע אוֹ טֽוֹב
verses.en: And Lavan and Betu'el answered and said: "From YHWH the thing has gone forth; we are not able to speak to you evil or good.
verses.ar: فأجابَ لابانُ وبَتوئيلُ وقالا: «مِنَ الرَّبِّ خَرَجَ الأمرُ؛ لا نَستَطيعُ أن نَقولَ لَكَ شَرًّا أو خَيرًا.

verses.he: הִנֵּֽה רִבְקָ֥ה לְ/פָנֶ֖י/ךָ קַ֣ח וָ/לֵ֑ךְ וּ/תְהִ֤י אִשָּׁה֙ לְ/בֶן אֲדֹנֶ֔י/ךָ כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֖ר דִּבֶּ֥ר יְהוָֽה
verses.en: Behold, Rivqah is before you; take her and go, and let her be a woman for the son of your master, as YHWH has spoken."
verses.ar: ها رِفقَةُ أمامَكَ؛ خُذها واذهَبْ ولتَكُنِ امرَأةً لِابنِ سَيِّدِكَ كَما تَكَلَّمَ الرَّبُّ.»

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֕י כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר שָׁמַ֛ע עֶ֥בֶד אַבְרָהָ֖ם אֶת דִּבְרֵי/הֶ֑ם וַ/יִּשְׁתַּ֥חוּ אַ֖רְצָ/ה לַֽ/יהוָֽה
verses.en: And it was, when the servant of Avraham heard their words, that he bowed down to the earth before YHWH.
verses.ar: وكانَ حينَ سَمِعَ عَبدُ إبراهيمَ كَلامَهُم أنَّهُ سَجَدَ إلى الأرضِ لِلرَّبِّ.

verses.he: וַ/יּוֹצֵ֨א הָ/עֶ֜בֶד כְּלֵי כֶ֨סֶף וּ/כְלֵ֤י זָהָב֙ וּ/בְגָדִ֔ים וַ/יִּתֵּ֖ן לְ/רִבְקָ֑ה וּ/מִ֨גְדָּנֹ֔ת נָתַ֥ן לְ/אָחִ֖י/הָ וּ/לְ/אִמָּֽ/הּ
verses.en: And the servant brought out vessels of silver and vessels of gold and garments, and gave them to Rivqah; and precious gifts he gave to her brother and to her mother.
verses.ar: وأخرَجَ العَبدُ آنِيَةَ فِضَّةٍ وآنِيَةَ ذَهَبٍ وثِيابًا وأعطاها لِرِفقَةَ؛ وتُحَفًا أعطى لأخيها ولأُمِّها.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאכְל֣וּ וַ/יִּשְׁתּ֗וּ ה֛וּא וְ/הָ/אֲנָשִׁ֥ים אֲשֶׁר עִמּ֖/וֹ וַ/יָּלִ֑ינוּ וַ/יָּק֣וּמוּ בַ/בֹּ֔קֶר וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר שַׁלְּחֻ֥/נִי לַֽ/אדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: And they ate and drank, he and the men who were with him, and they lodged. And they rose in the morning, and he said: "Send me to my master."
verses.ar: فأكَلوا وشَرِبوا هُوَ والرِّجالُ الَّذينَ مَعَهُ وباتوا. وقاموا في الصَّباحِ فقالَ: «أرسِلوني إلى سَيِّدي.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אָחִ֨י/הָ֙ וְ/אִמָּ֔/הּ תֵּשֵׁ֨ב הַ/נַּעֲרָ֥ אִתָּ֛/נוּ יָמִ֖ים א֣וֹ עָשׂ֑וֹר אַחַ֖ר תֵּלֵֽךְ
verses.en: And her brother and her mother said: "Let the young woman stay with us some days, or ten; after that she shall go."
verses.ar: فقالَ أخوها وأُمُّها: «لِتُقِمِ الفَتاةُ عِندَنا أيّامًا أو عَشَرَةً؛ بَعدَ ذلِكَ تَذهَبُ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אֲלֵ/הֶם֙ אַל תְּאַחֲר֣וּ אֹתִ֔/י וַֽ/יהוָ֖ה הִצְלִ֣יחַ דַּרְכִּ֑/י שַׁלְּח֕וּ/נִי וְ/אֵלְכָ֖ה לַֽ/אדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: And he said to them: "Do not delay me, for YHWH has made my way prosper. Send me away, that I may go to my master."
verses.ar: فقالَ لَهُم: «لا تُؤَخِّروني والرَّبُّ قَد أنجَحَ طَريقي. أرسِلوني لأذهَبَ إلى سَيِّدي.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאמְר֖וּ נִקְרָ֣א לַֽ/נַּעֲרָ֑ וְ/נִשְׁאֲלָ֖ה אֶת פִּֽי/הָ
verses.en: And they said: "Let us call the young woman and ask her own mouth."
verses.ar: فقالوا: «نَدعو الفَتاةَ ونَسألُ فاها.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרְא֤וּ לְ/רִבְקָה֙ וַ/יֹּאמְר֣וּ אֵלֶ֔י/הָ הֲ/תֵלְכִ֖י עִם הָ/אִ֣ישׁ הַ/זֶּ֑ה וַ/תֹּ֖אמֶר אֵלֵֽךְ
verses.en: And they called Rivqah and said to her: "Will you go with this man?" And she said: "I will go."
verses.ar: فدَعَوا رِفقَةَ وقالوا لَها: «أتَذهَبينَ مَعَ هذا الرَّجُلِ؟» فقالَت: «أذهَبُ.»

verses.he: וַֽ/יְשַׁלְּח֛וּ אֶת רִבְקָ֥ה אֲחֹתָ֖/ם וְ/אֶת מֵנִקְתָּ֑/הּ וְ/אֶת עֶ֥בֶד אַבְרָהָ֖ם וְ/אֶת אֲנָשָֽׁי/ו
verses.en: And they sent away Rivqah their sister and her nurse, and the servant of Avraham and his men.
verses.ar: فأرسَلوا رِفقَةَ أُختَهُم ومُرضِعَتَها وعَبدَ إبراهيمَ ورِجالَهُ.

verses.he: וַ/יְבָרֲכ֤וּ אֶת רִבְקָה֙ וַ/יֹּ֣אמְרוּ לָ֔/הּ אֲחֹתֵ֕/נוּ אַ֥תְּ הֲיִ֖י לְ/אַלְפֵ֣י רְבָבָ֑ה וְ/יִירַ֣שׁ זַרְעֵ֔/ךְ אֵ֖ת שַׁ֥עַר שֹׂנְאָֽי/ו
verses.en: And they blessed Rivqah and said to her: "Our sister, may you become thousands of ten-thousands; and may your seed possess the gate of those who hate them."
verses.ar: وبارَكوا رِفقَةَ وقالوا لَها: «يا أُختَنا، كوني أُلوفَ رُبَواتٍ؛ ولِيَرِثْ نَسلُكِ بابَ مُبغِضيهِ.»

verses.he: וַ/תָּ֨קָם רִבְקָ֜ה וְ/נַעֲרֹתֶ֗י/הָ וַ/תִּרְכַּ֨בְנָה֙ עַל הַ/גְּמַלִּ֔ים וַ/תֵּלַ֖כְנָה אַחֲרֵ֣י הָ/אִ֑ישׁ וַ/יִּקַּ֥ח הָ/עֶ֛בֶד אֶת רִבְקָ֖ה וַ/יֵּלַֽךְ
verses.en: And Rivqah rose, and her young women, and they rode upon the camels and went after the man; and the servant took Rivqah and went.
verses.ar: فقامَت رِفقَةُ وفَتَياتُها ورَكِبنَ الجِمالَ وسِرنَ وَراءَ الرَّجُلِ؛ فأخَذَ العَبدُ رِفقَةَ وذَهَبَ.

verses.he: וְ/יִצְחָק֙ בָּ֣א מִ/בּ֔וֹא בְּאֵ֥ר לַחַ֖י רֹאִ֑י וְ/ה֥וּא יוֹשֵׁ֖ב בְּ/אֶ֥רֶץ הַ/נֶּֽגֶב
verses.en: And Yitschak had come from the way of Be'er-Lachai-Ro'i, for he was dwelling in the land of the Negev.
verses.ar: وإسحاقُ قَد جاءَ مِن طَريقِ بِئرِ لَحَيِ رُؤيي، لأنَّهُ كانَ ساكِنًا في أرضِ النَّقَبِ.

verses.he: וַ/יֵּצֵ֥א יִצְחָ֛ק לָ/שׂ֥וּחַ בַּ/שָּׂדֶ֖ה לִ/פְנ֣וֹת עָ֑רֶב וַ/יִּשָּׂ֤א עֵינָי/ו֙ וַ/יַּ֔רְא וְ/הִנֵּ֥ה גְמַלִּ֖ים בָּאִֽים
verses.en: And Yitschak went out to meditate in the field toward the turning of evening; and he lifted up his eyes and saw — and behold, camels coming.
verses.ar: وخَرَجَ إسحاقُ لِيَتَأمَّلَ في الحَقلِ عِندَ إقبالِ المَساءِ؛ فرَفَعَ عَينَيهِ ونَظَرَ — وإذا جِمالٌ قادِمَةٌ.

verses.he: וַ/תִּשָּׂ֤א רִבְקָה֙ אֶת עֵינֶ֔י/הָ וַ/תֵּ֖רֶא אֶת יִצְחָ֑ק וַ/תִּפֹּ֖ל מֵ/עַ֥ל הַ/גָּמָֽל
verses.en: And Rivqah lifted up her eyes and saw Yitschak, and she fell from upon the camel.
verses.ar: فرَفَعَت رِفقَةُ عَينَيها ورَأت إسحاقَ فسَقَطَت عَنِ الجَمَلِ.

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר אֶל הָ/עֶ֗בֶד מִֽי הָ/אִ֤ישׁ הַלָּזֶה֙ הַ/הֹלֵ֤ךְ בַּ/שָּׂדֶה֙ לִ/קְרָאתֵ֔/נוּ וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר הָ/עֶ֖בֶד ה֣וּא אֲדֹנִ֑/י וַ/תִּקַּ֥ח הַ/צָּעִ֖יף וַ/תִּתְכָּֽס
verses.en: And she said unto the servant: "Who is this man walking in the field to meet us?" And the servant said: "He is my master." And she took the veil and covered herself.
verses.ar: وقالَت لِلعَبدِ: «مَن هذا الرَّجُلُ الماشي في الحَقلِ لِلِقائِنا؟» فقالَ العَبدُ: «هُوَ سَيِّدي.» فأخَذَتِ الخِمارَ وتَغَطَّت.

verses.he: וַ/יְסַפֵּ֥ר הָ/עֶ֖בֶד לְ/יִצְחָ֑ק אֵ֥ת כָּל הַ/דְּבָרִ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָשָֽׂה
verses.en: And the servant told Yitschak all the things that he had done.
verses.ar: فأخبَرَ العَبدُ إسحاقَ بِكُلِّ الأُمورِ الَّتي صَنَعَ.

verses.he: וַ/יְבִאֶ֣/הָ יִצְחָ֗ק הָ/אֹ֨הֱלָ/ה֙ שָׂרָ֣ה אִמּ֔/וֹ וַ/יִּקַּ֧ח אֶת רִבְקָ֛ה וַ/תְּהִי ל֥/וֹ לְ/אִשָּׁ֖ה וַ/יֶּאֱהָבֶ֑/הָ וַ/יִּנָּחֵ֥ם יִצְחָ֖ק אַחֲרֵ֥י אִמּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Yitschak brought her into the tent of Sarah his mother; and he took Rivqah, and she became his woman, and he loved her. And Yitschak was comforted after his mother.
verses.ar: فأدخَلَها إسحاقُ إلى خَيمَةِ سارَةَ أُمِّهِ؛ وأخَذَ رِفقَةَ فصارَت لَهُ امرَأةً وأحَبَّها. وتَعَزّى إسحاقُ بَعدَ أُمِّهِ.

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Bereshit Chapter 24 — A Woman for Yitschak

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.2: יָרֵךְ (yarekh) — "thigh"
Root י-ר-ך (y-r-kh): thigh, loin, side. "Place your hand beneath my thigh" — a solemn oath-gesture connected to the seat of procreation. The servant swears by placing his hand near the source of Avraham's seed, the very thing the oath concerns (finding a wife for that seed's continuation). This is not euphemism but ritual solemnity.

### v.12: חֶסֶד (ḥesed) — "loyal-kindness"
Root ח-ס-ד (ḥ-s-d): loyal love, covenant faithfulness, steadfast kindness. Conventionally "mercy" or "lovingkindness" — but חסד is richer than either. It is covenant loyalty expressed as kindness: the love that persists because of a bond, not merely a feeling. It occurs three times in this chapter (v.12, 14, 27, 49) — the servant's entire mission is framed by חסד. There is no exact Arabic cognate, but the concept overlaps with Arabic رحمة (raḥmah, mercy) and وفاء (wafā', faithfulness).

### v.14: הֹכַחְתָּ (hokhaḥta) — "You have appointed"
Root י-כ-ח (y-k-ḥ): to prove, to decide, to appoint, to reprove. The servant asks for a sign — not fortune-telling, but a way to recognize God's choice. The word implies that God has already decided; the servant seeks to discern, not to determine. The sign involves חסד — the woman who shows spontaneous generous kindness (watering ten camels is enormous labor) is the one God has designated.

### v.16: בְּתוּלָה (betulah) — "a maiden"
Root ב-ת-ל: a young woman of marriageable age, a virgin. The text emphasizes: "a maiden, and no man had known her" — the second clause clarifies the first, suggesting that בתולה alone might not guarantee virginity but describes youth and marriageability.

### v.21: מִשְׁתָּאֵה (mishtaeh) — "gazing... in silence"
Root ש-א-ה (sh-'-h): to gaze, to be desolate, to be astonished. This rare verb captures the servant's posture: he watches Rivqah in silent wonder, waiting to see if God has answered. The verb carries both "gazing" and "astonishment" — he is stunned and watchful simultaneously.

### v.27: אֱמֶת (emet) — "faithfulness"
Root א-מ-ן (ʾ-m-n): truth, faithfulness, reliability. Paired with חסד: "His loyal-kindness and His faithfulness." The אמת/חסד pair is one of the great theological word-pairs of the Hebrew Bible — God's character expressed as faithful love and loving faithfulness. Cognate with Arabic أمن (amn, security/trust) and أمين (amīn, trustworthy/faithful) — the root of آمين (āmīn, amen).

### v.48: דֶּרֶךְ אֱמֶת (derekh emet) — "the way of truth"
Root ד-ר-ך (d-r-kh): way, path, road. The servant says God led him "on the way of truth" — the right road, the faithful path. The image of life as a path (derekh) is central to Hebrew thought. Cognate with Arabic درك (dark, to reach/attain) — the way that leads to its destination.

### v.63: לָשׂוּחַ (lasuaḥ) — "to meditate"
Root ש-ו-ח (sh-w-ḥ): to meditate, to muse, possibly to walk about. Yitschak goes out "to meditate in the field" at evening. The verb is rare and its exact meaning debated — perhaps prayer, perhaps walking meditation, perhaps simply contemplation. The image of Yitschak in the field at twilight, meditating, when he lifts his eyes and sees the camels coming — is one of the most evocative scenes in Genesis.

### v.67: וַיִּנָּחֵם (vayyinnaḥem) — "was comforted"
Root נ-ח-ם (n-ḥ-m): to comfort, to be consoled, to relent. "Yitschak was comforted after his mother." Rivqah fills the void left by Sarah's death. The same root as נחמה (neḥamah, consolation) and the name Naḥum. The love story resolves in comfort — not passion but consolation.

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| חסד (ḥesed) | — | loyal-kindness (cf. رحمة, raḥmah) | 1:1 — الرحمن الرحيم, the merciful covenant-keeping God |
| אמן (emet) | أمن (amn) | truth/faithfulness/trust | 2:285 — the believers trust (آمنوا) in God |
| ברך (barakh) | بارك (bāraka) | to bless | 24:35 — "blessed" (مبارك) in the light verse |
| דרך (derekh) | — | way/path (cf. صراط, ṣirāṭ) | 1:6 — "guide us the straight path" |
| שמע (shamaʿ) | سمع (samiʿa) | to hear | Throughout — God hears prayer |
| נחם (naḥam) | — | comfort (cf. رحم, raḥima) | Mercy and comfort from the same relational field |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

'Abdu'l-Bahá draws on the betrothal narrative as an allegory of the soul's journey toward the Beloved. The servant (the seeker) is sent by the master (God) to find the bride (the soul prepared for divine union). The sign — spontaneous generosity and kindness to strangers and animals alike — reveals the spiritual readiness of the soul. Rivqah's decisive "I will go" (v.58) represents the free consent of the soul to the divine call. The narrative emphasizes that the match is divinely orchestrated: "From the LORD the thing has gone forth" (v.50). Human effort (the servant's journey) and divine guidance (the sign at the well) work together.

## Integrative Links

- **v.12 "loyal-kindness" (חסד) <> Qur'an 1:1-3**: The servant invokes God's חסד; the Qur'an opens with God's רחמה — both describe the faithful, covenantal love of God
- **v.27 "the way of truth" <> Qur'an 1:6**: "Guide us the straight path" — the servant thanks God for being led on the derekh emet; the Muslim prays daily for the ṣirāṭ al-mustaqīm
- **v.58 "I will go" <> Qur'an 33:36**: "It is not for a believer, man or woman, when God and His Messenger have decided a matter, that they should have any option" — Rivqah's free consent mirrors the voluntary submission of the believer
- **v.67 "and he loved her" <> Qur'an 30:21**: "And He placed between you affection (مودّة) and mercy (رحمة)" — the marriage of Yitschak and Rivqah as an embodiment of the Qur'anic vision of marriage

chapters.title: Bereshit — פרק 25
chapters.subtitle: Genesis — Chapter 25

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֧סֶף אַבְרָהָ֛ם וַ/יִּקַּ֥ח אִשָּׁ֖ה וּ/שְׁמָ֥/הּ קְטוּרָֽה
verses.en: And Avraham took again a wife, and her name was Keturah.
verses.ar: وعادَ إبراهيمُ فأخَذَ امرَأةً اسمُها قَطورَةُ.

verses.he: וַ/תֵּ֣לֶד ל֗/וֹ אֶת זִמְרָן֙ וְ/אֶת יָקְשָׁ֔ן וְ/אֶת מְדָ֖ן וְ/אֶת מִדְיָ֑ן וְ/אֶת יִשְׁבָּ֖ק וְ/אֶת שֽׁוּחַ
verses.en: And she bore him Zimran, and Yokshan, and Medan, and Midyan, and Yishbak, and Shuach.
verses.ar: فوَلَدَت لَهُ زِمرانَ ويُقشانَ ومَدانَ ومِديانَ ويِشباقَ وشوحًا.

verses.he: וְ/יָקְשָׁ֣ן יָלַ֔ד אֶת שְׁבָ֖א וְ/אֶת דְּדָ֑ן וּ/בְנֵ֣י דְדָ֔ן הָי֛וּ אַשּׁוּרִ֥ם וּ/לְטוּשִׁ֖ים וּ/לְאֻמִּֽים
verses.en: And Yokshan begot Sheva and Dedan. And the sons of Dedan were the Asshurim, and the Letushim, and the Leummim.
verses.ar: ويُقشانُ وَلَدَ شَبا ودَدانَ. وبَنو دَدانَ: الأشّورِيّونَ واللَّطوشِيّونَ واللَّأُمِّيّونَ.

verses.he: וּ/בְנֵ֣י מִדְיָ֗ן עֵיפָ֤ה וָ/עֵ֨פֶר֙ וַ/חֲנֹ֔ךְ וַ/אֲבִידָ֖ע וְ/אֶלְדָּעָ֑ה כָּל אֵ֖לֶּה בְּנֵ֥י קְטוּרָֽה
verses.en: And the sons of Midyan: Ephah, and Epher, and Chanokh, and Avida, and Eldaah. All these were the sons of Keturah.
verses.ar: وبَنو مِديانَ: عيفَةُ وعِفرُ وحَنوكُ وأبيداعُ والدَّعَةُ. كُلُّ هؤلاءِ بَنو قَطورَةَ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּתֵּ֧ן אַבְרָהָ֛ם אֶת כָּל אֲשֶׁר ל֖/וֹ לְ/יִצְחָֽק
verses.en: And Avraham gave all that he had unto Yitschak.
verses.ar: وأعطى إبراهيمُ كُلَّ ما لَهُ لإسحاقَ.

verses.he: וְ/לִ/בְנֵ֤י הַ/פִּֽילַגְשִׁים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר לְ/אַבְרָהָ֔ם נָתַ֥ן אַבְרָהָ֖ם מַתָּנֹ֑ת וַֽ/יְשַׁלְּחֵ֞/ם מֵ/עַ֨ל יִצְחָ֤ק בְּנ/וֹ֙ בְּ/עוֹדֶ֣/נּוּ חַ֔י קֵ֖דְמָ/ה אֶל אֶ֥רֶץ קֶֽדֶם
verses.en: And to the sons of the concubines that Avraham had, Avraham gave gifts; and he sent them away from Yitschak his son, while he yet lived, eastward, unto the land of the East.
verses.ar: ولِبَني السَّرارِيِّ اللَّواتي لإبراهيمَ أعطى إبراهيمُ عَطايا؛ وأرسَلَهُم مِن عِندِ إسحاقَ ابنِهِ وهُوَ حَيٌّ بَعدُ، شَرقًا إلى أرضِ المَشرِقِ.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֗לֶּה יְמֵ֛י שְׁנֵֽי חַיֵּ֥י אַבְרָהָ֖ם אֲשֶׁר חָ֑י מְאַ֥ת שָׁנָ֛ה וְ/שִׁבְעִ֥ים שָׁנָ֖ה וְ/חָמֵ֥שׁ שָׁנִֽים
verses.en: And these are the days of the years of Avraham's life which he lived: a hundred years and seventy years and five years.
verses.ar: وهذِهِ أيّامُ سِني حَياةِ إبراهيمَ الَّتي عاشَها: مِئَةُ سَنَةٍ وسَبعونَ سَنَةً وخَمسُ سِنينَ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּגְוַ֨ע וַ/יָּ֧מָת אַבְרָהָ֛ם בְּ/שֵׂיבָ֥ה טוֹבָ֖ה זָקֵ֣ן וְ/שָׂבֵ֑עַ וַ/יֵּאָ֖סֶף אֶל עַמָּֽי/ו
verses.en: And Avraham breathed his last and died in a good grey age, old and satisfied; and he was gathered unto his people.
verses.ar: وأسلَمَ إبراهيمُ رُوحَهُ وماتَ في شَيبَةٍ صالِحَةٍ، شَيخًا وشَبعانَ؛ وضُمَّ إلى قَومِهِ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְבְּר֨וּ אֹת֜/וֹ יִצְחָ֤ק וְ/יִשְׁמָעֵאל֙ בָּנָ֔י/ו אֶל מְעָרַ֖ת הַ/מַּכְפֵּלָ֑ה אֶל שְׂדֵ֞ה עֶפְרֹ֤ן בֶּן צֹ֨חַר֙ הַֽ/חִתִּ֔י אֲשֶׁ֖ר עַל פְּנֵ֥י מַמְרֵֽא
verses.en: And Yitschak and Yishmael his sons buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron son of Tsochar the Chittite, which faces Mamre —
verses.ar: فدَفَنَهُ إسحاقُ وإسماعيلُ ابناهُ في مَغارَةِ المَكفيلَةِ، في حَقلِ عِفرونَ بنِ صوحَرَ الحِثِّيِّ الَّذي أمامَ مَمرِيَ —

verses.he: הַ/שָּׂדֶ֛ה אֲשֶׁר קָנָ֥ה אַבְרָהָ֖ם מֵ/אֵ֣ת בְּנֵי חֵ֑ת שָׁ֛מָּ/ה קֻבַּ֥ר אַבְרָהָ֖ם וְ/שָׂרָ֥ה אִשְׁתּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: the field which Avraham had purchased from the sons of Chet. There was Avraham buried, and Sarah his wife.
verses.ar: الحَقلُ الَّذي اشتَراهُ إبراهيمُ مِن بَني حِثَّ. هُناكَ دُفِنَ إبراهيمُ وسارَةُ امرَأتُهُ.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֗י אַחֲרֵי֙ מ֣וֹת אַבְרָהָ֔ם וַ/יְבָ֥רֶךְ אֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶת יִצְחָ֣ק בְּנ֑/וֹ וַ/יֵּ֣שֶׁב יִצְחָ֔ק עִם בְּאֵ֥ר לַחַ֖י רֹאִֽי
verses.en: And it was, after the death of Avraham, that God blessed Yitschak his son; and Yitschak dwelt by the Well of the Living One Who Sees.
verses.ar: وكانَ بَعدَ مَوتِ إبراهيمَ أنَّ اللهَ بارَكَ إسحاقَ ابنَهُ؛ وسَكَنَ إسحاقُ عِندَ بِئرِ الحَيِّ الَّذي يَراني.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֛לֶּה תֹּלְדֹ֥ת יִשְׁמָעֵ֖אל בֶּן אַבְרָהָ֑ם אֲשֶׁ֨ר יָלְדָ֜ה הָגָ֧ר הַ/מִּצְרִ֛ית שִׁפְחַ֥ת שָׂרָ֖ה לְ/אַבְרָהָֽם
verses.en: And these are the begettings of Yishmael son of Avraham, whom Hagar the Mitsrite, handmaid of Sarah, bore unto Avraham.
verses.ar: وهذِهِ مَواليدُ إسماعيلَ بنِ إبراهيمَ الَّذي وَلَدَتهُ هاجَرُ المِصرِيَّةُ جارِيَةُ سارَةَ لإبراهيمَ.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֗לֶּה שְׁמוֹת֙ בְּנֵ֣י יִשְׁמָעֵ֔אל בִּ/שְׁמֹתָ֖/ם לְ/תוֹלְדֹתָ֑/ם בְּכֹ֤ר יִשְׁמָעֵאל֙ נְבָיֹ֔ת וְ/קֵדָ֥ר וְ/אַדְבְּאֵ֖ל וּ/מִבְשָֽׂם
verses.en: And these are the names of the sons of Yishmael, by their names, according to their begettings: the firstborn of Yishmael, Nevayot; and Kedar, and Adbeel, and Mivsam,
verses.ar: وهذِهِ أسماءُ بَني إسماعيلَ بِأسمائِهِم حَسَبَ مَواليدِهِم: بِكرُ إسماعيلَ نَبايوتُ، وقيدارُ وأدبَئيلُ ومِبسامُ،

verses.he: וּ/מִשְׁמָ֥ע וְ/דוּמָ֖ה וּ/מַשָּֽׂא
verses.en: and Mishma, and Dumah, and Massa,
verses.ar: ومِشمَعُ ودومَةُ ومَسّا،

verses.he: חֲדַ֣ד וְ/תֵימָ֔א יְט֥וּר נָפִ֖ישׁ וָ/קֵֽדְמָה
verses.en: Chadad, and Tema, Yetur, Naphish, and Kedmah.
verses.ar: حَدَدُ وتيما ويَطورُ ونافيشُ وقِدمَةُ.

verses.he: אֵ֣לֶּה הֵ֞ם בְּנֵ֤י יִשְׁמָעֵאל֙ וְ/אֵ֣לֶּה שְׁמֹתָ֔/ם בְּ/חַצְרֵי/הֶ֖ם וּ/בְ/טִֽירֹתָ֑/ם שְׁנֵים עָשָׂ֥ר נְשִׂיאִ֖ם לְ/אֻמֹּתָֽ/ם
verses.en: These are the sons of Yishmael, and these are their names, by their enclosures and by their strongholds — twelve princes according to their peoples.
verses.ar: هؤلاءِ بَنو إسماعيلَ وهذِهِ أسماؤهُم بِدِيارِهِم وحُصونِهِم — اثنا عَشَرَ أميرًا حَسَبَ أقوامِهِم.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֗לֶּה שְׁנֵי֙ חַיֵּ֣י יִשְׁמָעֵ֔אל מְאַ֥ת שָׁנָ֛ה וּ/שְׁלֹשִׁ֥ים שָׁנָ֖ה וְ/שֶׁ֣בַע שָׁנִ֑ים וַ/יִּגְוַ֣ע וַ/יָּ֔מָת וַ/יֵּאָ֖סֶף אֶל עַמָּֽי/ו
verses.en: And these are the years of the life of Yishmael: a hundred years and thirty years and seven years; and he breathed his last and died, and was gathered unto his people.
verses.ar: وهذِهِ سِنو حَياةِ إسماعيلَ: مِئَةُ سَنَةٍ وثَلاثونَ سَنَةً وسَبعُ سِنينَ؛ فأسلَمَ رُوحَهُ وماتَ وضُمَّ إلى قَومِهِ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּשְׁכְּנ֨וּ מֵֽ/חֲוִילָ֜ה עַד שׁ֗וּר אֲשֶׁר֙ עַל פְּנֵ֣י מִצְרַ֔יִם בֹּאֲ/כָ֖ה אַשּׁ֑וּרָ/ה עַל פְּנֵ֥י כָל אֶחָ֖י/ו נָפָֽל
verses.en: And they dwelt from Chavilah unto Shur, which faces Mitsrayim, as you go toward Asshur. Before the face of all his brothers he fell.
verses.ar: وسَكَنوا مِن حَويلَةَ إلى شورَ الَّتي أمامَ مِصرَ حينَ تَذهَبُ نَحوَ أشّورَ. أمامَ وُجوهِ كُلِّ إخوَتِهِ سَقَطَ.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֛לֶּה תּוֹלְדֹ֥ת יִצְחָ֖ק בֶּן אַבְרָהָ֑ם אַבְרָהָ֖ם הוֹלִ֥יד אֶת יִצְחָֽק
verses.en: And these are the begettings of Yitschak son of Avraham: Avraham begot Yitschak.
verses.ar: وهذِهِ مَواليدُ إسحاقَ بنِ إبراهيمَ: إبراهيمُ وَلَدَ إسحاقَ.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֤י יִצְחָק֙ בֶּן אַרְבָּעִ֣ים שָׁנָ֔ה בְּ/קַחְתּ֣/וֹ אֶת רִבְקָ֗ה בַּת בְּתוּאֵל֙ הָֽ/אֲרַמִּ֔י מִ/פַּדַּ֖ן אֲרָ֑ם אֲח֛וֹת לָבָ֥ן הָ/אֲרַמִּ֖י ל֥/וֹ לְ/אִשָּֽׁה
verses.en: And Yitschak was forty years old when he took Rivkah, daughter of Betuel the Aramean of Paddan-Aram, sister of Lavan the Aramean, to himself as wife.
verses.ar: وكانَ إسحاقُ ابنَ أربَعينَ سَنَةً حينَ أخَذَ رِفقَةَ بِنتَ بَتوئيلَ الأرامِيِّ مِن فَدّانِ أرامَ أُختَ لابانَ الأرامِيِّ لَهُ امرَأةً.

verses.he: וַ/יֶּעְתַּ֨ר יִצְחָ֤ק לַֽ/יהוָה֙ לְ/נֹ֣כַח אִשְׁתּ֔/וֹ כִּ֥י עֲקָרָ֖ה הִ֑וא וַ/יֵּעָ֤תֶר ל/וֹ֙ יְהוָ֔ה וַ/תַּ֖הַר רִבְקָ֥ה אִשְׁתּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Yitschak pleaded with YHWH on behalf of his wife, for she was barren; and YHWH was entreated by him, and Rivkah his wife conceived.
verses.ar: وتَضَرَّعَ إسحاقُ إلى الرَّبِّ مِن أجلِ امرَأتِهِ لأنَّها كانَت عاقِرًا؛ فاستَجابَ لَهُ الرَّبُّ، فحَمَلَت رِفقَةُ امرَأتُهُ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּתְרֹֽצֲצ֤וּ הַ/בָּנִים֙ בְּ/קִרְבָּ֔/הּ וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר אִם כֵּ֔ן לָ֥/מָּה זֶּ֖ה אָנֹ֑כִי וַ/תֵּ֖לֶךְ לִ/דְרֹ֥שׁ אֶת יְהוָֽה
verses.en: And the children struggled violently within her, and she said: "If it be so, why am I thus?" And she went to seek YHWH.
verses.ar: فتَصارَعَ الوَلَدانِ بِعُنفٍ في بَطنِها فقالَت: «إن كانَ كَذلِكَ فلِمَ أنا هكَذا؟» فذَهَبَت لِتَسألَ الرَّبَّ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר יְהוָ֜ה לָ֗/הּ שְׁנֵ֤י גיים גוֹיִם֙ בְּ/בִטְנֵ֔/ךְ וּ/שְׁנֵ֣י לְאֻמִּ֔ים מִ/מֵּעַ֖יִ/ךְ יִפָּרֵ֑דוּ וּ/לְאֹם֙ מִ/לְאֹ֣ם יֶֽאֱמָ֔ץ וְ/רַ֖ב יַעֲבֹ֥ד צָעִֽיר
verses.en: And YHWH said unto her: "Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples from your innards shall be parted; and people over people shall prevail, and the greater shall serve the younger."
verses.ar: فقالَ لَها الرَّبُّ: «أُمَّتانِ في بَطنِكِ، وشَعبانِ مِن أحشائِكِ يَفتَرِقانِ؛ وشَعبٌ على شَعبٍ يَقوى، والأكبَرُ يَخدِمُ الأصغَرَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּמְלְא֥וּ יָמֶ֖י/הָ לָ/לֶ֑דֶת וְ/הִנֵּ֥ה תוֹמִ֖ם בְּ/בִטְנָֽ/הּ
verses.en: And her days to give birth were fulfilled, and behold — twins in her womb.
verses.ar: فتَمَّت أيّامُها لِتَلِدَ وإذا تَوأمانِ في بَطنِها.

verses.he: וַ/יֵּצֵ֤א הָ/רִאשׁוֹן֙ אַדְמוֹנִ֔י כֻּלּ֖/וֹ כְּ/אַדֶּ֣רֶת שֵׂעָ֑ר וַ/יִּקְרְא֥וּ שְׁמ֖/וֹ עֵשָֽׂו
verses.en: And the first came out ruddy, all of him like a mantle of hair; and they called his name Esav.
verses.ar: فخَرَجَ الأوَّلُ أحمَرَ كُلُّهُ كَرِداءِ شَعرٍ؛ فسَمَّوهُ عيسو.

verses.he: וְ/אַֽחֲרֵי כֵ֞ן יָצָ֣א אָחִ֗י/ו וְ/יָד֤/וֹ אֹחֶ֨זֶת֙ בַּ/עֲקֵ֣ב עֵשָׂ֔ו וַ/יִּקְרָ֥א שְׁמ֖/וֹ יַעֲקֹ֑ב וְ/יִצְחָ֛ק בֶּן שִׁשִּׁ֥ים שָׁנָ֖ה בְּ/לֶ֥דֶת אֹתָֽ/ם
verses.en: And after that came out his brother, and his hand was grasping the heel of Esav; and his name was called Yaakov — "Heel-Grasper." And Yitschak was sixty years old when she bore them.
verses.ar: وبَعدَهُ خَرَجَ أخوهُ ويَدُهُ قابِضَةٌ على عَقِبِ عيسو؛ فسُمِّيَ اسمُهُ يعقوبَ — «قابِضُ العَقِبِ». وكانَ إسحاقُ ابنَ سِتّينَ سَنَةً حينَ وَلَدَتهُما.

verses.he: וַֽ/יִּגְדְּלוּ֙ הַ/נְּעָרִ֔ים וַ/יְהִ֣י עֵשָׂ֗ו אִ֛ישׁ יֹדֵ֥עַ צַ֖יִד אִ֣ישׁ שָׂדֶ֑ה וְ/יַעֲקֹב֙ אִ֣ישׁ תָּ֔ם יֹשֵׁ֖ב אֹהָלִֽים
verses.en: And the lads grew up; and Esav became a man knowing the hunt, a man of the field; and Yaakov was a man of wholeness, dwelling in tents.
verses.ar: فكَبِرَ الغُلامانِ؛ وصارَ عيسو رَجُلًا يَعرِفُ الصَّيدَ، رَجُلَ حَقلٍ؛ ويعقوبُ كانَ رَجُلًا تامًّا ساكِنًا في الخِيامِ.

verses.he: וַ/יֶּאֱהַ֥ב יִצְחָ֛ק אֶת עֵשָׂ֖ו כִּי צַ֣יִד בְּ/פִ֑י/ו וְ/רִבְקָ֖ה אֹהֶ֥בֶת אֶֽת יַעֲקֹֽב
verses.en: And Yitschak loved Esav, for the hunt was in his mouth; but Rivkah loved Yaakov.
verses.ar: وأحَبَّ إسحاقُ عيسو لأنَّ الصَّيدَ في فَمِهِ؛ ورِفقَةُ أحَبَّت يعقوبَ.

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֥זֶד יַעֲקֹ֖ב נָזִ֑יד וַ/יָּבֹ֥א עֵשָׂ֛ו מִן הַ/שָּׂדֶ֖ה וְ/ה֥וּא עָיֵֽף
verses.en: And Yaakov boiled a boiled-thing; and Esav came from the field, and he was faint.
verses.ar: وطَبَخَ يعقوبُ طَبيخًا؛ فجاءَ عيسو مِنَ الحَقلِ وهُوَ مُعيٍ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר עֵשָׂ֜ו אֶֽל יַעֲקֹ֗ב הַלְעִיטֵ֤/נִי נָא֙ מִן הָ/אָדֹ֤ם הָ/אָדֹם֙ הַ/זֶּ֔ה כִּ֥י עָיֵ֖ף אָנֹ֑כִי עַל כֵּ֥ן קָרָֽא שְׁמ֖/וֹ אֱדֽוֹם
verses.en: And Esav said unto Yaakov: "Let me gulp down from this red, this red thing, for I am faint." Therefore his name was called Edom — "Red."
verses.ar: فقالَ عيسو ليعقوبَ: «أطعِمني مِن هذا الأحمَرِ هذا الأحمَرِ، لأنّي مُعيٍ.» لِذلِكَ سُمِّيَ اسمُهُ أدومَ — «الأحمَرُ».

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֑ב מִכְרָ֥/ה כַ/יּ֛וֹם אֶת בְּכֹֽרָתְ/ךָ֖ לִֽ/י
verses.en: And Yaakov said: "Sell me, as of this day, your firstborn-right."
verses.ar: فقالَ يعقوبُ: «بِعني اليَومَ بُكورِيَّتَكَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר עֵשָׂ֔ו הִנֵּ֛ה אָנֹכִ֥י הוֹלֵ֖ךְ לָ/מ֑וּת וְ/לָ/מָּה זֶּ֥ה לִ֖/י בְּכֹרָֽה
verses.en: And Esav said: "Behold, I am going to die; and of what use is a firstborn-right to me?"
verses.ar: فقالَ عيسو: «ها أنا ذاهِبٌ لِلمَوتِ؛ وما نَفعُ البُكورِيَّةِ لي؟»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֗ב הִשָּׁ֤בְעָ/ה לִּ/י֙ כַּ/יּ֔וֹם וַ/יִּשָּׁבַ֖ע ל֑/וֹ וַ/יִּמְכֹּ֥ר אֶת בְּכֹרָת֖/וֹ לְ/יַעֲקֹֽב
verses.en: And Yaakov said: "Swear to me as of this day." And he swore to him, and sold his firstborn-right unto Yaakov.
verses.ar: فقالَ يعقوبُ: «احلِفْ لي اليَومَ.» فحَلَفَ لَهُ وباعَ بُكورِيَّتَهُ ليعقوبَ.

verses.he: וְ/יַעֲקֹ֞ב נָתַ֣ן לְ/עֵשָׂ֗ו לֶ֚חֶם וּ/נְזִ֣יד עֲדָשִׁ֔ים וַ/יֹּ֣אכַל וַ/יֵּ֔שְׁתְּ וַ/יָּ֖קָם וַ/יֵּלַ֑ךְ וַ/יִּ֥בֶז עֵשָׂ֖ו אֶת הַ/בְּכֹרָֽה
verses.en: And Yaakov gave Esav bread and a stew of lentils; and he ate, and he drank, and he rose, and he went. And Esav despised the firstborn-right.
verses.ar: فأعطى يعقوبُ عيسو خُبزًا ويَخنَةَ عَدَسٍ؛ فأكَلَ وشَرِبَ وقامَ وذَهَبَ. واحتَقَرَ عيسو البُكورِيَّةَ.

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Bereshit Chapter 25 — The Begettings of Yitschak

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.1: קְטוּרָה (Keturah)
Possibly from root ק-ט-ר (q-ṭ-r): to burn incense, to send up smoke. Keturah = "the incense-woman" or "the fragrant one." Some rabbinic sources identify her with Hagar; the text presents her as a distinct figure. The incense connection is suggestive — she is associated with the rising of fragrant smoke, a priestly/sacred image.

### v.8: וַיִּגְוַע (vayyigvaʿ) — "breathed his last"
Root ג-ו-ע (g-w-ʿ): to expire, to breathe out the last breath. Distinct from מות (mut, to die) which follows immediately. The text gives both: he expired AND he died — the physical cessation of breath, then the state of death. "Old and satisfied" (שָׂבֵעַ, saveaʿ) — root ש-ב-ע (s-b-ʿ): to be full, satisfied, sated. Avraham dies sated with life, not merely "full of years" but satisfied, having had enough.

### v.9: Together — Yitschak and Yishmael
The two sons bury their father together. Despite the separation in chapter 21, they reunite at Avraham's grave. This detail, easily overlooked, is profoundly significant: the two lines — through whom Jews and Arabs trace descent — come together in grief and in honoring their father. The reconciliation at the grave is a model that neither tradition has yet fully realized.

### v.12-18: The begettings of Yishmael
The text gives Yishmael's genealogy with care and respect: twelve princes (נְשִׂיאִם, nesi'im — the same word used for Avraham as "prince of God" in 23:6). Yishmael's line is princely. He lives 137 years, "breathes his last and dies, and is gathered unto his people." The formula is honorable — the same used for Avraham.

### v.22: וַיִּתְרֹצֲצוּ (vayyitrotsatsu) — "struggled violently"
Root ר-צ-ץ (r-ts-ts): to crush, to oppress, to struggle violently. NOT merely "jostled" — the verb implies violent crushing. The twins are at war in the womb. Rivqah's cry "why am I thus?" (lammah zeh anokhi) expresses existential distress, not mere discomfort.

### v.23: רַב יַעֲבֹד צָעִיר (rav yaʿavod tsaʿir) — "the greater shall serve the younger"
This phrase is grammatically ambiguous: it could mean "the elder shall serve the younger" OR "the elder — the younger shall serve him." The ambiguity may be intentional — the oracle is deliberately unclear, as oracles often are. History plays out the tension: both readings find partial fulfillment.

### v.25: עֵשָׂו (Esav) — "Esav"
The name may derive from ע-ש-ה (ʿ-s-h, to make/do) — "the made one, the finished one" — or from a root meaning "hairy" (שֵׂעָר, seʿar, hair). He is described as אַדְמוֹנִי (admoni, ruddy/red) — connecting to אדם (adam, earth/red) and to אֱדוֹם (Edom, the nation).

### v.26: עָקֵב (ʿaqev) — "heel"
Root ע-ק-ב (ʿ-q-b): heel, to follow at the heel, to supplant, to deceive by grabbing the heel. Yaakov (יעקב) = "Heel-Grasper" or "Supplanter." The name carries both meanings throughout the narrative: he grasps his brother's heel at birth, supplants him for the blessing, and is later renamed to shed this identity. Cognate with Arabic عقب (ʿaqib, consequence/result) and عاقبة (ʿāqibah, outcome, end) — what comes after, what follows at the heel.

### v.27: אִישׁ תָּם (ish tam) — "a man of wholeness"
Root ת-מ-ם (t-m-m): whole, complete, blameless. The same root used for Noach (6:9). Conventionally "plain" or "quiet" — but תם is richer: whole, complete, without duplicity. Ironic, given that Yaakov will soon practice deception — but the text declares his fundamental character as tam. Cognate with Arabic تمام (tamām, completeness).

### v.31: בְּכֹרָה (bekhorah) — "firstborn-right"
Root ב-כ-ר (b-k-r): firstborn, first fruits, early. The בכורה is not merely birth-order but a package of rights: double inheritance, family leadership, priestly function. Esav sells this for a bowl of stew — the text's final verdict: "Esav despised the firstborn-right" (v.34). Root cognate with Arabic بكر (bakr, firstborn/early) — as in باكورة (bākūrah, first fruits).

### v.30: הָאָדֹם הָאָדֹם (ha-adom ha-adom) — "this red, this red thing"
The doubled adjective shows Esav's desperate, inarticulate hunger. He does not even name the food — just "this red, this red thing." From this, his alternate name: Edom (אדום, red). A nation named for a moment of appetite.

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| עקב (ʿaqev) | عقب (ʿaqib) | heel/consequence | The supplanter and what follows — aftermath |
| בכר (bekhor) | بكر (bakr) | firstborn/early | Primogeniture and its spiritual significance |
| תמם (tam) | تمام (tamām) | wholeness/completeness | Completeness as a spiritual quality |
| שבע (saveaʿ) | شبع (shabiʿa) | to be sated/full | Avraham dies satisfied; Esav craves satiation |
| זרע (zeraʿ) | زرع (zaraʿa) | seed/offspring | Both lines — through Yitschak and Yishmael — as divine seed |
| נשא (nasi') | — | prince/elevated one | Cf. رفع (rafaʿa, to elevate) — Yishmael's sons as princes |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

The juxtaposition of Yishmael's and Yitschak's genealogies in this chapter is significant from a Bahá'í perspective: both lines are divinely blessed, both produce "princes" and "nations." Bahá'u'lláh's lineage traces through both Abrahamic lines — He is descended from Avraham through multiple paths, uniting in His person the promise given to both sons. The Yaakov-Esav rivalry is read as an allegory of the spiritual and the material: Esav (the hunter, the man of the field, the one who lives by immediate appetite) versus Yaakov (the man of wholeness, the tent-dweller, the one who values the birthright). The birthright is spiritual primacy, and it can be "sold" — traded away for immediate material satisfaction.

## Integrative Links

- **v.9 "Yitschak and Yishmael buried him" <> Qur'an 2:133**: "Were you witnesses when death approached Yaakov?" — the patriarchal deathbed as a moment of spiritual testimony; here the two sons unite at the father's burial
- **v.12-18 Yishmael's line <> Qur'an 2:136**: "We make no distinction between any of His messengers" — both Abrahamic lines carry prophetic blessing
- **v.23 "two nations in your womb" <> Qur'an 49:13**: "We made you peoples and tribes that you may know one another" — the division of humanity into nations serves a divine purpose
- **v.34 "Esav despised the firstborn-right" <> Qur'an 7:176**: One who is given the signs of God but strips himself of them — selling the eternal for the temporal

chapters.title: Bereshit — פרק 26
chapters.subtitle: Genesis — Chapter 26

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֤י רָעָב֙ בָּ/אָ֔רֶץ מִ/לְּ/בַד֙ הָ/רָעָ֣ב הָ/רִאשׁ֔וֹן אֲשֶׁ֥ר הָיָ֖ה בִּ/ימֵ֣י אַבְרָהָ֑ם וַ/יֵּ֧לֶךְ יִצְחָ֛ק אֶל אֲבִימֶּ֥לֶךְ מֶֽלֶךְ פְּלִשְׁתִּ֖ים גְּרָֽרָ/ה
verses.en: And there was a famine in the land, besides the first famine which was in the days of Avraham. And Yitschak went unto Avimelekh, king of the Pelishtim, to Gerar.
verses.ar: وكانَ جوعٌ في الأرضِ غَيرُ الجوعِ الأوَّلِ الَّذي كانَ في أيّامِ إبراهيمَ. فذَهَبَ إسحاقُ إلى أبيمالِكَ مَلِكِ الفِلَسطينِيّينَ إلى جَرارَ.

verses.he: וַ/יֵּרָ֤א אֵלָי/ו֙ יְהוָ֔ה וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר אַל תֵּרֵ֣ד מִצְרָ֑יְמָ/ה שְׁכֹ֣ן בָּ/אָ֔רֶץ אֲשֶׁ֖ר אֹמַ֥ר אֵלֶֽי/ךָ
verses.en: And YHWH appeared unto him and said: "Do not go down to Mitsrayim; dwell in the land which I shall say unto you.
verses.ar: فَتَجَلّى لَهُ الرَّبُّ وقالَ: «لا تَنزِلْ إلى مِصرَ؛ اسكُنْ في الأرضِ الَّتي أقولُها لَكَ.

verses.he: גּ֚וּר בָּ/אָ֣רֶץ הַ/זֹּ֔את וְ/אֶֽהְיֶ֥ה עִמְּ/ךָ֖ וַ/אֲבָרְכֶ֑/ךָּ כִּֽי לְ/ךָ֣ וּֽ/לְ/זַרְעֲ/ךָ֗ אֶתֵּן֙ אֶת כָּל הָֽ/אֲרָצֹ֣ת הָ/אֵ֔ל וַ/הֲקִֽמֹתִי֙ אֶת הַ/שְּׁבֻעָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר נִשְׁבַּ֖עְתִּי לְ/אַבְרָהָ֥ם אָבִֽי/ךָ
verses.en: Sojourn in this land, and I will be with you and bless you; for to you and to your seed I will give all these lands, and I will uphold the oath which I swore unto Avraham your father.
verses.ar: تَغَرَّبْ في هذِهِ الأرضِ وأكونُ مَعَكَ وأُبارِكُكَ؛ لأنَّ لَكَ ولِنَسلِكَ أُعطي كُلَّ هذِهِ الأراضي، وأُقيمُ القَسَمَ الَّذي أقسَمتُهُ لإبراهيمَ أبيكَ.

verses.he: וְ/הִרְבֵּיתִ֤י אֶֽת זַרְעֲ/ךָ֙ כְּ/כוֹכְבֵ֣י הַ/שָּׁמַ֔יִם וְ/נָתַתִּ֣י לְ/זַרְעֲ/ךָ֔ אֵ֥ת כָּל הָ/אֲרָצֹ֖ת הָ/אֵ֑ל וְ/הִתְבָּרֲכ֣וּ בְ/זַרְעֲ/ךָ֔ כֹּ֖ל גּוֹיֵ֥י הָ/אָֽרֶץ
verses.en: And I will multiply your seed as the stars of the heavens, and I will give to your seed all these lands; and in your seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed —
verses.ar: وأُكَثِّرُ نَسلَكَ كنُجومِ السَّماواتِ، وأُعطي نَسلَكَ كُلَّ هذِهِ الأراضي؛ وتَتَبارَكُ بِنَسلِكَ جَميعُ أُمَمِ الأرضِ —

verses.he: עֵ֕קֶב אֲשֶׁר שָׁמַ֥ע אַבְרָהָ֖ם בְּ/קֹלִ֑/י וַ/יִּשְׁמֹר֙ מִשְׁמַרְתִּ֔/י מִצְוֺתַ֖/י חֻקּוֹתַ֥/י וְ/תוֹרֹתָֽ/י
verses.en: on account of Avraham hearkening to My voice, and guarding My charge, My commandments, My statutes, and My teachings."
verses.ar: لأنَّ إبراهيمَ سَمِعَ صَوتي وحَفِظَ حِفظي ووَصاياي وفَرائِضي وتَعاليمي.»

verses.he: וַ/יֵּ֥שֶׁב יִצְחָ֖ק בִּ/גְרָֽר
verses.en: And Yitschak dwelt in Gerar.
verses.ar: فسَكَنَ إسحاقُ في جَرارَ.

verses.he: וַֽ/יִּשְׁאֲל֞וּ אַנְשֵׁ֤י הַ/מָּקוֹם֙ לְ/אִשְׁתּ֔/וֹ וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר אֲחֹ֣תִ/י הִ֑וא כִּ֤י יָרֵא֙ לֵ/אמֹ֣ר אִשְׁתִּ֔/י פֶּן יַֽהַרְגֻ֜/נִי אַנְשֵׁ֤י הַ/מָּקוֹם֙ עַל רִבְקָ֔ה כִּֽי טוֹבַ֥ת מַרְאֶ֖ה הִֽיא
verses.en: And the men of the place asked about his wife, and he said: "She is my sister." For he feared to say "my wife," lest the men of the place kill me on account of Rivkah, for she was good of appearance.
verses.ar: وسَألَهُ رِجالُ المَوضِعِ عَنِ امرَأتِهِ فقالَ: «هِيَ أُختي.» لأنَّهُ خافَ أن يَقولَ «امرَأتي» لِئَلّا يَقتُلَني رِجالُ المَوضِعِ بِسَبَبِ رِفقَةَ لأنَّها حَسَنَةُ المَنظَرِ.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֗י כִּ֣י אָֽרְכוּ ל֥/וֹ שָׁם֙ הַ/יָּמִ֔ים וַ/יַּשְׁקֵ֗ף אֲבִימֶ֨לֶךְ֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים בְּעַ֖ד הַֽ/חַלּ֑וֹן וַ/יַּ֗רְא וְ/הִנֵּ֤ה יִצְחָק֙ מְצַחֵ֔ק אֵ֖ת רִבְקָ֥ה אִשְׁתּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And it was, when the days had lengthened for him there, that Avimelekh king of the Pelishtim looked out through the window, and he saw — and behold, Yitschak was laughing-with Rivkah his wife.
verses.ar: وكانَ حينَ طالَت لَهُ الأيّامُ هُناكَ أنَّ أبيمالِكَ مَلِكَ الفِلَسطينِيّينَ أشرَفَ مِنَ النّافِذَةِ فرَأى — وإذا إسحاقُ يُلاعِبُ رِفقَةَ امرَأتَهُ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרָ֨א אֲבִימֶ֜לֶךְ לְ/יִצְחָ֗ק וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר֙ אַ֣ךְ הִנֵּ֤ה אִשְׁתְּ/ךָ֙ הִ֔וא וְ/אֵ֥יךְ אָמַ֖רְתָּ אֲחֹ֣תִ/י הִ֑וא וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָי/ו֙ יִצְחָ֔ק כִּ֣י אָמַ֔רְתִּי פֶּן אָמ֖וּת עָלֶֽי/הָ
verses.en: And Avimelekh called Yitschak and said: "Surely, behold, she is your wife! And how did you say, 'She is my sister'?" And Yitschak said unto him: "Because I said, 'Lest I die on account of her.'"
verses.ar: فدَعا أبيمالِكُ إسحاقَ وقالَ: «حَقًّا ها هِيَ امرَأتُكَ! فكَيفَ قُلتَ: 'هِيَ أُختي'؟» فقالَ لَهُ إسحاقُ: «لأنّي قُلتُ: 'لِئَلّا أموتَ بِسَبَبِها.'»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר אֲבִימֶ֔לֶךְ מַה זֹּ֖את עָשִׂ֣יתָ לָּ֑/נוּ כִּ֠/מְעַט שָׁכַ֞ב אַחַ֤ד הָ/עָם֙ אֶת אִשְׁתֶּ֔/ךָ וְ/הֵבֵאתָ֥ עָלֵ֖י/נוּ אָשָֽׁם
verses.en: And Avimelekh said: "What is this you have done to us? One of the people might easily have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guilt-debt upon us."
verses.ar: فقالَ أبيمالِكُ: «ما هذا الَّذي صَنَعتَ بِنا؟ كادَ أحَدُ الشَّعبِ أن يَضطَجِعَ مَعَ امرَأتِكَ فتَجلِبُ عَلَينا ذَنبًا.»

verses.he: וַ/יְצַ֣ו אֲבִימֶ֔לֶךְ אֶת כָּל הָ/עָ֖ם לֵ/אמֹ֑ר הַ/נֹּגֵ֜עַ בָּ/אִ֥ישׁ הַ/זֶּ֛ה וּ/בְ/אִשְׁתּ֖/וֹ מ֥וֹת יוּמָֽת
verses.en: And Avimelekh commanded all the people, saying: "Whoever touches this man or his wife shall surely be put to death."
verses.ar: فأمَرَ أبيمالِكُ كُلَّ الشَّعبِ قائِلًا: «مَن يَمَسُّ هذا الرَّجُلَ أو امرَأتَهُ يُقتَلُ قَتلًا.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּזְרַ֤ע יִצְחָק֙ בָּ/אָ֣רֶץ הַ/הִ֔וא וַ/יִּמְצָ֛א בַּ/שָּׁנָ֥ה הַ/הִ֖וא מֵאָ֣ה שְׁעָרִ֑ים וַֽ/יְבָרֲכֵ֖/הוּ יְהוָֽה
verses.en: And Yitschak sowed in that land, and found in that year a hundredfold; and YHWH blessed him.
verses.ar: فزَرَعَ إسحاقُ في تِلكَ الأرضِ فأصابَ في تِلكَ السَّنَةِ مِئَةَ ضِعفٍ؛ وبارَكَهُ الرَّبُّ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּגְדַּ֖ל הָ/אִ֑ישׁ וַ/יֵּ֤לֶךְ הָלוֹךְ֙ וְ/גָדֵ֔ל עַ֥ד כִּֽי גָדַ֖ל מְאֹֽד
verses.en: And the man grew great, and went on growing greater, until he was very great.
verses.ar: فعَظُمَ الرَّجُلُ ومَضى يَزدادُ عِظَمًا حَتّى عَظُمَ جِدًّا.

verses.he: וַֽ/יְהִי ל֤/וֹ מִקְנֵה צֹאן֙ וּ/מִקְנֵ֣ה בָקָ֔ר וַ/עֲבֻדָּ֖ה רַבָּ֑ה וַ/יְקַנְא֥וּ אֹת֖/וֹ פְּלִשְׁתִּֽים
verses.en: And he had possessions of flocks, and possessions of herds, and a great household; and the Pelishtim envied him.
verses.ar: وكانَ لَهُ مَواشي غَنَمٍ ومَواشي بَقَرٍ وحَشَمٌ كَثيرٌ؛ فحَسَدَهُ الفِلَسطينِيّونَ.

verses.he: וְ/כָל הַ/בְּאֵרֹ֗ת אֲשֶׁ֤ר חָֽפְרוּ֙ עַבְדֵ֣י אָבִ֔י/ו בִּ/ימֵ֖י אַבְרָהָ֣ם אָבִ֑י/ו סִתְּמ֣וּ/ם פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים וַ/יְמַלְא֖וּ/ם עָפָֽר
verses.en: And all the wells which the servants of his father had dug in the days of Avraham his father — the Pelishtim had stopped them up and filled them with earth.
verses.ar: وجَميعُ الآبارِ الَّتي حَفَرَها عَبيدُ أبيهِ في أيّامِ إبراهيمَ أبيهِ — سَدَّها الفِلَسطينِيّونَ ومَلَؤوها تُرابًا.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר אֲבִימֶ֖לֶךְ אֶל יִצְחָ֑ק לֵ֚ךְ מֵֽ/עִמָּ֔/נוּ כִּֽי עָצַֽמְתָּ מִמֶּ֖/נּוּ מְאֹֽד
verses.en: And Avimelekh said unto Yitschak: "Go away from us, for you have become far mightier than we."
verses.ar: فقالَ أبيمالِكُ لإسحاقَ: «اذهَبْ مِن عِندِنا، فإنَّكَ قَد أقوى مِنّا جِدًّا.»

verses.en: And Yitschak went from there, and he encamped in the valley of Gerar, and dwelt there.
verses.ar: فذَهَبَ إسحاقُ مِن هُناكَ ونَزَلَ في وادي جَرارَ وسَكَنَ هُناكَ.

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֨שָׁב יִצְחָ֜ק וַ/יַּחְפֹּ֣ר אֶת בְּאֵרֹ֣ת הַ/מַּ֗יִם אֲשֶׁ֤ר חָֽפְרוּ֙ בִּ/ימֵי֙ אַבְרָהָ֣ם אָבִ֔י/ו וַ/יְסַתְּמ֣וּ/ם פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים אַחֲרֵ֖י מ֣וֹת אַבְרָהָ֑ם וַ/יִּקְרָ֤א לָ/הֶן֙ שֵׁמ֔וֹת כַּ/שֵּׁמֹ֕ת אֲשֶׁר קָרָ֥א לָ/הֶ֖ן אָבִֽי/ו
verses.en: And Yitschak returned and dug again the wells of water which they had dug in the days of Avraham his father, and which the Pelishtim had stopped up after the death of Avraham; and he called them by the names, the same names by which his father had called them.
verses.ar: وعادَ إسحاقُ فحَفَرَ آبارَ الماءِ الَّتي حَفَروها في أيّامِ إبراهيمَ أبيهِ والَّتي سَدَّها الفِلَسطينِيّونَ بَعدَ مَوتِ إبراهيمَ؛ وسَمّاها بِالأسماءِ نَفسِها الَّتي سَمّاها أبوهُ.

verses.he: וַ/יַּחְפְּר֥וּ עַבְדֵֽי יִצְחָ֖ק בַּ/נָּ֑חַל וַ/יִּ֨מְצְאוּ שָׁ֔ם בְּאֵ֖ר מַ֥יִם חַיִּֽים
verses.en: And the servants of Yitschak dug in the valley, and they found there a well of living water.
verses.ar: وحَفَرَ عَبيدُ إسحاقَ في الوادي فوَجَدوا هُناكَ بِئرَ ماءٍ حَيٍّ.

verses.he: וַ/יָּרִ֜יבוּ רֹעֵ֣י גְרָ֗ר עִם רֹעֵ֥י יִצְחָ֛ק לֵ/אמֹ֖ר לָ֣/נוּ הַ/מָּ֑יִם וַ/יִּקְרָ֤א שֵֽׁם הַ/בְּאֵר֙ עֵ֔שֶׂק כִּ֥י הִֽתְעַשְּׂק֖וּ עִמּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And the shepherds of Gerar quarreled with the shepherds of Yitschak, saying: "The water is ours." And he called the name of the well Esek — "Contention" — for they contended with him.
verses.ar: فخاصَمَ رُعاةُ جَرارَ رُعاةَ إسحاقَ قائِلينَ: «الماءُ لَنا.» فسَمّى البِئرَ عِسقَ — «خِصامٌ» — لأنَّهُم خاصَموهُ.

verses.he: וַֽ/יַּחְפְּרוּ֙ בְּאֵ֣ר אַחֶ֔רֶת וַ/יָּרִ֖יבוּ גַּם עָלֶ֑י/הָ וַ/יִּקְרָ֥א שְׁמָ֖/הּ שִׂטְנָֽה
verses.en: And they dug another well, and they quarreled over it also; and he called its name Sitnah — "Accusation."
verses.ar: وحَفَروا بِئرًا أُخرى فخاصَموا عَلَيها أيضًا؛ فسَمّاها سِطنَةَ — «عَداوَةٌ».

verses.he: וַ/יַּעְתֵּ֣ק מִ/שָּׁ֗ם וַ/יַּחְפֹּר֙ בְּאֵ֣ר אַחֶ֔רֶת וְ/לֹ֥א רָב֖וּ עָלֶ֑י/הָ וַ/יִּקְרָ֤א שְׁמָ/הּ֙ רְחֹב֔וֹת וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר כִּֽי עַתָּ֞ה הִרְחִ֧יב יְהוָ֛ה לָ֖/נוּ וּ/פָרִ֥ינוּ בָ/אָֽרֶץ
verses.en: And he moved from there, and dug another well, and they did not quarrel over it; and he called its name Rechovot — "Wide-Spaces" — and said: "For now YHWH has made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land."
verses.ar: فانتَقَلَ مِن هُناكَ وحَفَرَ بِئرًا أُخرى فلَم يَتَخاصَموا عَلَيها؛ فسَمّاها رَحوبوتَ — «رَحابَةٌ» — وقالَ: «لأنَّ الرَّبَّ الآنَ قَد أوسَعَ لَنا فنُثمِرُ في الأرضِ.»

verses.en: And he went up from there to Beer-Sheva.
verses.ar: فصَعِدَ مِن هُناكَ إلى بِئرِ السَّبعِ.

verses.he: וַ/יֵּרָ֨א אֵלָ֤י/ו יְהוָה֙ בַּ/לַּ֣יְלָה הַ/ה֔וּא וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר אָנֹכִ֕י אֱלֹהֵ֖י אַבְרָהָ֣ם אָבִ֑י/ךָ אַל תִּירָא֙ כִּֽי אִתְּ/ךָ֣ אָנֹ֔כִי וּ/בֵֽרַכְתִּ֨י/ךָ֙ וְ/הִרְבֵּיתִ֣י אֶֽת זַרְעֲ/ךָ֔ בַּ/עֲב֖וּר אַבְרָהָ֥ם עַבְדִּֽ/י
verses.en: And YHWH appeared unto him that night and said: "I am the God of Avraham your father. Do not fear, for I am with you; and I will bless you and multiply your seed, for the sake of Avraham My servant."
verses.ar: فَتَجَلّى لَهُ الرَّبُّ تِلكَ اللَّيلَةَ وقالَ: «أنا إلهُ إبراهيمَ أبيكَ. لا تَخَفْ فإنّي مَعَكَ؛ وأُبارِكُكَ وأُكَثِّرُ نَسلَكَ مِن أجلِ إبراهيمَ عَبدي.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּ֧בֶן שָׁ֣ם מִזְבֵּ֗חַ וַ/יִּקְרָא֙ בְּ/שֵׁ֣ם יְהוָ֔ה וַ/יֶּט שָׁ֖ם אָהֳל֑/וֹ וַ/יִּכְרוּ שָׁ֥ם עַבְדֵי יִצְחָ֖ק בְּאֵֽר
verses.en: And he built there a slaughter-place, and called upon the name of YHWH, and pitched his tent there; and the servants of Yitschak dug there a well.
verses.ar: فبَنى هُناكَ مَذبَحًا ودَعا بِاسمِ الرَّبِّ ونَصَبَ هُناكَ خَيمَتَهُ؛ وحَفَرَ هُناكَ عَبيدُ إسحاقَ بِئرًا.

verses.he: וַ/אֲבִימֶ֕לֶךְ הָלַ֥ךְ אֵלָ֖י/ו מִ/גְּרָ֑ר וַ/אֲחֻזַּת֙ מֵֽרֵעֵ֔/הוּ וּ/פִיכֹ֖ל שַׂר צְבָאֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Avimelekh went unto him from Gerar, and Achuzzat his companion, and Pikhol the commander of his army.
verses.ar: وجاءَ إلَيهِ أبيمالِكُ مِن جَرارَ وأحُزَّتُ صاحِبُهُ وفيكولُ رَئيسُ جَيشِهِ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אֲלֵ/הֶם֙ יִצְחָ֔ק מַדּ֖וּעַ בָּאתֶ֣ם אֵלָ֑/י וְ/אַתֶּם֙ שְׂנֵאתֶ֣ם אֹתִ֔/י וַ/תְּשַׁלְּח֖וּ/נִי מֵ/אִתְּ/כֶֽם
verses.en: And Yitschak said unto them: "Why have you come unto me, seeing that you hated me and sent me away from you?"
verses.ar: فقالَ لَهُم إسحاقُ: «لِمَ جِئتُم إلَيَّ وأنتُم أبغَضتُموني وأرسَلتُموني مِن عِندِكُم؟»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאמְר֗וּ רָא֣וֹ רָאִינוּ֮ כִּֽי הָיָ֣ה יְהוָ֣ה עִמָּ/ךְ֒ וַ/נֹּ֗אמֶר תְּהִ֨י נָ֥א אָלָ֛ה בֵּינוֹתֵ֖י/נוּ בֵּינֵ֣י/נוּ וּ/בֵינֶ֑/ךָ וְ/נִכְרְתָ֥ה בְרִ֖ית עִמָּֽ/ךְ
verses.en: And they said: "We have seen, yes seen, that YHWH has been with you; and we said, 'Let there be, now, a sworn-oath between us, between us and you, and let us cut a cutting-bond with you —
verses.ar: فقالوا: «رَأينا رُؤيَةً أنَّ الرَّبَّ كانَ مَعَكَ؛ فقُلنا: لِيَكُنِ الآنَ قَسَمٌ بَينَنا — بَينَنا وبَينَكَ — ولنَقطَعْ عَهدَ قَطعٍ مَعَكَ —

verses.he: אִם תַּעֲשֵׂ֨ה עִמָּ֜/נוּ רָעָ֗ה כַּ/אֲשֶׁר֙ לֹ֣א נְגַֽעֲנ֔וּ/ךָ וְ/כַ/אֲשֶׁ֨ר עָשִׂ֤ינוּ עִמְּ/ךָ֙ רַק ט֔וֹב וַ/נְּשַׁלֵּֽחֲ/ךָ֖ בְּ/שָׁל֑וֹם אַתָּ֥ה עַתָּ֖ה בְּר֥וּךְ יְהוָֽה
verses.en: that you will do us no evil, just as we have not touched you, and just as we have done to you only good and have sent you away in wholeness. You are now the blessed of YHWH.'"
verses.ar: ألّا تَصنَعَ بِنا شَرًّا، كَما لَم نَمَسَّكَ وكَما لَم نَصنَعْ بِكَ إلّا خَيرًا وأرسَلناكَ بِسَلامٍ. أنتَ الآنَ مُبارَكُ الرَّبِّ.»

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֤עַשׂ לָ/הֶם֙ מִשְׁתֶּ֔ה וַ/יֹּאכְל֖וּ וַ/יִּשְׁתּֽוּ
verses.en: And he made them a feast, and they ate and drank.
verses.ar: فصَنَعَ لَهُم وَليمَةً فأكَلوا وشَرِبوا.

verses.he: וַ/יַּשְׁכִּ֣ימוּ בַ/בֹּ֔קֶר וַ/יִּשָּׁבְע֖וּ אִ֣ישׁ לְ/אָחִ֑י/ו וַ/יְשַׁלְּחֵ֣/ם יִצְחָ֔ק וַ/יֵּלְכ֥וּ מֵ/אִתּ֖/וֹ בְּ/שָׁלֽוֹם
verses.en: And they arose early in the morning, and swore each to his brother; and Yitschak sent them away, and they went from him in wholeness.
verses.ar: وبَكَّروا في الصَّباحِ وحَلَفَ كُلٌّ لأخيهِ؛ فأرسَلَهُم إسحاقُ وذَهَبوا مِن عِندِهِ بِسَلامٍ.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֣י בַּ/יּ֣וֹם הַ/ה֗וּא וַ/יָּבֹ֨אוּ֙ עַבְדֵ֣י יִצְחָ֔ק וַ/יַּגִּ֣דוּ ל֔/וֹ עַל אֹד֥וֹת הַ/בְּאֵ֖ר אֲשֶׁ֣ר חָפָ֑רוּ וַ/יֹּ֥אמְרוּ ל֖/וֹ מָצָ֥אנוּ מָֽיִם
verses.en: And it was on that day, that the servants of Yitschak came and told him concerning the well which they had dug, and they said to him: "We have found water."
verses.ar: وكانَ في ذلِكَ اليَومِ أنَّ عَبيدَ إسحاقَ جاؤوا وأخبَروهُ عَنِ البِئرِ الَّتي حَفَروها وقالوا لَهُ: «وَجَدنا ماءً.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרָ֥א אֹתָ֖/הּ שִׁבְעָ֑ה עַל כֵּ֤ן שֵׁם הָ/עִיר֙ בְּאֵ֣ר שֶׁ֔בַע עַ֖ד הַ/יּ֥וֹם הַ/זֶּֽה
verses.en: And he called it Shivah — "Seven" — therefore the name of the city is Beer-Sheva — "Well of the Oath" — unto this day.
verses.ar: فسَمّاها شِبعَةَ — «سَبعَةٌ» — لِذلِكَ اسمُ المَدينَةِ بِئرُ السَّبعِ — «بِئرُ القَسَمِ» — إلى هذا اليَومِ.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֤י עֵשָׂו֙ בֶּן אַרְבָּעִ֣ים שָׁנָ֔ה וַ/יִּקַּ֤ח אִשָּׁה֙ אֶת יְהוּדִ֔ית בַּת בְּאֵרִ֖י הַֽ/חִתִּ֑י וְ/אֶת בָּ֣שְׂמַ֔ת בַּת אֵילֹ֖ן הַֽ/חִתִּֽי
verses.en: And Esav was forty years old, and he took as wife Yehudit daughter of Beeri the Chittite, and Basemat daughter of Elon the Chittite.
verses.ar: وكانَ عيسو ابنَ أربَعينَ سَنَةً فأخَذَ امرَأةً: يَهوديتَ بِنتَ بِئيري الحِثِّيِّ، وبَسمَةَ بِنتَ إيلونَ الحِثِّيِّ.

verses.he: וַ/תִּהְיֶ֖יןָ מֹ֣רַת ר֑וּחַ לְ/יִצְחָ֖ק וּ/לְ/רִבְקָֽה
verses.en: And they were a bitterness of spirit unto Yitschak and unto Rivkah.
verses.ar: فكانَتا مَرارَةَ رُوحٍ لإسحاقَ ولِرِفقَةَ.

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Bereshit Chapter 26 — The Wells of Yitschak

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.3: גּוּר (gur) — "sojourn"
Root ג-ו-ר (g-w-r): to sojourn, to dwell as an alien. Yitschak is told to sojourn, not to settle permanently — the same condition of his father. The patriarchs are always sojourners, never owners (except of a burial cave). This echoes the Bahá'í and Qur'anic concept: this world is a place of passage, not a permanent home.

### v.5: מִשְׁמַרְתִּי, מִצְוֹתַי, חֻקּוֹתַי, וְתוֹרֹתָי (mishmarti, mitsvotai, ḥuqqotai, ve-torotai) — "My charge, My commandments, My statutes, and My teachings"
Four distinct terms for divine instruction: (1) מִשְׁמֶרֶת (mishmeret, charge/guarding) from ש-מ-ר (sh-m-r, to guard); (2) מִצְוָה (mitsvah, commandment) from צ-ו-ה (ts-w-h, to command); (3) חֻקָּה (ḥuqqah, statute) from ח-ק-ק (ḥ-q-q, to engrave/legislate); (4) תּוֹרָה (torah, teaching/instruction) from י-ר-ה (y-r-h, to teach/to throw/to direct). This is remarkable: the full vocabulary of Sinai-type legislation is attributed to Avraham BEFORE Sinai. Avraham already "kept the Torah" in some form.

### v.8: מְצַחֵק (metsaḥeq) — "laughing-with"
Root צ-ח-ק (ts-ḥ-q): to laugh, to play, to sport. The same root as Yitschak's name (יצחק, "he laughs"). Avimelekh sees Yitschak "laughing-with" (metsaḥeq) Rivqah — a play on his very name. Yitschak IS laughter, and he is caught laughing/playing with his wife. The wordplay is untranslatable but the text signals it: the man named "he-laughs" is seen laughing. The verb carries erotic overtones — this is intimate play.

### v.10: אָשָׁם (asham) — "guilt-debt"
Root א-ש-ם (ʾ-sh-m): guilt, guilt-offering, liability. NOT merely "guilt" as an emotion but a debt incurred by wrongdoing — a liability that must be discharged. Avimelekh speaks in legal terms: had someone lain with Rivqah, a guilt-debt would have been created that required payment.

### v.12: מֵאָה שְׁעָרִים (me'ah she'arim) — "a hundredfold"
Root ש-ע-ר (sh-ʿ-r): gate, measure, portion. The "hundred gates/measures" that Yitschak's sowing yields. The agricultural blessing is immediate and abundant — the land responds to the blessed one who sows in it.

### v.18-22: The well names
Three wells narrate a spiritual journey: (1) עֵשֶׂק (Esek, "Contention") — from ע-ש-ק (ʿ-s-q, to contend/oppress); (2) שִׂטְנָה (Sitnah, "Accusation") — from ש-ט-ן (s-ṭ-n, to accuse/oppose) — the same root as שָׂטָן (satan, "the accuser/adversary"); (3) רְחֹבוֹת (Rechovot, "Wide-Spaces") — from ר-ח-ב (r-ḥ-b, to be wide/spacious). The progression: contention, then accusation/adversity, then finally spaciousness. This is a pattern of spiritual progress — through struggle and opposition to expansiveness. Cognate with Arabic: شيطان (shayṭān) shares the root of Sitnah.

### v.19: מַיִם חַיִּים (mayim ḥayyim) — "living water"
Root ח-י-ה (ḥ-y-h): living. "Living water" is spring water — water that moves, flows, is fresh. Distinct from stagnant cistern water. The concept of "living water" recurs throughout Scripture as a symbol of divine sustenance. Cognate with Arabic ماء حي (mā' ḥayy) — living/flowing water.

### v.28: שְׁבוּעָה (shevuʿah) — "sworn-oath"
Root ש-ב-ע (sh-b-ʿ): to swear, related to שבע (shevaʿ, seven). The oath and the number seven are linguistically connected in Hebrew — to swear is to "seven oneself," possibly from the seven witnesses or seven sacrificial animals in covenant ceremonies. This connects to the place-name Be'er-Sheva: both "Well of the Oath" and "Well of Seven."

### v.28: בְּרִית (berit) — "cutting-bond"
The text uses כרת ברית (karat berit) — "cut a cutting-bond." The covenant between Yitschak and Avimelekh mirrors Avraham's earlier treaties (ch. 21). The foreign king recognizes divine blessing and seeks peace through covenant.

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| שטן (satan) | شيطان (shayṭān) | adversary/accuser | Throughout — the adversarial principle |
| תורה (torah) | — | teaching/instruction (cf. هداية, hidāyah) | 3:3 — God "sent down the Torah" |
| צחק (tsaḥaq) | ضحك (ḍaḥika) | to laugh | 11:71 — Sarah laughed at the angelic news |
| חיה (ḥayyah) | حياة (ḥayāh) | life/living | 2:164 — rain as "living water" reviving the earth |
| רחב (raḥav) | رحب (raḥuba) | spaciousness | The concept of divine expansiveness |
| שבע (shevaʿ) | سبع (sabʿ) | seven | Seven as the sacred number in both traditions |
| שלם (shalom) | سلام (salām) | peace/wholeness | 26:31 — they depart "in wholeness" (בשלום/بسلام) |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

The well-digging narrative is rich with spiritual symbolism. 'Abdu'l-Bahá teaches that wells represent the sources of divine knowledge — each Manifestation of God digs new wells (reveals new teachings), and the opponents of truth stop them up (suppress or distort the teachings). Yitschak re-digs his father's wells (revives his father's religion) and also digs new ones. The progression from Contention to Accusation to Wide-Spaces mirrors the pattern of every new revelation: initial opposition, then active persecution, then finally acceptance and expansion. The name Rechovot (Wide-Spaces) anticipates Bahá'u'lláh's declaration: "The earth is but one country, and mankind its citizens."

## Integrative Links

- **v.4 "in your seed shall all nations be blessed" <> Qur'an 21:107**: "We have not sent you except as a mercy to the worlds" — the universal blessing through the Abrahamic line
- **v.5 "My commandments, My statutes, My teachings" <> Qur'an 2:124**: "When his Lord tested Avraham with commands and he fulfilled them" — Avraham as the keeper of divine law before Sinai
- **v.18 "he called them by the same names" <> Qur'an 2:136**: The continuity of revelation — Yitschak preserves his father's work, as each prophet preserves the essential truth of the previous ones
- **v.22 "Rechovot — Wide-Spaces" <> Qur'an 39:10**: "The earth of God is spacious" — after contention and accusation, God provides expansiveness
- **v.24 "do not fear" <> Qur'an 41:30**: "Do not fear and do not grieve" — the divine assurance repeated across scriptures

chapters.title: Bereshit — פרק 27
chapters.subtitle: Genesis — Chapter 27

verses.he: וַ/יְהִי֙ כִּֽי זָקֵ֣ן יִצְחָ֔ק וַ/תִּכְהֶ֥יןָ עֵינָ֖י/ו מֵ/רְאֹ֑ת וַ/יִּקְרָ֞א אֶת עֵשָׂ֣ו בְּנ֣/וֹ הַ/גָּדֹ֗ל וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָי/ו֙ בְּנִ֔/י וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר אֵלָ֖י/ו הִנֵּֽנִ/י
verses.en: And it was, when Yitschak was old and his eyes were too dim to see, that he called Esav his elder son and said unto him: "My son." And he said unto him: "Here I am."
verses.ar: وكانَ حينَ شاخَ إسحاقُ وكَلَّت عَيناهُ عَنِ الرُّؤيَةِ أنَّهُ دَعا عيسو ابنَهُ الأكبَرَ وقالَ لَهُ: «يا بُنَيَّ.» فقالَ لَهُ: «ها أنا.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר הִנֵּה נָ֖א זָקַ֑נְתִּי לֹ֥א יָדַ֖עְתִּי י֥וֹם מוֹתִֽ/י
verses.en: And he said: "Behold now, I am old; I do not know the day of my death.
verses.ar: فقالَ: «ها أنا قَد شِختُ ولا أعلَمُ يَومَ مَوتي.

verses.he: וְ/עַתָּה֙ שָׂא נָ֣א כֵלֶ֔י/ךָ תֶּלְיְ/ךָ֖ וְ/קַשְׁתֶּ֑/ךָ וְ/צֵא֙ הַ/שָּׂדֶ֔ה וְ/צ֥וּדָ/ה לִּ֖/י צידה צָֽיִד
verses.en: And now, take up, I pray, your gear — your quiver and your bow — and go out to the field, and hunt me a hunt.
verses.ar: فالآنَ خُذْ أرجوكَ عُدَّتَكَ — جُعبَتَكَ وقَوسَكَ — واخرُجْ إلى الحَقلِ واصطَدْ لي صَيدًا.

verses.he: וַ/עֲשֵׂה לִ֨/י מַטְעַמִּ֜ים כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָהַ֛בְתִּי וְ/הָבִ֥יאָ/ה לִּ֖/י וְ/אֹכֵ֑לָה בַּ/עֲב֛וּר תְּבָרֶכְ/ךָ֥ נַפְשִׁ֖/י בְּ/טֶ֥רֶם אָמֽוּת
verses.en: And make me delicacies, such as I love, and bring them to me, and I will eat, so that my breath may bless you before I die."
verses.ar: واصنَعْ لي أطايِبَ كَما أُحِبُّ وأحضِرها لي فآكُلَ، لِكَي تُبارِكَكَ نَفسي قَبلَ أن أموتَ.»

verses.he: וְ/רִבְקָ֣ה שֹׁמַ֔עַת בְּ/דַבֵּ֣ר יִצְחָ֔ק אֶל עֵשָׂ֖ו בְּנ֑/וֹ וַ/יֵּ֤לֶךְ עֵשָׂו֙ הַ/שָּׂדֶ֔ה לָ/צ֥וּד צַ֖יִד לְ/הָבִֽיא
verses.en: And Rivkah was hearing when Yitschak spoke unto Esav his son. And Esav went to the field to hunt a hunt, to bring it.
verses.ar: ورِفقَةُ سامِعَةٌ حينَ كَلَّمَ إسحاقُ عيسو ابنَهُ. فذَهَبَ عيسو إلى الحَقلِ لِيَصطادَ صَيدًا لِيَأتيَ بِهِ.

verses.he: וְ/רִבְקָה֙ אָֽמְרָ֔ה אֶל יַעֲקֹ֥ב בְּנָ֖/הּ לֵ/אמֹ֑ר הִנֵּ֤ה שָׁמַ֨עְתִּי֙ אֶת אָבִ֔י/ךָ מְדַבֵּ֛ר אֶל עֵשָׂ֥ו אָחִ֖י/ךָ לֵ/אמֹֽר
verses.en: And Rivkah said unto Yaakov her son, saying: "Behold, I heard your father speaking unto Esav your brother, saying:
verses.ar: فقالَت رِفقَةُ ليعقوبَ ابنِها قائِلَةً: «ها قَد سَمِعتُ أباكَ يُكَلِّمُ عيسو أخاكَ قائِلًا:

verses.he: הָבִ֨יאָ/ה לִּ֥/י צַ֛יִד וַ/עֲשֵׂה לִ֥/י מַטְעַמִּ֖ים וְ/אֹכֵ֑לָה וַ/אֲבָרֶכְ/כָ֛ה לִ/פְנֵ֥י יְהוָ֖ה לִ/פְנֵ֥י מוֹתִֽ/י
verses.en: 'Bring me game and make me delicacies, and I will eat, and I will bless you before YHWH, before my death.'
verses.ar: 'ائتِني بِصَيدٍ واصنَعْ لي أطايِبَ فآكُلَ وأُبارِكَكَ أمامَ الرَّبِّ قَبلَ مَوتي.'

verses.he: וְ/עַתָּ֥ה בְנִ֖/י שְׁמַ֣ע בְּ/קֹלִ֑/י לַ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר אֲנִ֖י מְצַוָּ֥ה אֹתָֽ/ךְ
verses.en: And now, my son, heed my voice, in that which I command you.
verses.ar: والآنَ يا بُنَيَّ اسمَعْ صَوتي فيما آمُرُكَ.

verses.he: לֶךְ נָא֙ אֶל הַ/צֹּ֔אן וְ/קַֽח לִ֣/י מִ/שָּׁ֗ם שְׁנֵ֛י גְּדָיֵ֥י עִזִּ֖ים טֹבִ֑ים וְ/אֶֽעֱשֶׂ֨ה אֹתָ֧/ם מַטְעַמִּ֛ים לְ/אָבִ֖י/ךָ כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָהֵֽב
verses.en: Go, I pray, to the flock, and take for me from there two good kids of goats, and I will make them into delicacies for your father, such as he loves.
verses.ar: اذهَبْ أرجوكَ إلى القَطيعِ وخُذْ لي مِن هُناكَ جَديَينِ طَيِّبَينِ مِنَ المَعزِ، فأصنَعَهُما أطايِبَ لأبيكَ كَما يُحِبُّ.

verses.he: וְ/הֵבֵאתָ֥ לְ/אָבִ֖י/ךָ וְ/אָכָ֑ל בַּ/עֲבֻ֛ר אֲשֶׁ֥ר יְבָרֶכְ/ךָ֖ לִ/פְנֵ֥י מוֹתֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And you shall bring them to your father, and he will eat, so that he may bless you before his death."
verses.ar: وتُحضِرُها لأبيكَ فيَأكُلَ لِكَي يُبارِكَكَ قَبلَ مَوتِهِ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֔ב אֶל רִבְקָ֖ה אִמּ֑/וֹ הֵ֣ן עֵשָׂ֤ו אָחִ/י֙ אִ֣ישׁ שָׂעִ֔ר וְ/אָנֹכִ֖י אִ֥ישׁ חָלָֽק
verses.en: And Yaakov said unto Rivkah his mother: "Behold, Esav my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man.
verses.ar: فقالَ يعقوبُ لِرِفقَةَ أُمِّهِ: «ها عيسو أخي رَجُلٌ أشعَرُ وأنا رَجُلٌ أملَسُ.

verses.he: אוּלַ֤י יְמֻשֵּׁ֨/נִי֙ אָבִ֔/י וְ/הָיִ֥יתִי בְ/עֵינָ֖י/ו כִּ/מְתַעְתֵּ֑עַ וְ/הֵבֵאתִ֥י עָלַ֛/י קְלָלָ֖ה וְ/לֹ֥א בְרָכָֽה
verses.en: Perhaps my father will feel me, and I shall be in his eyes as a mocker, and I shall bring upon myself a curse and not a blessing."
verses.ar: رُبَّما يَجُسُّني أبي فأكونُ في عَينَيهِ كَمُستَهزِئٍ وأجلِبُ على نَفسي لَعنَةً لا بَرَكَةً.»

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֤אמֶר ל/וֹ֙ אִמּ֔/וֹ עָלַ֥/י קִלְלָתְ/ךָ֖ בְּנִ֑/י אַ֛ךְ שְׁמַ֥ע בְּ/קֹלִ֖/י וְ/לֵ֥ךְ קַֽח לִֽ/י
verses.en: And his mother said unto him: "Upon me be your curse, my son. Only heed my voice, and go, take for me."
verses.ar: فقالَت لَهُ أُمُّهُ: «عَلَيَّ لَعنَتُكَ يا بُنَيَّ. فَقَطِ اسمَعْ صَوتي واذهَبْ خُذْ لي.»

verses.he: וַ/יֵּ֨לֶךְ֙ וַ/יִּקַּ֔ח וַ/יָּבֵ֖א לְ/אִמּ֑/וֹ וַ/תַּ֤עַשׂ אִמּ/וֹ֙ מַטְעַמִּ֔ים כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֖ר אָהֵ֥ב אָבִֽי/ו
verses.en: And he went, and took, and brought them to his mother; and his mother made delicacies, such as his father loved.
verses.ar: فذَهَبَ وأخَذَ وأحضَرَ لأُمِّهِ؛ فصَنَعَت أُمُّهُ أطايِبَ كَما يُحِبُّ أبوهُ.

verses.he: וַ/תִּקַּ֣ח רִ֠בְקָה אֶת בִּגְדֵ֨י עֵשָׂ֜ו בְּנָ֤/הּ הַ/גָּדֹל֙ הַ/חֲמֻדֹ֔ת אֲשֶׁ֥ר אִתָּ֖/הּ בַּ/בָּ֑יִת וַ/תַּלְבֵּ֥שׁ אֶֽת יַעֲקֹ֖ב בְּנָ֥/הּ הַ/קָּטָֽן
verses.en: And Rivkah took the garments of Esav her elder son — the coveted ones — which were with her in the house, and clothed Yaakov her younger son.
verses.ar: وأخَذَت رِفقَةُ ثِيابَ عيسو ابنِها الأكبَرِ — الفاخِرَةَ — الَّتي عِندَها في البَيتِ، وألبَسَتها يعقوبَ ابنَها الأصغَرَ.

verses.he: וְ/אֵ֗ת עֹרֹת֙ גְּדָיֵ֣י הָֽ/עִזִּ֔ים הִלְבִּ֖ישָׁה עַל יָדָ֑י/ו וְ/עַ֖ל חֶלְקַ֥ת צַוָּארָֽי/ו
verses.en: And the skins of the kids of goats she put upon his hands and upon the smooth of his neck.
verses.ar: وجُلودَ جَديَيِ المَعزِ ألبَسَتها على يَدَيهِ وعلى مَلاسَةِ عُنُقِهِ.

verses.he: וַ/תִּתֵּ֧ן אֶת הַ/מַּטְעַמִּ֛ים וְ/אֶת הַ/לֶּ֖חֶם אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשָׂ֑תָה בְּ/יַ֖ד יַעֲקֹ֥ב בְּנָֽ/הּ
verses.en: And she gave the delicacies and the bread which she had made into the hand of Yaakov her son.
verses.ar: وأعطَتِ الأطايِبَ والخُبزَ الَّتي صَنَعَت في يَدِ يعقوبَ ابنِها.

verses.he: וַ/יָּבֹ֥א אֶל אָבִ֖י/ו וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר אָבִ֑/י וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר הִנֶּ֔נִּ/י מִ֥י אַתָּ֖ה בְּנִֽ/י
verses.en: And he came unto his father and said: "My father." And he said: "Here I am. Who are you, my son?"
verses.ar: فدَخَلَ على أبيهِ وقالَ: «يا أبي.» فقالَ: «ها أنا. مَن أنتَ يا بُنَيَّ؟»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֜ב אֶל אָבִ֗י/ו אָנֹכִי֙ עֵשָׂ֣ו בְּכֹרֶ֔/ךָ עָשִׂ֕יתִי כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר דִּבַּ֖רְתָּ אֵלָ֑/י קֽוּם נָ֣א שְׁבָ֗/ה וְ/אָכְלָ/ה֙ מִ/צֵּידִ֔/י בַּ/עֲב֖וּר תְּבָרֲכַ֥/נִּי נַפְשֶֽׁ/ךָ
verses.en: And Yaakov said unto his father: "I am Esav your firstborn. I have done as you spoke unto me. Rise up, I pray, sit and eat from my hunt, so that your breath may bless me."
verses.ar: فقالَ يعقوبُ لأبيهِ: «أنا عيسو بِكرُكَ. فَعَلتُ كَما كَلَّمتَني. قُمْ أرجوكَ اجلِسْ وكُلْ مِن صَيدي لِكَي تُبارِكَني نَفسُكَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר יִצְחָק֙ אֶל בְּנ֔/וֹ מַה זֶּ֛ה מִהַ֥רְתָּ לִ/מְצֹ֖א בְּנִ֑/י וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר כִּ֥י הִקְרָ֛ה יְהוָ֥ה אֱלֹהֶ֖י/ךָ לְ/פָנָֽ/י
verses.en: And Yitschak said unto his son: "How is it that you found it so quickly, my son?" And he said: "Because YHWH your God caused it to happen before me."
verses.ar: فقالَ إسحاقُ لِابنِهِ: «كَيفَ وَجَدتَهُ سَريعًا يا بُنَيَّ؟» فقالَ: «لأنَّ الرَّبَّ إلهَكَ أوقَعَهُ أمامي.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר יִצְחָק֙ אֶֽל יַעֲקֹ֔ב גְּשָׁ/ה נָּ֥א וַ/אֲמֻֽשְׁ/ךָ֖ בְּנִ֑/י הַֽ/אַתָּ֥ה זֶ֛ה בְּנִ֥/י עֵשָׂ֖ו אִם לֹֽא
verses.en: And Yitschak said unto Yaakov: "Come near, I pray, and let me feel you, my son — are you truly my son Esav, or not?"
verses.ar: فقالَ إسحاقُ ليعقوبَ: «ادنُ أرجوكَ فأجُسَّكَ يا بُنَيَّ — أأنتَ حَقًّا ابني عيسو أم لا؟»

verses.he: וַ/יִּגַּ֧שׁ יַעֲקֹ֛ב אֶל יִצְחָ֥ק אָבִ֖י/ו וַ/יְמֻשֵּׁ֑/הוּ וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר הַ/קֹּל֙ ק֣וֹל יַעֲקֹ֔ב וְ/הַ/יָּדַ֖יִם יְדֵ֥י עֵשָֽׂו
verses.en: And Yaakov drew near unto Yitschak his father, and he felt him; and he said: "The voice is the voice of Yaakov, but the hands are the hands of Esav."
verses.ar: فدَنا يعقوبُ مِن إسحاقَ أبيهِ فجَسَّهُ وقالَ: «الصَّوتُ صَوتُ يعقوبَ، لكِنَّ اليَدَينِ يَدا عيسو.»

verses.he: וְ/לֹ֣א הִכִּיר֔/וֹ כִּֽי הָי֣וּ יָדָ֗י/ו כִּ/ידֵ֛י עֵשָׂ֥ו אָחִ֖י/ו שְׂעִרֹ֑ת וַֽ/יְבָרְכֵֽ/הוּ
verses.en: And he did not recognize him, for his hands were hairy like the hands of Esav his brother; and he blessed him.
verses.ar: ولَم يَعرِفهُ لأنَّ يَدَيهِ كانَتا شَعراوَينِ كَيَدَي عيسو أخيهِ؛ فبارَكَهُ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר אַתָּ֥ה זֶ֖ה בְּנִ֣/י עֵשָׂ֑ו וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר אָֽנִי
verses.en: And he said: "Are you truly my son Esav?" And he said: "I am."
verses.ar: فقالَ: «أأنتَ حَقًّا ابني عيسو؟» فقالَ: «أنا هُوَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר הַגִּ֤שָׁ/ה לִּ/י֙ וְ/אֹֽכְלָה֙ מִ/צֵּ֣יד בְּנִ֔/י לְמַ֥עַן תְּבָֽרֶכְ/ךָ֖ נַפְשִׁ֑/י וַ/יַּגֶּשׁ ל/וֹ֙ וַ/יֹּאכַ֔ל וַ/יָּ֧בֵא ל֦/וֹ יַ֖יִן וַ/יֵּֽשְׁתְּ
verses.en: And he said: "Bring it near to me, and I will eat from the hunt of my son, so that my breath may bless you." And he brought it near to him, and he ate; and he brought him wine, and he drank.
verses.ar: فقالَ: «قَدِّمْ لي فآكُلَ مِن صَيدِ ابني لِكَي تُبارِكَكَ نَفسي.» فقَدَّمَ لَهُ فأكَلَ؛ وأحضَرَ لَهُ خَمرًا فشَرِبَ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר אֵלָ֖י/ו יִצְחָ֣ק אָבִ֑י/ו גְּשָׁ/ה נָּ֥א וּ/שְׁקָ/ה לִּ֖/י בְּנִֽ/י
verses.en: And Yitschak his father said unto him: "Come near, I pray, and kiss me, my son."
verses.ar: فقالَ لَهُ إسحاقُ أبوهُ: «ادنُ أرجوكَ وقَبِّلني يا بُنَيَّ.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּגַּשׁ֙ וַ/יִּשַּׁק ל֔/וֹ וַ/יָּ֛רַח אֶת רֵ֥יחַ בְּגָדָ֖י/ו וַֽ/יְבָרֲכֵ֑/הוּ וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר רְאֵה֙ רֵ֣יחַ בְּנִ֔/י כְּ/רֵ֣יחַ שָׂדֶ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר בֵּרֲכ֖/וֹ יְהוָֽה
verses.en: And he came near and kissed him; and he smelled the scent of his garments, and he blessed him, and said: "See, the scent of my son is as the scent of a field which YHWH has blessed.
verses.ar: فدَنا وقَبَّلَهُ؛ فشَمَّ رائِحَةَ ثِيابِهِ فبارَكَهُ وقالَ: «انظُرْ، رائِحَةُ ابني كَرائِحَةِ حَقلٍ بارَكَهُ الرَّبُّ.

verses.he: וְ/יִֽתֶּן לְ/ךָ֙ הָ/אֱלֹהִ֔ים מִ/טַּל֙ הַ/שָּׁמַ֔יִם וּ/מִ/שְׁמַנֵּ֖י הָ/אָ֑רֶץ וְ/רֹ֥ב דָּגָ֖ן וְ/תִירֹֽשׁ
verses.en: And may God give you of the dew of the heavens, and of the fatness of the earth, and abundance of grain and new wine.
verses.ar: فلِيُعطِكَ اللهُ مِن نَدى السَّماواتِ ومِن دَسَمِ الأرضِ وكَثرَةَ حِنطَةٍ وخَمرٍ جَديدٍ.

verses.he: יַֽעַבְד֣וּ/ךָ עַמִּ֗ים ו/ישתחו וְ/יִֽשְׁתַּחֲו֤וּ לְ/ךָ֙ לְאֻמִּ֔ים הֱוֵ֤ה גְבִיר֙ לְ/אַחֶ֔י/ךָ וְ/יִשְׁתַּחֲוּ֥וּ לְ/ךָ֖ בְּנֵ֣י אִמֶּ֑/ךָ אֹרְרֶ֣י/ךָ אָר֔וּר וּֽ/מְבָרֲכֶ֖י/ךָ בָּרֽוּךְ
verses.en: Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be lord over your brothers, and let the sons of your mother bow down to you. Those who curse you — cursed; and those who bless you — blessed."
verses.ar: لِتَخدِمكَ شُعوبٌ ولتَسجُدْ لَكَ أُمَمٌ. كُنْ سَيِّدًا لإخوَتِكَ ولِيَسجُدْ لَكَ بَنو أُمِّكَ. لاعِنوكَ مَلعونونَ ومُبارِكوكَ مُبارَكونَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יְהִ֗י כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֨ר כִּלָּ֣ה יִצְחָק֮ לְ/בָרֵ֣ךְ אֶֽת יַעֲקֹב֒ וַ/יְהִ֗י אַ֣ךְ יָצֹ֤א יָצָא֙ יַעֲקֹ֔ב מֵ/אֵ֥ת פְּנֵ֖י יִצְחָ֣ק אָבִ֑י/ו וְ/עֵשָׂ֣ו אָחִ֔י/ו בָּ֖א מִ/צֵּידֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And it was, as Yitschak had finished, yes finished, blessing Yaakov — and Yaakov had just, yes just, gone out from the face of Yitschak his father — that Esav his brother came in from his hunt.
verses.ar: وكانَ حينَ فَرَغَ إسحاقُ مِن مُبارَكَةِ يعقوبَ — ويعقوبُ قَد خَرَجَ خُروجًا مِن أمامِ إسحاقَ أبيهِ — أنَّ عيسو أخاهُ جاءَ مِن صَيدِهِ.

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֤עַשׂ גַּם הוּא֙ מַטְעַמִּ֔ים וַ/יָּבֵ֖א לְ/אָבִ֑י/ו וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר לְ/אָבִ֗י/ו יָקֻ֤ם אָבִ/י֙ וְ/יֹאכַל֙ מִ/צֵּ֣יד בְּנ֔/וֹ בַּ/עֲב֖וּר תְּבָרֲכַ֥/נִּי נַפְשֶֽׁ/ךָ
verses.en: And he also made delicacies, and brought them to his father, and said to his father: "Let my father arise and eat from the hunt of his son, so that your breath may bless me."
verses.ar: وصَنَعَ هُوَ أيضًا أطايِبَ وأحضَرَها لأبيهِ وقالَ لأبيهِ: «لِيَقُمْ أبي ويَأكُلْ مِن صَيدِ ابنِهِ لِكَي تُبارِكَني نَفسُكَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר ל֛/וֹ יִצְחָ֥ק אָבִ֖י/ו מִי אָ֑תָּה וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר אֲנִ֛י בִּנְ/ךָ֥ בְכֹֽרְ/ךָ֖ עֵשָֽׂו
verses.en: And Yitschak his father said to him: "Who are you?" And he said: "I am your son, your firstborn, Esav."
verses.ar: فقالَ لَهُ إسحاقُ أبوهُ: «مَن أنتَ؟» فقالَ: «أنا ابنُكَ بِكرُكَ عيسو.»

verses.he: וַ/יֶּחֱרַ֨ד יִצְחָ֣ק חֲרָדָה֮ גְּדֹלָ֣ה עַד מְאֹד֒ וַ/יֹּ֡אמֶר מִֽי אֵפ֡וֹא ה֣וּא הַ/צָּֽד צַיִד֩ וַ/יָּ֨בֵא לִ֜/י וָ/אֹכַ֥ל מִ/כֹּ֛ל בְּ/טֶ֥רֶם תָּב֖וֹא וָ/אֲבָרֲכֵ֑/הוּ גַּם בָּר֖וּךְ יִהְיֶֽה
verses.en: And Yitschak trembled a trembling great beyond measure, and said: "Who then is the one who hunted a hunt and brought it to me, and I ate of all before you came, and I blessed him? Indeed, blessed he shall be."
verses.ar: فارتَعَدَ إسحاقُ ارتِعادًا عَظيمًا فَوقَ الحَدِّ وقالَ: «فمَن ذاكَ الَّذي اصطادَ صَيدًا وأتاني بِهِ فأكَلتُ مِنَ الكُلِّ قَبلَ أن تَأتيَ وبارَكتُهُ؟ مُبارَكًا يَكونُ.»

verses.he: כִּ/שְׁמֹ֤עַ עֵשָׂו֙ אֶת דִּבְרֵ֣י אָבִ֔י/ו וַ/יִּצְעַ֣ק צְעָקָ֔ה גְּדֹלָ֥ה וּ/מָרָ֖ה עַד מְאֹ֑ד וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר לְ/אָבִ֔י/ו בָּרֲכֵ֥/נִי גַם אָ֖נִי אָבִֽ/י
verses.en: When Esav heard the words of his father, he cried a cry great and exceedingly bitter, and said to his father: "Bless me — me also — my father!"
verses.ar: فلَمّا سَمِعَ عيسو كَلامَ أبيهِ صَرَخَ صَرخَةً عَظيمَةً ومُرَّةً جِدًّا وقالَ لأبيهِ: «بارِكني — أنا أيضًا — يا أبي!»
