verses.en: And Lavan had gone to shear his flock; and Rachel stole the divination-figures that belonged to her father.
verses.ar: ولابانُ كانَ قَد ذَهَبَ لِيَجُزَّ غَنَمَهُ؛ فَسَرَقَت راحيلُ صُوَرَ العِرافَةِ الَّتي لِأبيها.

verses.he: וַ/יִּגְנֹ֣ב יַעֲקֹ֔ב אֶת לֵ֥ב לָבָ֖ן הָ/אֲרַמִּ֑י עַל בְּלִי֙ הִגִּ֣יד ל֔/וֹ כִּ֥י בֹרֵ֖חַ הֽוּא
verses.en: And Yaakov stole the heart of Lavan the Aramean, in that he did not tell him that he was fleeing.
verses.ar: وسَرَقَ يَعقوبُ قَلبَ لابانَ الآرامِيِّ، إذ لَم يُخبِرهُ أنَّهُ فارٌّ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּבְרַ֥ח הוּא֙ וְ/כָל אֲשֶׁר ל֔/וֹ וַ/יָּ֖קָם וַ/יַּעֲבֹ֣ר אֶת הַ/נָּהָ֑ר וַ/יָּ֥שֶׂם אֶת פָּנָ֖י/ו הַ֥ר הַ/גִּלְעָֽד
verses.en: And he fled — he and all that was his — and he arose and crossed the River, and set his face toward the hill-country of Gilad.
verses.ar: ففَرَّ هُوَ وكُلُّ ما لَهُ، وقامَ وعَبَرَ النَّهرَ، ووَجَّهَ وَجهَهُ نَحوَ جَبَلِ جِلعادَ.

verses.he: וַ/יֻּגַּ֥ד לְ/לָבָ֖ן בַּ/יּ֣וֹם הַ/שְּׁלִישִׁ֑י כִּ֥י בָרַ֖ח יַעֲקֹֽב
verses.en: And it was told to Lavan on the third day that Yaakov had fled.
verses.ar: فأُخبِرَ لابانُ في اليَومِ الثّالِثِ أنَّ يَعقوبَ قَد فَرَّ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקַּ֤ח אֶת אֶחָי/ו֙ עִמּ֔/וֹ וַ/יִּרְדֹּ֣ף אַחֲרָ֔י/ו דֶּ֖רֶךְ שִׁבְעַ֣ת יָמִ֑ים וַ/יַּדְבֵּ֥ק אֹת֖/וֹ בְּ/הַ֥ר הַ/גִּלְעָֽד
verses.en: And he took his kinsmen with him, and pursued after him a journey of seven days, and overtook him in the hill-country of Gilad.
verses.ar: فأخَذَ أقارِبَهُ مَعَهُ ولَحِقَ بِهِ مَسيرَةَ سَبعَةِ أيّامٍ، وأدرَكَهُ في جَبَلِ جِلعادَ.

verses.he: וַ/יָּבֹ֧א אֱלֹהִ֛ים אֶל לָבָ֥ן הָ/אֲרַמִּ֖י בַּ/חֲלֹ֣ם הַ/לָּ֑יְלָה וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר ל֗/וֹ הִשָּׁ֧מֶר לְ/ךָ֛ פֶּן תְּדַבֵּ֥ר עִֽם יַעֲקֹ֖ב מִ/טּ֥וֹב עַד רָֽע
verses.en: And God came unto Lavan the Aramean in a dream of the night, and said unto him: "Guard yourself, lest you speak with Yaakov from good to evil."
verses.ar: فَجاءَ اللهُ إلى لابانَ الآرامِيِّ في حُلمِ اللَّيلِ وقالَ لَهُ: «احذَرْ لِنَفسِكَ أن تُكَلِّمَ يَعقوبَ مِن خَيرٍ إلى شَرٍّ.»

verses.he: וַ/יַּשֵּׂ֥ג לָבָ֖ן אֶֽת יַעֲקֹ֑ב וְ/יַעֲקֹ֗ב תָּקַ֤ע אֶֽת אָהֳל/וֹ֙ בָּ/הָ֔ר וְ/לָבָ֛ן תָּקַ֥ע אֶת אֶחָ֖י/ו בְּ/הַ֥ר הַ/גִּלְעָֽד
verses.en: And Lavan overtook Yaakov; and Yaakov had pitched his tent in the hill-country, and Lavan pitched with his kinsmen in the hill-country of Gilad.
verses.ar: وأدرَكَ لابانُ يَعقوبَ؛ ويَعقوبُ كانَ قَد نَصَبَ خَيمَتَهُ في الجَبَلِ، ولابانُ نَصَبَ مَعَ أقارِبِهِ في جَبَلِ جِلعادَ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר לָבָן֙ לְ/יַעֲקֹ֔ב מֶ֣ה עָשִׂ֔יתָ וַ/תִּגְנֹ֖ב אֶת לְבָבִ֑/י וַ/תְּנַהֵג֙ אֶת בְּנֹתַ֔/י כִּ/שְׁבֻי֖וֹת חָֽרֶב
verses.en: And Lavan said unto Yaakov: "What have you done, that you stole my heart, and drove my daughters as captives of the sword?
verses.ar: فقالَ لابانُ لِيَعقوبَ: «ماذا صَنَعتَ؟ سَرَقتَ قَلبي، وسُقتَ بَناتي كَسَبايا السَّيفِ!

verses.en: Why did you hide yourself to flee, and steal from me, and not tell me? I would have sent you away with joy and with songs, with drum and with lyre.
verses.ar: لِماذا اختَبَأتَ لِتَفِرَّ، وسَرَقتَ مِنّي، ولَم تُخبِرني؟ فَكُنتُ أُرسِلُكَ بِفَرَحٍ وبِأغانٍ، بِدُفٍّ وبِعودٍ.

verses.en: And you did not allow me to kiss my sons and my daughters. Now you have done foolishly.
verses.ar: ولَم تَدَعني أُقَبِّلُ بَنِيَّ وبَناتي. الآنَ قَد فَعَلتَ حَماقَةً.

verses.he: יֶשׁ לְ/אֵ֣ל יָדִ֔/י לַ/עֲשׂ֥וֹת עִמָּ/כֶ֖ם רָ֑ע וֵֽ/אלֹהֵ֨י אֲבִי/כֶ֜ם אֶ֣מֶשׁ אָמַ֧ר אֵלַ֣/י לֵ/אמֹ֗ר הִשָּׁ֧מֶר לְ/ךָ֛ מִ/דַּבֵּ֥ר עִֽם יַעֲקֹ֖ב מִ/טּ֥וֹב עַד רָֽע
verses.en: It is in the power of my hand to do you evil; but the God of your father spoke unto me last night, saying: 'Guard yourself from speaking with Yaakov from good to evil.'
verses.ar: في طاقَةِ يَدي أن أصنَعَ بِكُم سوءًا؛ لكِنَّ إلهَ أبيكُم كَلَّمَني البارِحَةَ قائِلًا: 'احذَرْ مِن أن تُكَلِّمَ يَعقوبَ مِن خَيرٍ إلى شَرٍّ.'

verses.he: וְ/עַתָּה֙ הָלֹ֣ךְ הָלַ֔כְתָּ כִּֽי נִכְסֹ֥ף נִכְסַ֖פְתָּה לְ/בֵ֣ית אָבִ֑י/ךָ לָ֥/מָּה גָנַ֖בְתָּ אֶת אֱלֹהָֽ/י
verses.en: And now — you have gone, yes gone, because you longed, yes longed, for the house of your father — but why did you steal my gods?"
verses.ar: والآنَ — ذَهَبتَ ذَهابًا لِأنَّكَ اشتَقتَ اشتِياقًا إلى بَيتِ أبيكَ — فَلِماذا سَرَقتَ آلِهَتي؟»

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֥עַן יַעֲקֹ֖ב וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר לְ/לָבָ֑ן כִּ֣י יָרֵ֔אתִי כִּ֣י אָמַ֔רְתִּי פֶּן תִּגְזֹ֥ל אֶת בְּנוֹתֶ֖י/ךָ מֵ/עִמִּֽ/י
verses.en: And Yaakov answered and said unto Lavan: "Because I was afraid, for I said: 'Lest you tear your daughters from me.'
verses.ar: فأجابَ يَعقوبُ وقالَ لِلابانَ: «لِأنّي خِفتُ، إذ قُلتُ: 'لَعَلَّكَ تَنتَزِعُ بَناتِكَ مِنّي.'

verses.he: עִ֠ם אֲשֶׁ֨ר תִּמְצָ֣א אֶת אֱלֹהֶי/ךָ֮ לֹ֣א יִֽחְיֶה֒ נֶ֣גֶד אַחֵ֧י/נוּ הַֽכֶּר לְ/ךָ֛ מָ֥ה עִמָּדִ֖/י וְ/קַֽח לָ֑/ךְ וְ/לֹֽא יָדַ֣ע יַעֲקֹ֔ב כִּ֥י רָחֵ֖ל גְּנָבָֽתַ/ם
verses.en: With whomever you find your gods — he shall not live. Before our kinsmen, discern for yourself what is with me, and take it for yourself." For Yaakov did not know that Rachel had stolen them.
verses.ar: مَن تَجِدُ عِندَهُ آلِهَتَكَ — لا يَحيا. أمامَ أقارِبِنا تَبَيَّنْ لِنَفسِكَ ما عِندي، وخُذهُ لَكَ.» فَلَم يَكُن يَعقوبُ يَعلَمُ أنَّ راحيلَ سَرَقَتها.

verses.he: וַ/יָּבֹ֨א לָבָ֜ן בְּ/אֹ֥הֶל יַעֲקֹ֣ב וּ/בְ/אֹ֣הֶל לֵאָ֗ה וּ/בְ/אֹ֛הֶל שְׁתֵּ֥י הָ/אֲמָהֹ֖ת וְ/לֹ֣א מָצָ֑א וַ/יֵּצֵא֙ מֵ/אֹ֣הֶל לֵאָ֔ה וַ/יָּבֹ֖א בְּ/אֹ֥הֶל רָחֵֽל
verses.en: And Lavan went into the tent of Yaakov, and into the tent of Leah, and into the tent of the two handmaids, and did not find them. And he went out of the tent of Leah, and came into the tent of Rachel.
verses.ar: فَدَخَلَ لابانُ خَيمَةَ يَعقوبَ، وخَيمَةَ لَيئَةَ، وخَيمَةَ الجارِيَتَينِ، فَلَم يَجِدْ. وخَرَجَ مِن خَيمَةِ لَيئَةَ ودَخَلَ خَيمَةَ راحيلَ.

verses.he: וְ/רָחֵ֞ל לָקְחָ֣ה אֶת הַ/תְּרָפִ֗ים וַ/תְּשִׂמֵ֛/ם בְּ/כַ֥ר הַ/גָּמָ֖ל וַ/תֵּ֣שֶׁב עֲלֵי/הֶ֑ם וַ/יְמַשֵּׁ֥שׁ לָבָ֛ן אֶת כָּל הָ/אֹ֖הֶל וְ/לֹ֥א מָצָֽא
verses.en: And Rachel had taken the divination-figures and put them in the camel-saddle, and sat upon them. And Lavan felt through the whole tent, and did not find them.
verses.ar: وراحيلُ كانَت قَد أخَذَت صُوَرَ العِرافَةِ ووَضَعَتها في رَحلِ الجَمَلِ وجَلَسَت عَلَيها. فَتَحَسَّسَ لابانُ الخَيمَةَ كُلَّها فَلَم يَجِدها.

verses.he: וַ/תֹּ֣אמֶר אֶל אָבִ֗י/הָ אַל יִ֨חַר֙ בְּ/עֵינֵ֣י אֲדֹנִ֔/י כִּ֣י ל֤וֹא אוּכַל֙ לָ/ק֣וּם מִ/פָּנֶ֔י/ךָ כִּי דֶ֥רֶךְ נָשִׁ֖ים לִ֑/י וַ/יְחַפֵּ֕שׂ וְ/לֹ֥א מָצָ֖א אֶת הַ/תְּרָפִֽים
verses.en: And she said unto her father: "Let it not burn in the eyes of my lord that I cannot rise before you, for the way of women is upon me." And he searched, and did not find the divination-figures.
verses.ar: فَقالَت لِأبيها: «لا يَحتَدَّ في عَينَي سَيِّدي أنّي لا أستَطيعُ القِيامَ أمامَكَ، فإنَّ عادَةَ النِّساءِ عَلَيَّ.» ففَتَّشَ فَلَم يَجِد صُوَرَ العِرافَةِ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּ֥חַר לְ/יַעֲקֹ֖ב וַ/יָּ֣רֶב בְּ/לָבָ֑ן וַ/יַּ֤עַן יַעֲקֹב֙ וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר לְ/לָבָ֔ן מַה פִּשְׁעִ/י֙ מַ֣ה חַטָּאתִ֔/י כִּ֥י דָלַ֖קְתָּ אַחֲרָֽ/י
verses.en: And Yaakov burned, and contended with Lavan. And Yaakov answered and said unto Lavan: "What is my trespass? What is my missing-of-the-mark, that you have pursued hotly after me?
verses.ar: فاحتَدَّ يَعقوبُ وخاصَمَ لابانَ. وأجابَ يَعقوبُ وقالَ لِلابانَ: «ما مَعصِيَتي؟ ما خَطيئَتي أنَّكَ لاحَقتَني بِحَرارَةٍ؟

verses.he: כִּֽי מִשַּׁ֣שְׁתָּ אֶת כָּל כֵּלַ֗/י מַה מָּצָ֨אתָ֙ מִ/כֹּ֣ל כְּלֵי בֵיתֶ֔/ךָ שִׂ֣ים כֹּ֔ה נֶ֥גֶד אַחַ֖/י וְ/אַחֶ֑י/ךָ וְ/יוֹכִ֖יחוּ בֵּ֥ין שְׁנֵֽי/נוּ
verses.en: For you have felt through all my goods — what have you found of all the goods of your house? Set it here, before my kinsmen and your kinsmen, and let them judge between the two of us.
verses.ar: إذ تَحَسَّستَ كُلَّ أمتِعَتي — ماذا وَجَدتَ مِن كُلِّ أمتِعَةِ بَيتِكَ؟ ضَعهُ هُنا أمامَ أقارِبي وأقارِبِكَ، ولِيَحكُموا بَينَنا نَحنُ الاثنَينِ.

verses.he: זֶה֩ עֶשְׂרִ֨ים שָׁנָ֤ה אָנֹכִי֙ עִמָּ֔/ךְ רְחֵלֶ֥י/ךָ וְ/עִזֶּ֖י/ךָ לֹ֣א שִׁכֵּ֑לוּ וְ/אֵילֵ֥י צֹאנְ/ךָ֖ לֹ֥א אָכָֽלְתִּי
verses.en: These twenty years I have been with you: your ewes and your she-goats have not miscarried, and the rams of your flock I have not eaten.
verses.ar: هذِهِ عِشرونَ سَنَةً أنا عِندَكَ: نِعاجُكَ ومَعزُكَ لَم تُسقِطْ، وكِباشَ غَنَمِكَ لَم آكُلْ.

verses.he: טְרֵפָה֙ לֹא הֵבֵ֣אתִי אֵלֶ֔י/ךָ אָנֹכִ֣י אֲחַטֶּ֔/נָּה מִ/יָּדִ֖/י תְּבַקְשֶׁ֑/נָּה גְּנֻֽבְתִ֣י י֔וֹם וּ/גְנֻֽבְתִ֖י לָֽיְלָה
verses.en: The torn I did not bring unto you — I bore the loss of it; from my hand you would seek it, stolen by day and stolen by night.
verses.ar: الفَريسَةَ لَم أُحضِرها إلَيكَ — أنا تَحَمَّلتُ خَسارَتَها؛ مِن يَدي كُنتَ تَطلُبُها، مَسروقَةً بِالنَّهارِ ومَسروقَةً بِاللَّيلِ.

verses.he: הָיִ֧יתִי בַ/יּ֛וֹם אֲכָלַ֥/נִי חֹ֖רֶב וְ/קֶ֣רַח בַּ/לָּ֑יְלָה וַ/תִּדַּ֥ד שְׁנָתִ֖/י מֵֽ/עֵינָֽ/י
verses.en: I was thus: by day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night, and my sleep fled from my eyes.
verses.ar: كُنتُ هكذا: بِالنَّهارِ أكَلَني الحَرُّ، والصَّقيعُ بِاللَّيلِ، ونَومي هَرَبَ مِن عَينَيَّ.

verses.he: זֶה לִּ֞/י עֶשְׂרִ֣ים שָׁנָה֮ בְּ/בֵיתֶ/ךָ֒ עֲבַדְתִּ֜י/ךָ אַרְבַּֽע עֶשְׂרֵ֤ה שָׁנָה֙ בִּ/שְׁתֵּ֣י בְנֹתֶ֔י/ךָ וְ/שֵׁ֥שׁ שָׁנִ֖ים בְּ/צֹאנֶ֑/ךָ וַ/תַּחֲלֵ֥ף אֶת מַשְׂכֻּרְתִּ֖/י עֲשֶׂ֥רֶת מֹנִֽים
verses.en: This is twenty years for me in your house: I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you changed my wages ten times.
verses.ar: هذِهِ عِشرونَ سَنَةً لي في بَيتِكَ: خَدَمتُكَ أربَعَ عَشرَةَ سَنَةً عَن ابنَتَيكَ، وسِتَّ سِنينَ عَن غَنَمِكَ، وبَدَّلتَ أُجرَتي عَشرَ مَرّاتٍ.

verses.he: לוּלֵ֡י אֱלֹהֵ֣י אָבִ/י֩ אֱלֹהֵ֨י אַבְרָהָ֜ם וּ/פַ֤חַד יִצְחָק֙ הָ֣יָה לִ֔/י כִּ֥י עַתָּ֖ה רֵיקָ֣ם שִׁלַּחְתָּ֑/נִי אֶת עָנְיִ֞/י וְ/אֶת יְגִ֧יעַ כַּפַּ֛/י רָאָ֥ה אֱלֹהִ֖ים וַ/יּ֥וֹכַח אָֽמֶשׁ
verses.en: Were it not that the God of my father — the God of Avraham and the Dread of Yitschak — had been with me, surely now you would have sent me away empty. My affliction and the toil of my palms God has seen, and He rebuked you last night."
verses.ar: لَولا أنَّ إلهَ أبي — إلهَ إبراهيمَ ورَهبَةَ يِصحاقَ — كانَ مَعي، لَأرسَلتَني الآنَ فارِغًا. بُؤسي وكَدَّ كَفَّيَّ رَآهُ اللهُ، فَزَجَرَكَ البارِحَةَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֨עַן לָבָ֜ן וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר אֶֽל יַעֲקֹ֗ב הַ/בָּנ֨וֹת בְּנֹתַ֜/י וְ/הַ/בָּנִ֤ים בָּנַ/י֙ וְ/הַ/צֹּ֣אן צֹאנִ֔/י וְ/כֹ֛ל אֲשֶׁר אַתָּ֥ה רֹאֶ֖ה לִ/י ה֑וּא וְ/לִ/בְנֹתַ֞/י מָֽה אֶֽעֱשֶׂ֤ה לָ/אֵ֨לֶּה֙ הַ/יּ֔וֹם א֥וֹ לִ/בְנֵי/הֶ֖ן אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָלָֽדוּ
verses.en: And Lavan answered and said unto Yaakov: "The daughters are my daughters, and the sons are my sons, and the flock is my flock, and all that you see — it is mine. And to my daughters — what can I do to these today, or to their sons whom they have borne?
verses.ar: فأجابَ لابانُ وقالَ لِيَعقوبَ: «البَناتُ بَناتي، والبَنونَ بَنِيَّ، والغَنَمُ غَنَمي، وكُلُّ ما تَرى — لي هُوَ. ولِبَناتي — ماذا أصنَعُ لِهؤلاءِ اليَومَ، أو لِبَنيهِنَّ الَّذينَ وَلَدنَ؟

verses.he: וְ/עַתָּ֗ה לְכָ֛/ה נִכְרְתָ֥ה בְרִ֖ית אֲנִ֣י וָ/אָ֑תָּה וְ/הָיָ֥ה לְ/עֵ֖ד בֵּינִ֥/י וּ/בֵינֶֽ/ךָ
verses.en: And now, come, let us cut a cutting-bond, I and you; and let it be a witness between me and you."
verses.ar: والآنَ تَعالَ نَقطَعْ عَهدًا، أنا وأنتَ؛ ولِيَكُن شاهِدًا بَيني وبَينَكَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּקַּ֥ח יַעֲקֹ֖ב אָ֑בֶן וַ/יְרִימֶ֖/הָ מַצֵּבָֽה
verses.en: And Yaakov took a stone, and raised it as a standing-pillar.
verses.ar: فأخَذَ يَعقوبُ حَجَرًا ورَفَعَهُ نُصُبًا.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֤ב לְ/אֶחָי/ו֙ לִקְט֣וּ אֲבָנִ֔ים וַ/יִּקְח֥וּ אֲבָנִ֖ים וַ/יַּֽעֲשׂוּ גָ֑ל וַ/יֹּ֥אכְלוּ שָׁ֖ם עַל הַ/גָּֽל
verses.en: And Yaakov said unto his kinsmen: "Gather stones." And they took stones and made a mound; and they ate there upon the mound.
verses.ar: وقالَ يَعقوبُ لِأقارِبِهِ: «اجمَعوا حِجارَةً.» فأخَذوا حِجارَةً وصَنَعوا رُجمَةً؛ وأكَلوا هُناكَ على الرُّجمَةِ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרָא ל֣/וֹ לָבָ֔ן יְגַ֖ר שָׂהֲדוּתָ֑א וְ/יַֽעֲקֹ֔ב קָ֥רָא ל֖/וֹ גַּלְעֵֽד
verses.en: And Lavan called it Yegar-Sahaduta — and Yaakov called it Galed.
verses.ar: وسَمّاها لابانُ يَجَرْ سَهَدوتا — وسَمّاها يَعقوبُ جَلعيدَ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר לָבָ֔ן הַ/גַּ֨ל הַ/זֶּ֥ה עֵ֛ד בֵּינִ֥/י וּ/בֵינְ/ךָ֖ הַ/יּ֑וֹם עַל כֵּ֥ן קָרָֽא שְׁמ֖/וֹ גַּלְעֵֽד
verses.en: And Lavan said: "This mound is a witness between me and you today." Therefore its name was called Galed — "Mound of Witness" —
verses.ar: وقالَ لابانُ: «هذِهِ الرُّجمَةُ شاهِدَةٌ بَيني وبَينَكَ اليَومَ.» لِذلِكَ سُمِّيَت جَلعيدَ — «رُجمَةُ الشَّهادَةِ» —

verses.he: וְ/הַ/מִּצְפָּה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר אָמַ֔ר יִ֥צֶף יְהוָ֖ה בֵּינִ֣/י וּ/בֵינֶ֑/ךָ כִּ֥י נִסָּתֵ֖ר אִ֥ישׁ מֵ/רֵעֵֽ/הוּ
verses.en: and the Mitspah — "Watchtower" — for he said: "May YHWH watch between me and you, when we are hidden each from his fellow.
verses.ar: والمِصفاةُ — «بُرجُ المُراقَبَةِ» — إذ قالَ: «لِيُراقِبِ الرَّبُّ بَيني وبَينَكَ حينَ نَغيبُ كُلٌّ عَن صاحِبِهِ.

verses.he: אִם תְּעַנֶּ֣ה אֶת בְּנֹתַ֗/י וְ/אִם תִּקַּ֤ח נָשִׁים֙ עַל בְּנֹתַ֔/י אֵ֥ין אִ֖ישׁ עִמָּ֑/נוּ רְאֵ֕ה אֱלֹהִ֥ים עֵ֖ד בֵּינִ֥/י וּ/בֵינֶֽ/ךָ
verses.en: If you afflict my daughters, and if you take wives besides my daughters — no man is with us; see, God is witness between me and you."
verses.ar: إن أذلَلتَ بَناتي، وإن أخَذتَ نِساءً سِوى بَناتي — لا إنسانَ مَعَنا؛ انظُرْ، اللهُ شاهِدٌ بَيني وبَينَكَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר לָבָ֖ן לְ/יַעֲקֹ֑ב הִנֵּ֣ה הַ/גַּ֣ל הַ/זֶּ֗ה וְ/הִנֵּה֙ הַ/מַצֵּבָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָרִ֖יתִי בֵּינִ֥/י וּ/בֵינֶֽ/ךָ
verses.en: And Lavan said unto Yaakov: "Behold this mound, and behold this standing-pillar, which I have cast between me and you.
verses.ar: وقالَ لابانُ لِيَعقوبَ: «هذِهِ الرُّجمَةُ، وهذا النُّصُبُ الَّذي ألقَيتُ بَيني وبَينَكَ.

verses.he: עֵ֚ד הַ/גַּ֣ל הַ/זֶּ֔ה וְ/עֵדָ֖ה הַ/מַּצֵּבָ֑ה אִם אָ֗נִי לֹֽא אֶֽעֱבֹ֤ר אֵלֶ֨י/ךָ֙ אֶת הַ/גַּ֣ל הַ/זֶּ֔ה וְ/אִם אַ֠תָּה לֹא תַעֲבֹ֨ר אֵלַ֜/י אֶת הַ/גַּ֥ל הַ/זֶּ֛ה וְ/אֶת הַ/מַּצֵּבָ֥ה הַ/זֹּ֖את לְ/רָעָֽה
verses.en: Witness is this mound, and witness is the standing-pillar: I shall not pass over this mound unto you, and you shall not pass over this mound and this standing-pillar unto me, for evil.
verses.ar: شاهِدَةٌ هذِهِ الرُّجمَةُ، وشاهِدٌ هذا النُّصُبُ: لا أنا أعبُرُ هذِهِ الرُّجمَةَ إلَيكَ، ولا أنتَ تَعبُرُ هذِهِ الرُّجمَةَ وهذا النُّصُبَ إلَيَّ، لِلشَّرِّ.

verses.he: אֱלֹהֵ֨י אַבְרָהָ֜ם וֵֽ/אלֹהֵ֤י נָחוֹר֙ יִשְׁפְּט֣וּ בֵינֵ֔י/נוּ אֱלֹהֵ֖י אֲבִי/הֶ֑ם וַ/יִּשָּׁבַ֣ע יַעֲקֹ֔ב בְּ/פַ֖חַד אָבִ֥י/ו יִצְחָֽק
verses.en: The God of Avraham and the God of Nachor — the God of their father — shall judge between us." And Yaakov swore by the Dread of his father Yitschak.
verses.ar: إلهُ إبراهيمَ وإلهُ ناحورَ — إلهُ أبيهِما — يَحكُمُ بَينَنا.» فَحَلَفَ يَعقوبُ بِرَهبَةِ أبيهِ يِصحاقَ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּזְבַּ֨ח יַעֲקֹ֥ב זֶ֨בַח֙ בָּ/הָ֔ר וַ/יִּקְרָ֥א לְ/אֶחָ֖י/ו לֶ/אֱכָל לָ֑חֶם וַ/יֹּ֣אכְלוּ לֶ֔חֶם וַ/יָּלִ֖ינוּ בָּ/הָֽר
verses.en: And Yaakov slaughtered a slaughter upon the hill-country, and called his kinsmen to eat bread; and they ate bread, and lodged on the hill-country.
verses.ar: وذَبَحَ يَعقوبُ ذَبيحَةً في الجَبَلِ، ودَعا أقارِبَهُ لِيَأكُلوا خُبزًا؛ فأكَلوا خُبزًا وباتوا في الجَبَلِ.

verses.en: And Lavan rose early in the morning, and kissed his sons and his daughters, and blessed them. And Lavan went, and returned unto his place.
verses.ar: وبَكَّرَ لابانُ في الصَّباحِ وقَبَّلَ بَنيهِ وبَناتِهِ وبارَكَهُم. ومَضى لابانُ ورَجَعَ إلى مَكانِهِ.

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Bereshit Chapter 31 — The Flight from Lavan

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.1: כָּבוֹד (kavod) — "weight"
Root כ-ב-ד (k-b-d): weight, heaviness, honor, glory. "From that which belonged to our father he has made all this weight (כבוד)." The sons of Lavan use kavod in its material sense — Yaakov has accumulated "weight," i.e., substance, wealth. The same word that means "glory" when applied to God means "heavy possessions" when applied to a man. This is root-recovery at its most revealing: glory IS weight; honor IS substance. Cognate with Arabic كبد (kabad, liver) — the heavy organ, the seat of emotion in Arabic; and the Qur'anic concept of God's glory as His substantial, weighty presence.

### v.2: פָּנִים (panim) — "face"
Root פ-נ-ה (p-n-h): face, presence, turning. "Yaakov saw the face of Lavan, and behold — it was not toward him as yesterday and the day before." The face reveals the inner disposition. Panim is always plural in Hebrew — the face has many aspects, many turnings. Lavan's face has turned. Throughout this chapter and the next, "face" is a key motif, culminating in Yaakov seeing "the face of God" at Peniel (32:31).

### v.13: הָאֵל בֵּית אֵל (ha-El Beit-El) — "the God of Beit-El"
God identifies Himself by location — the God who appeared at Beit-El, where Yaakov anointed the pillar. The divine self-identification is relational and historical: not abstract theology but "I am the God of your experience, the one you encountered at that place."

### v.19: תְּרָפִים (terafim) — "divination-figures"
The terafim are household idols or divination objects — their exact form is debated. Rachel steals her father's terafim. Why? Possibly to prevent Lavan from divining their escape route; possibly as a claim on inheritance (possession of household gods may have conferred inheritance rights in Mesopotamian law); possibly as a lingering attachment to paganism. The text does not explain her motive.

### v.20: וַיִּגְנֹב יַעֲקֹב אֶת־לֵב לָבָן (vayyignov Yaʿaqov et-lev Lavan) — "Yaakov stole the heart of Lavan"
Root ג-נ-ב (g-n-v): to steal. "Stole the heart" is an idiom meaning "to deceive" — he stole away without Lavan's knowledge. The phrase is vivid: Yaakov, who already stole a blessing and a birthright, now steals a heart (in the sense of outwitting). The theme of theft and deception runs through his life until he is renamed.

### v.36: חַטָּאתִי (ḥatta'ti) — "my missing-of-the-mark"
Root ח-ט-א (ḥ-ṭ-'): to miss the mark, to err. Yaakov demands: "What is my trespass (פשע, peshaʿ)? What is my missing-of-the-mark (חטאת)?" Two different words for wrongdoing: פשע (peshaʿ, deliberate transgression/rebellion) and חטאת (ḥatta't, missing the mark/going astray). Yaakov denies both: he has neither rebelled nor erred.

### v.42: פַּחַד יִצְחָק (Paḥad Yitsḥaq) — "the Dread of Yitschak"
Root פ-ח-ד (p-ḥ-d): to dread, to fear, to tremble. This is a unique divine title — God as "the Dread (or the Terror) of Yitschak." Some translate it as "the Kinsman of Yitschak" (reading פחד as related to an Aramaic root for kinsman), but the traditional reading "Dread" is powerful: God as the one before whom Yitschak trembles. This may connect to the trembling of chapter 27:33 — the great trembling that seized Yitschak when he realized the blessing had gone to Yaakov. The God of Yitschak is the God of awe and trembling.

### v.44: בְּרִית (berit) — "cutting-bond"
Root ב-ר-ת (b-r-t): covenant. Lavan proposes: "Let us cut a cutting-bond." The covenant between Yaakov and Lavan is a boundary treaty — a non-aggression pact sealed with witnesses (the mound, the pillar, God). It defines the border between their territories and spheres of influence.

### v.47: יְגַר שָׂהֲדוּתָא / גַּלְעֵד (Yegar Sahaduta / Gal'ed)
Lavan names the mound in Aramaic (יגר שהדותא, Yegar Sahaduta = "Mound of Witness"); Yaakov names it in Hebrew (גלעד, Gal'ed = "Mound of Witness"). The two languages say the same thing — the meaning is shared even as the tongues differ. This is one of only two Aramaic phrases in the Torah (the other is in Jeremiah), marking Lavan as an Aramean and the border between the Aramaic and Hebrew worlds.

### v.49: הַמִּצְפָּה (ha-Mitspah) — "the Watchtower"
Root צ-פ-ה (ts-p-h): to watch, to look out, to keep watch. "May the LORD watch (יצף, yitseph) between me and you when we are hidden each from his fellow." Often quoted today as a blessing of fellowship, but in context it is a warning: may God watch over us because we do not trust each other. The watchtower is a point of surveillance, not of friendship.

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| כבד (kavod) | كبد (kabad) | weight/heaviness/glory | The substantial presence of God — glory as weight |
| גנב (ganav) | — | to steal (cf. سرق, saraqa) | 12:81 — "Your son has stolen" (in the Yusuf narrative) |
| חטא (ḥata') | خطأ (khaṭa') | to miss the mark | Throughout — missing-the-mark vs. deliberate sin |
| פחד (paḥad) | — | dread/fear (cf. خوف, khawf) | 106:4 — "secured them from fear" |
| עד (ʿed) | شهيد (shahīd) | witness | 3:98 — "God is witness over what you do" |
| שלם (shalom) | سلام (salām) | peace/wholeness | v.55 — Lavan blesses and departs; the covenant secures שלום |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

The flight from Lavan represents the departure of the soul from attachment to the material world. Yaakov's twenty years of service correspond to a period of spiritual testing and maturation. The terafim that Rachel steals represent the lingering hold of old beliefs and practices — even as one moves toward spiritual truth, remnants of the past are carried along. The covenant at Gal'ed establishes boundaries, which is a necessary spiritual principle: healthy boundaries between the material and the spiritual, between old attachments and new commitments. 'Abdu'l-Bahá teaches that the spiritual journey requires leaving behind "the land of the father" (habitual ways of thinking) even while honoring what was learned there.

The bilingual naming of the mound — Aramaic and Hebrew saying the same thing — illustrates a principle dear to Bahá'í thought: different languages expressing the same essential truth. "The earth is but one country, and mankind its citizens."

## Integrative Links

- **v.3 "return to the land of your fathers" <> Qur'an 2:35-36**: The command to return — Adam told to "descend" from the Garden, Yaakov told to return to the Land; departure and return as the rhythm of spiritual life
- **v.13 "the God of Beit-El" <> Qur'an 2:125**: God identified by sacred place — the God of Beit-El, the Lord of the Ka'ba
- **v.42 "the Dread of Yitschak" <> Qur'an 59:21**: "If We had sent down this Qur'an upon a mountain, you would have seen it humbled, cracking from the awe (خشية) of God" — the divine as the source of sacred dread
- **v.49 "May the LORD watch between me and you" <> Qur'an 3:98**: "God is witness over what you do" — God as the watchful guarantor of covenants between parties who cannot trust each other
- **v.53 "the God of Avraham and the God of Nachor" <> Qur'an 29:46**: "Our God and your God is one" — the shared divine source behind different family traditions

chapters.title: Bereshit — פרק 32
chapters.subtitle: Genesis — Chapter 32

verses.he: וַ/יַּשְׁכֵּ֨ם לָבָ֜ן בַּ/בֹּ֗קֶר וַ/יְנַשֵּׁ֧ק לְ/בָנָ֛י/ו וְ/לִ/בְנוֹתָ֖י/ו וַ/יְבָ֣רֶךְ אֶתְ/הֶ֑ם וַ/יֵּ֛לֶךְ וַ/יָּ֥שָׁב לָבָ֖ן לִ/מְקֹמֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Lavan rose early in the morning, and kissed his sons and his daughters, and blessed them; and Lavan went and returned to his place.
verses.ar: وبَكَّرَ لابانُ في الصَّباحِ وقَبَّلَ بَنيهِ وبَناتِهِ وبارَكَهُم؛ ومَضى لابانُ ورَجَعَ إلى مَكانِهِ.

verses.he: וְ/יַעֲקֹ֖ב הָלַ֣ךְ לְ/דַרְכּ֑/וֹ וַ/יִּפְגְּעוּ ב֖/וֹ מַלְאֲכֵ֥י אֱלֹהִֽים
verses.en: And Yaakov went on his way, and messengers of God encountered him.
verses.ar: ومَضى يَعقوبُ في طَريقِهِ، فَلَقِيَهُ مَلائِكَةُ اللهِ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר יַעֲקֹב֙ כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֣ר רָאָ֔/ם מַחֲנֵ֥ה אֱלֹהִ֖ים זֶ֑ה וַ/יִּקְרָ֛א שֵֽׁם הַ/מָּק֥וֹם הַ/ה֖וּא מַֽחֲנָֽיִם
verses.en: And Yaakov said when he saw them: "This is the camp of God!" And he called the name of that place Machanayim.
verses.ar: وقالَ يَعقوبُ حينَ رَآهُم: «هذا مُعَسكَرُ اللهِ!» فَسَمّى ذلِكَ المَوضِعَ مَحَنايِمَ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּשְׁלַ֨ח יַעֲקֹ֤ב מַלְאָכִים֙ לְ/פָנָ֔י/ו אֶל עֵשָׂ֖ו אָחִ֑י/ו אַ֥רְצָ/ה שֵׂעִ֖יר שְׂדֵ֥ה אֱדֽוֹם
verses.en: And Yaakov sent messengers before him unto Esav his brother, to the land of Se'ir, the field of Edom.
verses.ar: وأرسَلَ يَعقوبُ رُسُلًا أمامَهُ إلى عيسو أخيهِ، إلى أرضِ سِعيرَ، حَقلِ أَدومَ.

verses.he: וַ/יְצַ֤ו אֹתָ/ם֙ לֵ/אמֹ֔ר כֹּ֣ה תֹאמְר֔וּ/ן לַֽ/אדֹנִ֖/י לְ/עֵשָׂ֑ו כֹּ֤ה אָמַר֙ עַבְדְּ/ךָ֣ יַעֲקֹ֔ב עִם לָבָ֣ן גַּ֔רְתִּי וָ/אֵחַ֖ר עַד עָֽתָּה
verses.en: And he commanded them, saying: "Thus shall you say to my lord, to Esav: Thus says your servant Yaakov: I have sojourned with Lavan, and I have lingered until now.
verses.ar: وأمَرَهُم قائِلًا: «هكذا تَقولونَ لِسَيِّدي عيسو: هكذا يَقولُ عَبدُكَ يَعقوبُ: عِندَ لابانَ تَغَرَّبتُ، وتَأخَّرتُ إلى الآنَ.

verses.he: וַֽ/יְהִי לִ/י֙ שׁ֣וֹר וַ/חֲמ֔וֹר צֹ֖אן וְ/עֶ֣בֶד וְ/שִׁפְחָ֑ה וָֽ/אֶשְׁלְחָ/ה֙ לְ/הַגִּ֣יד לַֽ/אדֹנִ֔/י לִ/מְצֹא חֵ֖ן בְּ/עֵינֶֽי/ךָ
verses.en: And I have come to have ox and donkey, flock and servant and maidservant; and I have sent to tell my lord, to find favor in your eyes."
verses.ar: وقَد صارَ لي ثَورٌ وحِمارٌ، وغَنَمٌ وعَبدٌ وأمَةٌ؛ وأرسَلتُ لِأُخبِرَ سَيِّدي، لِأجِدَ نِعمَةً في عَينَيكَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יָּשֻׁ֨בוּ֙ הַ/מַּלְאָכִ֔ים אֶֽל יַעֲקֹ֖ב לֵ/אמֹ֑ר בָּ֤אנוּ אֶל אָחִ֨י/ךָ֙ אֶל עֵשָׂ֔ו וְ/גַם֙ הֹלֵ֣ךְ לִ/קְרָֽאתְ/ךָ֔ וְ/אַרְבַּע מֵא֥וֹת אִ֖ישׁ עִמּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And the messengers returned to Yaakov, saying: "We came to your brother, to Esav, and he is also coming to meet you, and four hundred men with him."
verses.ar: فَرَجَعَ الرُّسُلُ إلى يَعقوبَ قائِلينَ: «جِئنا إلى أخيكَ عيسو، وهُوَ أيضًا قادِمٌ لِلِقائِكَ، ومَعَهُ أربَعُمِئَةِ رَجُلٍ.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּירָ֧א יַעֲקֹ֛ב מְאֹ֖ד וַ/יֵּ֣צֶר ל֑/וֹ וַ/יַּ֜חַץ אֶת הָ/עָ֣ם אֲשֶׁר אִתּ֗/וֹ וְ/אֶת הַ/צֹּ֧אן וְ/אֶת הַ/בָּקָ֛ר וְ/הַ/גְּמַלִּ֖ים לִ/שְׁנֵ֥י מַחֲנֽוֹת
verses.en: And Yaakov was greatly afraid, and it was distressing to him; and he divided the people that were with him, and the flock and the cattle and the camels, into two camps.
verses.ar: فَخافَ يَعقوبُ جِدًّا وضاقَ بِهِ الأمرُ؛ فَقَسَمَ القَومَ الَّذينَ مَعَهُ، والغَنَمَ والبَقَرَ والجِمالَ، إلى مُعَسكَرَينِ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר אִם יָב֥וֹא עֵשָׂ֛ו אֶל הַ/מַּחֲנֶ֥ה הָ/אַחַ֖ת וְ/הִכָּ֑/הוּ וְ/הָיָ֛ה הַ/מַּחֲנֶ֥ה הַ/נִּשְׁאָ֖ר לִ/פְלֵיטָֽה
verses.en: And he said: "If Esav comes to the one camp and strikes it, then the remaining camp shall be for escape."
verses.ar: وقالَ: «إن جاءَ عيسو إلى المُعَسكَرِ الواحِدِ وضَرَبَهُ، فالمُعَسكَرُ الباقي يَكونُ لِلنَّجاةِ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאמֶר֮ יַעֲקֹב֒ אֱלֹהֵי֙ אָבִ֣/י אַבְרָהָ֔ם וֵ/אלֹהֵ֖י אָבִ֣/י יִצְחָ֑ק יְהוָ֞ה הָ/אֹמֵ֣ר אֵלַ֗/י שׁ֧וּב לְ/אַרְצְ/ךָ֛ וּ/לְ/מוֹלַדְתְּ/ךָ֖ וְ/אֵיטִ֥יבָה עִמָּֽ/ךְ
verses.en: And Yaakov said: "God of my father Avraham, and God of my father Yitschak, O YHWH, who said to me: 'Return to your land and to your birthplace, and I will do good with you' —
verses.ar: وقالَ يَعقوبُ: «يا إلهَ أبي إبراهيمَ وإلهَ أبي يِصحاقَ، يا رَبُّ الَّذي قُلتَ لي: 'ارجِعْ إلى أرضِكَ وإلى مَولِدِكَ وأُحسِنُ إلَيكَ' —

verses.he: קָטֹ֜נְתִּי מִ/כֹּ֤ל הַ/חֲסָדִים֙ וּ/מִ/כָּל הָ֣/אֱמֶ֔ת אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָשִׂ֖יתָ אֶת עַבְדֶּ֑/ךָ כִּ֣י בְ/מַקְלִ֗/י עָבַ֨רְתִּי֙ אֶת הַ/יַּרְדֵּ֣ן הַ/זֶּ֔ה וְ/עַתָּ֥ה הָיִ֖יתִי לִ/שְׁנֵ֥י מַחֲנֽוֹת
verses.en: I am too small for all the loyal-kindnesses and for all the faithfulness which You have done for Your servant; for with my staff I crossed over this Yarden, and now I have become two camps.
verses.ar: صَغُرتُ عَن كُلِّ أَلطافِ الوَفاءِ وعَن كُلِّ الأمانَةِ الَّتي صَنَعتَها لِعَبدِكَ؛ فإنّي بِعَصايَ عَبَرتُ هذا الأُردُنَّ، والآنَ صِرتُ مُعَسكَرَينِ.

verses.he: הַצִּילֵ֥/נִי נָ֛א מִ/יַּ֥ד אָחִ֖/י מִ/יַּ֣ד עֵשָׂ֑ו כִּֽי יָרֵ֤א אָנֹכִי֙ אֹת֔/וֹ פֶּן יָב֣וֹא וְ/הִכַּ֔/נִי אֵ֖ם עַל בָּנִֽים
verses.en: Deliver me, I pray, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esav; for I fear him, lest he come and strike me, mother upon children.
verses.ar: أنقِذني أرجوكَ مِن يَدِ أخي، مِن يَدِ عيسو؛ فإنّي أخافُهُ لَعَلَّهُ يَأتي ويَضرِبُني، الأُمَّ مَعَ الأولادِ.

verses.he: וְ/אַתָּ֣ה אָמַ֔רְתָּ הֵיטֵ֥ב אֵיטִ֖יב עִמָּ֑/ךְ וְ/שַׂמְתִּ֤י אֶֽת זַרְעֲ/ךָ֙ כְּ/ח֣וֹל הַ/יָּ֔ם אֲשֶׁ֥ר לֹא יִסָּפֵ֖ר מֵ/רֹֽב
verses.en: And You, You said: 'I will do good, yes, do good with you, and I will set your seed like the sand of the sea, which cannot be counted for abundance.'"
verses.ar: وأنتَ قُلتَ: 'أُحسِنُ إحسانًا إلَيكَ، وأجعَلُ نَسلَكَ كَرَملِ البَحرِ الَّذي لا يُحصى مِنَ الكَثرَةِ.'»

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֥לֶן שָׁ֖ם בַּ/לַּ֣יְלָה הַ/ה֑וּא וַ/יִּקַּ֞ח מִן הַ/בָּ֧א בְ/יָד֛/וֹ מִנְחָ֖ה לְ/עֵשָׂ֥ו אָחִֽי/ו
verses.en: And he lodged there that night; and he took from what came into his hand a gift for Esav his brother:
verses.ar: فَباتَ هُناكَ تِلكَ اللَّيلَةَ؛ وأخَذَ مِمّا جاءَ في يَدِهِ هَدِيَّةً لِعيسو أخيهِ:

verses.he: עִזִּ֣ים מָאתַ֔יִם וּ/תְיָשִׁ֖ים עֶשְׂרִ֑ים רְחֵלִ֥ים מָאתַ֖יִם וְ/אֵילִ֥ים עֶשְׂרִֽים
verses.en: two hundred she-goats and twenty he-goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams,
verses.ar: مِئَتَي عَنزٍ وعِشرينَ تَيسًا، مِئَتَي نَعجَةٍ وعِشرينَ كَبشًا،

verses.he: גְּמַלִּ֧ים מֵינִיק֛וֹת וּ/בְנֵי/הֶ֖ם שְׁלֹשִׁ֑ים פָּר֤וֹת אַרְבָּעִים֙ וּ/פָרִ֣ים עֲשָׂרָ֔ה אֲתֹנֹ֣ת עֶשְׂרִ֔ים וַ/עְיָרִ֖ם עֲשָׂרָֽה
verses.en: thirty nursing camels and their young, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty she-donkeys and ten foals.
verses.ar: ثَلاثينَ ناقَةً مُرضِعَةً وأولادَها، أربَعينَ بَقَرَةً وعَشرَةَ ثيرانٍ، عِشرينَ أتانًا وعَشرَةَ أمهارٍ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּתֵּן֙ בְּ/יַד עֲבָדָ֔י/ו עֵ֥דֶר עֵ֖דֶר לְ/בַדּ֑/וֹ וַ֤/יֹּאמֶר אֶל עֲבָדָי/ו֙ עִבְר֣וּ לְ/פָנַ֔/י וְ/רֶ֣וַח תָּשִׂ֔ימוּ בֵּ֥ין עֵ֖דֶר וּ/בֵ֥ין עֵֽדֶר
verses.en: And he gave them into the hand of his servants, each flock by itself; and he said to his servants: "Cross over before me, and put a space between flock and flock."
verses.ar: وأعطاها في يَدِ عَبيدِهِ، كُلَّ قَطيعٍ على حِدَةٍ؛ وقالَ لِعَبيدِهِ: «اعبُروا أمامي، واجعَلوا فُرجَةً بَينَ قَطيعٍ وقَطيعٍ.»

verses.he: וַ/יְצַ֥ו אֶת הָ/רִאשׁ֖וֹן לֵ/אמֹ֑ר כִּ֣י יִֽפְגָּשְׁ/ךָ֞ עֵשָׂ֣ו אָחִ֗/י וִ/שְׁאֵֽלְ/ךָ֙ לֵ/אמֹ֔ר לְ/מִי אַ֨תָּה֙ וְ/אָ֣נָה תֵלֵ֔ךְ וּ/לְ/מִ֖י אֵ֥לֶּה לְ/פָנֶֽי/ךָ
verses.en: And he commanded the first, saying: "When Esav my brother meets you, and asks you, saying: 'Whose are you? And where are you going? And whose are these before you?'
verses.ar: وأمَرَ الأوَّلَ قائِلًا: «إذا لَقِيَكَ عيسو أخي وسَألَكَ قائِلًا: 'لِمَن أنتَ؟ وإلى أينَ تَذهَبُ؟ ولِمَن هذِهِ الَّتي أمامَكَ؟'

verses.he: וְ/אָֽמַרְתָּ֙ לְ/עַבְדְּ/ךָ֣ לְ/יַעֲקֹ֔ב מִנְחָ֥ה הִוא֙ שְׁלוּחָ֔ה לַֽ/אדֹנִ֖/י לְ/עֵשָׂ֑ו וְ/הִנֵּ֥ה גַם ה֖וּא אַחֲרֵֽי/נוּ
verses.en: Then you shall say: 'They belong to your servant Yaakov; it is a gift sent to my lord, to Esav; and behold, he also is behind us.'"
verses.ar: فَقُلْ: 'لِعَبدِكَ يَعقوبَ؛ هِيَ هَدِيَّةٌ مُرسَلَةٌ إلى سَيِّدي عيسو؛ وها هُوَ أيضًا وَراءَنا.'»

verses.he: וַ/יְצַ֞ו גַּ֣ם אֶת הַ/שֵּׁנִ֗י גַּ֚ם אֶת הַ/שְּׁלִישִׁ֔י גַּ֚ם אֶת כָּל הַ/הֹ֣לְכִ֔ים אַחֲרֵ֥י הָ/עֲדָרִ֖ים לֵ/אמֹ֑ר כַּ/דָּבָ֤ר הַ/זֶּה֙ תְּדַבְּר֣וּ/ן אֶל עֵשָׂ֔ו בְּ/מֹצַאֲ/כֶ֖ם אֹתֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And he commanded also the second, also the third, also all who followed after the flocks, saying: "According to this word shall you speak to Esav when you find him.
verses.ar: وأمَرَ أيضًا الثّانيَ والثّالِثَ وكُلَّ مَن يَتبَعُ القُطعانَ قائِلًا: «بِهذا الكَلامِ تُكَلِّمونَ عيسو حينَ تَجِدونَهُ.

verses.he: וַ/אֲמַרְתֶּ֕ם גַּ֗ם הִנֵּ֛ה עַבְדְּ/ךָ֥ יַעֲקֹ֖ב אַחֲרֵ֑י/נוּ כִּֽי אָמַ֞ר אֲכַפְּרָ֣ה פָנָ֗י/ו בַּ/מִּנְחָה֙ הַ/הֹלֶ֣כֶת לְ/פָנָ֔/י וְ/אַחֲרֵי כֵן֙ אֶרְאֶ֣ה פָנָ֔י/ו אוּלַ֖י יִשָּׂ֥א פָנָֽ/י
verses.en: And you shall say: 'Moreover, behold, your servant Yaakov is behind us.'" For he said: "I will cover over his face with the gift that goes before me, and afterward I will see his face; perhaps he will lift up my face."
verses.ar: وتَقولونَ: 'وأيضًا ها عَبدُكَ يَعقوبُ وَراءَنا.'» فإنَّهُ قالَ: «أُغَطّي وَجهَهُ بِالهَدِيَّةِ السّائِرَةِ أمامي، ثُمَّ أرى وَجهَهُ؛ لَعَلَّهُ يَرفَعُ وَجهي.»

verses.he: וַ/תַּעֲבֹ֥ר הַ/מִּנְחָ֖ה עַל פָּנָ֑י/ו וְ/ה֛וּא לָ֥ן בַּ/לַּֽיְלָה הַ/ה֖וּא בַּֽ/מַּחֲנֶֽה
verses.en: And the gift crossed over before him; and he lodged that night in the camp.
verses.ar: فَعَبَرَتِ الهَدِيَّةُ أمامَهُ، وهُوَ باتَ تِلكَ اللَّيلَةَ في المُعَسكَرِ.

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֣קָם בַּ/לַּ֣יְלָה ה֗וּא וַ/יִּקַּ֞ח אֶת שְׁתֵּ֤י נָשָׁי/ו֙ וְ/אֶת שְׁתֵּ֣י שִׁפְחֹתָ֔י/ו וְ/אֶת אַחַ֥ד עָשָׂ֖ר יְלָדָ֑י/ו וַֽ/יַּעֲבֹ֔ר אֵ֖ת מַעֲבַ֥ר יַבֹּֽק
verses.en: And he rose up that night, and took his two wives and his two maidservants and his eleven children, and crossed over the ford of Yabboq.
verses.ar: وقامَ في تِلكَ اللَّيلَةِ وأخَذَ زَوجَتَيهِ وجارِيَتَيهِ وأحَدَ عَشَرَ وَلَدَهُ، وعَبَرَ مَخاضَةَ يَبّوقَ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקָּחֵ֔/ם וַ/יַּֽעֲבִרֵ֖/ם אֶת הַ/נָּ֑חַל וַֽ/יַּעֲבֵ֖ר אֶת אֲשֶׁר ל/וֹ
verses.en: And he took them and sent them over the stream, and he sent over all that was his.
verses.ar: وأخَذَهُم وأعبَرَهُمُ الوادي، وأعبَرَ كُلَّ ما لَهُ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּוָּתֵ֥ר יַעֲקֹ֖ב לְ/בַדּ֑/וֹ וַ/יֵּאָבֵ֥ק אִישׁ֙ עִמּ֔/וֹ עַ֖ד עֲל֥וֹת הַ/שָּֽׁחַר
verses.en: And Yaakov was left alone; and a man wrestled with him until the rising of the dawn.
verses.ar: وبَقِيَ يَعقوبُ وَحدَهُ؛ وصارَعَهُ رَجُلٌ حَتّى طُلوعِ الفَجرِ.

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֗רְא כִּ֣י לֹ֤א יָכֹל֙ ל֔/וֹ וַ/יִּגַּ֖ע בְּ/כַף יְרֵכ֑/וֹ וַ/תֵּ֨קַע֙ כַּף יֶ֣רֶךְ יַעֲקֹ֔ב בְּ/הֵֽאָבְק֖/וֹ עִמּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And he saw that he could not prevail against him, and he touched the hollow of his thigh; and the hollow of Yaakov's thigh was wrenched as he wrestled with him.
verses.ar: ورَأى أنَّهُ لا يَقدِرُ عَلَيهِ، فَمَسَّ حُقَّ فَخِذِهِ؛ فانخَلَعَ حُقُّ فَخِذِ يَعقوبَ وهُوَ يُصارِعُهُ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר שַׁלְּחֵ֔/נִי כִּ֥י עָלָ֖ה הַ/שָּׁ֑חַר וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר֙ לֹ֣א אֲשַֽׁלֵּחֲ/ךָ֔ כִּ֖י אִם בֵּרַכְתָּֽ/נִי
verses.en: And he said: "Let me go, for the dawn has risen." And he said: "I will not let you go unless you have blessed me."
verses.ar: فقالَ: «أطلِقني فإنَّ الفَجرَ قَد طَلَعَ.» فقالَ: «لا أُطلِقُكَ إلّا إذا بارَكتَني.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר אֵלָ֖י/ו מַה שְּׁמֶ֑/ךָ וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר יַעֲקֹֽב
verses.en: And he said to him: "What is your name?" And he said: "Yaakov."
verses.ar: فقالَ لَهُ: «ما اسمُكَ؟» فقالَ: «يَعقوبُ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֗אמֶר לֹ֤א יַעֲקֹב֙ יֵאָמֵ֥ר עוֹד֙ שִׁמְ/ךָ֔ כִּ֖י אִם יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל כִּֽי שָׂרִ֧יתָ עִם אֱלֹהִ֛ים וְ/עִם אֲנָשִׁ֖ים וַ/תּוּכָֽל
verses.en: And he said: "Not Yaakov shall your name be said any more, but Yisra'el; for you have struggled with God and with men, and you have prevailed."
verses.ar: فقالَ: «لا يُقالُ اسمُكَ بَعدُ يَعقوبُ، بَل إسرائيلُ؛ لِأنَّكَ جاهَدتَ مَعَ اللهِ ومَعَ النّاسِ وغَلَبتَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּשְׁאַ֣ל יַעֲקֹ֗ב וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר֙ הַגִּֽידָ/ה נָּ֣א שְׁמֶ֔/ךָ וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר לָ֥/מָּה זֶּ֖ה תִּשְׁאַ֣ל לִ/שְׁמִ֑/י וַ/יְבָ֥רֶךְ אֹת֖/וֹ שָֽׁם
verses.en: And Yaakov asked, and said: "Tell me, I pray, your name." And he said: "Why is this that you ask for my name?" And he blessed him there.
verses.ar: وسَألَ يَعقوبُ وقالَ: «أخبِرني اسمَكَ أرجوكَ.» فقالَ: «لِماذا تَسألُ عَنِ اسمي؟» وبارَكَهُ هُناكَ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּקְרָ֧א יַעֲקֹ֛ב שֵׁ֥ם הַ/מָּק֖וֹם פְּנִיאֵ֑ל כִּֽי רָאִ֤יתִי אֱלֹהִים֙ פָּנִ֣ים אֶל פָּנִ֔ים וַ/תִּנָּצֵ֖ל נַפְשִֽׁ/י
verses.en: And Yaakov called the name of the place Peni'el: "For I have seen God face to face, and my breath-of-life has been delivered."
verses.ar: فَسَمّى يَعقوبُ المَوضِعَ فَنيئيلَ: «لِأنّي رَأيتُ اللهَ وَجهًا لِوَجهٍ، ونُجِّيَت نَفَسُ حَياتي.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּֽזְרַֽח ל֣/וֹ הַ/שֶּׁ֔מֶשׁ כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָבַ֖ר אֶת פְּנוּאֵ֑ל וְ/ה֥וּא צֹלֵ֖עַ עַל יְרֵכֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And the sun shone upon him as he crossed over Penu'el, and he was limping upon his thigh.
verses.ar: وأشرَقَت عَلَيهِ الشَّمسُ وهُوَ يَعبُرُ فَنوئيلَ، وهُوَ يَعرُجُ على فَخِذِهِ.

verses.he: עַל כֵּ֡ן לֹֽא יֹאכְל֨וּ בְנֵֽי יִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל אֶת גִּ֣יד הַ/נָּשֶׁ֗ה אֲשֶׁר֙ עַל כַּ֣ף הַ/יָּרֵ֔ךְ עַ֖ד הַ/יּ֣וֹם הַ/זֶּ֑ה כִּ֤י נָגַע֙ בְּ/כַף יֶ֣רֶךְ יַעֲקֹ֔ב בְּ/גִ֖יד הַ/נָּשֶֽׁה
verses.en: Therefore the children of Yisra'el do not eat the sinew of the displaced tendon, which is upon the hollow of the thigh, unto this day; for he touched the hollow of Yaakov's thigh at the sinew of the displaced tendon.
verses.ar: لِذلِكَ لا يَأكُلُ بَنو إسرائيلَ عِرقَ النَّسا الَّذي على حُقِّ الفَخِذِ إلى هذا اليَومِ؛ لِأنَّهُ مَسَّ حُقَّ فَخِذِ يَعقوبَ في عِرقِ النَّسا.

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Bereshit Chapter 32 — Yaakov Wrestles with God

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.2: מַלְאֲכֵי אֱלֹהִים (mal'akhei elohim) — "messengers of God"
Root מ-ל-ך/ל-א-ך: messenger. Yaakov encounters divine messengers — the same word used for the beings ascending and descending the stairway in 28:12. He is bookended by angelic encounters: messengers at departure (Beit-El) and messengers at return (Machanayim). The journey begun with a vision of angels concludes with another.

### v.3: מַחֲנַיִם (Machanayim) — "Two Camps"
Root ח-נ-ה (ḥ-n-h): to encamp, to pitch camp. The dual form: two camps. Yaakov names the place for the divine camp he sees, and then in v.8 he himself divides into two camps out of fear of Esav. The divine two-camp becomes a human two-camp — the structure mirrors from heavenly to earthly.

### v.5: עֶבֶד (ʿeved) — "servant"
Root ע-ב-ד (ʿ-b-d): to serve, to work, to worship. Yaakov refers to himself as Esav's "servant" — an extraordinary reversal. The one who was blessed as "lord over your brothers" (27:29) now calls his brother "lord" (אדני, adoni) and himself "servant." The stolen blessing has not made him lord; it has made him a frightened suppliant. Twenty years of exile have humbled the supplanter. Cognate with Arabic عبد (ʿabd, servant/worshiper) — the essential posture of the creature before the Creator.

### v.11: חֲסָדִים (ḥasadim) — "loyal-kindnesses"
Root ח-ס-ד (ḥ-s-d): loyal love, covenant faithfulness. "I am too small for all the loyal-kindnesses (ḥasadim) and for all the faithfulness (emet)." Yaakov's prayer is a model: he acknowledges his unworthiness, recalls God's promises, names his fear honestly, and asks for deliverance. The ḥesed-emet pair (loyal-kindness and faithfulness) returns from chapter 24 — the servant's prayer language is now Yaakov's.

### v.21: אֲכַפְּרָה פָנָיו (akhappe'rah fanav) — "I will cover over his face"
Root כ-פ-ר (k-p-r): to cover, to atone. "I will cover over (akhappe'rah) his face (fanav) with the gift (minḥah)." The same root as כפורת (kapporet, mercy-seat) and כפור (kippur, atonement). Yaakov plans to "atone" Esav's face — to cover over his anger with gifts. The language of sacrifice and atonement is applied to a human reconciliation. Cognate with Arabic كفر (kafara, to cover) — here the covering is restorative, not concealing.

### v.21: יִשָּׂא פָנַי (yissa fanai) — "he will lift up my face"
Root נ-ש-א (n-s-'): to lift, to bear. "Perhaps he will lift up my face" — accept me, show me favor. The phrase "lifting the face" means to show acceptance, to receive favorably. Yaakov hopes that the covering (כפר) of gifts will lead to the lifting (נשא) of his face. Covering leads to lifting — atonement leads to acceptance.

### v.23: יַבֹּק (Yabboq)
The river Yabboq (יבק) contains the consonants of Yaakov (יעקב) rearranged — the place-name puns on the patriarch's name. At the Yabboq, Yaakov will be unmade and remade. The wordplay extends to the wrestling: וַיֵּאָבֵק (vayyeavek, "and he wrestled") uses the same consonants as יבק. The river, the man, and the act are woven together phonetically.

### v.25: וַיֵּאָבֵק (vayyeavek) — "wrestled"
Root א-ב-ק (ʾ-b-q): to wrestle, to grapple, possibly related to אבק (avaq, dust) — they rolled in the dust. A man (אִישׁ, ish) wrestles with him. The identity of the "man" is never stated clearly — he is called "a man" (v.25), but Yaakov names the place for seeing God "face to face" (v.31), and Hosea 12:4 says he "struggled with God (אלהים) and with a messenger (מלאך)." The text preserves the ambiguity: human? angel? God? All at once?

### v.29: יִשְׂרָאֵל (Yisra'el) — "He struggles with God"
Root ש-ר-ה (s-r-h): to struggle, to contend, to persist, to be a prince. "For you have struggled (שרית, sarita) with God (אלהים, elohim) and with men, and you have prevailed (ותוכל, vatukhal)." The name Yisra'el encodes the defining act: wrestling/struggling with God. This is NOT a name of piety or submission but of tenacious engagement — holding on, refusing to let go, demanding a blessing. Israel is the people named for their struggle with God. Some connect the root to שרר (sarar, to rule/be a prince), yielding "God rules" or "prince of God" — but the context clearly explains it as struggle.

### v.31: פְּנִיאֵל (Peni'el) — "Face of God"
Root פ-נ-ה (p-n-h): face, presence. + אל (El, God). "For I have seen God face to face (פנים אל פנים, panim el panim) and my breath-of-life (נפשי, nafshi) has been delivered." The naming connects to the entire face-motif: Yaakov sought to "cover the face" of Esav (v.21), hoped Esav would "lift his face" (v.21), and now has seen the face of God. Face after face: the journey is toward the ultimate face.

### v.32: צוֹלֵעַ (tsoleiʿa) — "limping"
Root צ-ל-ע (ts-l-ʿ): to limp, to be lame. Yaakov crosses Penuel limping — the sun rises on a wounded man with a new name. He is Israel, the God-wrestler, but he walks with a permanent limp. The wound is the mark of the encounter: you cannot wrestle with God and walk away unmarked. The limp is both disability and badge of honor.

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| כפר (kafar) | كفر (kafara) | to cover/atone | Yaakov "covers" Esav's face — atonement as reconciliation |
| עבד (ʿeved) | عبد (ʿabd) | servant | 19:30 — "I am the servant of God" (Jesus) |
| פנים (panim) | — | face (cf. وجه, wajh) | 2:115 — "Wherever you turn, there is the face (wajh) of God" |
| שרה (sarah) | — | to struggle (cf. جاهد, jāhada) | 29:69 — "Those who struggle (جاهدوا) in Us, We will guide" |
| חסד (ḥesed) | — | loyal-kindness (cf. رحمة, raḥmah) | Yaakov's prayer invoking God's loyal-kindness |
| נפש (nefesh) | نفس (nafs) | breath/self/life | 32:31 "my נפש was delivered" — survival of the encounter |
| מלאך (mal'akh) | ملائكة (malā'ikah) | messenger/angel | 32:2 — divine messengers encounter Yaakov |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

The wrestling at Peniel is one of the most profound passages in all scripture. 'Abdu'l-Bahá interprets the struggle as the soul's engagement with divine reality — the spiritual seeker must contend with God, not in opposition but in the intensity of longing. The demand "I will not let you go unless you have blessed me" is the cry of the mystic: the soul that has touched the divine refuses to release until transformed. The new name (Yisra'el, "he struggles with God") replaces the old (Yaakov, "heel-grasper/supplanter") — transformation of identity through divine encounter. But the limp remains: the encounter leaves its mark. Bahá'u'lláh writes of the seeker: "He must so cleanse his heart that no remnant of either love or hate may linger therein, lest that love blindly incline him to error, or that hate repel him away from the truth."

The renaming also illustrates a Bahá'í principle: that the individual's identity is transformed when called by God to a higher purpose. Avram becomes Avraham, Sarai becomes Sarah, Yaakov becomes Yisra'el — and in each case the new name carries the new mission.

## Integrative Links

- **v.11 "I am too small for all the loyal-kindnesses" <> Qur'an 14:34**: "If you count the blessings of God, you cannot number them" — the acknowledgment of receiving beyond deserving
- **v.25 "a man wrestled with him" <> Qur'an 29:69**: "Those who struggle in Us, We will guide them to Our paths" — the divine encounter through struggle (jihad in its deepest sense)
- **v.29 "Yisra'el" <> Qur'an 3:93**: "All food was permitted to the children of Yisra'el" — the Qur'an uses the name bestowed here, honoring the identity born from the wrestling
- **v.31 "I have seen God face to face" <> Qur'an 2:115**: "Wherever you turn, there is the face (wajh) of God" — the face of God as the ultimate reality encountered
- **v.32 the limping <> Qur'an 94:5-6**: "With hardship comes ease" — the wound and the blessing are inseparable; the limp is the price and the proof of the encounter

chapters.title: Bereshit — פרק 33
chapters.subtitle: Genesis — Chapter 33

verses.he: וַ/יִּשָּׂ֨א יַעֲקֹ֜ב עֵינָ֗י/ו וַ/יַּרְא֙ וְ/הִנֵּ֣ה עֵשָׂ֣ו בָּ֔א וְ/עִמּ֕/וֹ אַרְבַּ֥ע מֵא֖וֹת אִ֑ישׁ וַ/יַּ֣חַץ אֶת הַ/יְלָדִ֗ים עַל לֵאָה֙ וְ/עַל רָחֵ֔ל וְ/עַ֖ל שְׁתֵּ֥י הַ/שְּׁפָחֽוֹת
verses.en: And Yaakov lifted up his eyes and saw, and behold, Esav was coming, and with him four hundred men. And he divided the children upon Le'ah and upon Rachel and upon the two maidservants.
verses.ar: ورَفَعَ يَعقوبُ عَينَيهِ ونَظَرَ، وإذا عيسو قادِمٌ ومَعَهُ أربَعُمِئَةِ رَجُلٍ. فَقَسَمَ الأولادَ على لَيئَةَ وعلى راحيلَ وعلى الجارِيَتَينِ.

verses.he: וַ/יָּ֧שֶׂם אֶת הַ/שְּׁפָח֛וֹת וְ/אֶת יַלְדֵי/הֶ֖ן רִֽאשֹׁנָ֑ה וְ/אֶת לֵאָ֤ה וִֽ/ילָדֶ֨י/הָ֙ אַחֲרֹנִ֔ים וְ/אֶת רָחֵ֥ל וְ/אֶת יוֹסֵ֖ף אַחֲרֹנִֽים
verses.en: And he set the maidservants and their children first, and Le'ah and her children after, and Rachel and Yoseph last.
verses.ar: ووَضَعَ الجارِيَتَينِ وأولادَهُما أوَّلًا، ولَيئَةَ وأولادَها بَعدَهُنَّ، وراحيلَ ويوسُفَ آخِرًا.

verses.he: וְ/ה֖וּא עָבַ֣ר לִ/פְנֵי/הֶ֑ם וַ/יִּשְׁתַּ֤חוּ אַ֨רְצָ/ה֙ שֶׁ֣בַע פְּעָמִ֔ים עַד גִּשְׁתּ֖/וֹ עַד אָחִֽי/ו
verses.en: And he himself crossed over before them, and bowed down to the earth seven times, until he drew near to his brother.
verses.ar: وهُوَ عَبَرَ أمامَهُم وسَجَدَ إلى الأرضِ سَبعَ مَرّاتٍ، حَتّى دَنا مِن أخيهِ.

verses.en: And Esav ran to meet him, and embraced him, and fell upon his neck, and kissed him; and they wept.
verses.ar: فَرَكَضَ عيسو لِلِقائِهِ واحتَضَنَهُ، ووَقَعَ على عُنُقِهِ وقَبَّلَهُ؛ وبَكَيا.

verses.he: וַ/יִּשָּׂ֣א אֶת עֵינָ֗י/ו וַ/יַּ֤רְא אֶת הַ/נָּשִׁים֙ וְ/אֶת הַ/יְלָדִ֔ים וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר מִי אֵ֣לֶּה לָּ֑/ךְ וַ/יֹּאמַ֕ר הַ/יְלָדִ֕ים אֲשֶׁר חָנַ֥ן אֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶת עַבְדֶּֽ/ךָ
verses.en: And he lifted up his eyes and saw the women and the children, and said: "Who are these to you?" And he said: "The children whom God has graciously given your servant."
verses.ar: ورَفَعَ عَينَيهِ ورَأى النِّساءَ والأولادَ وقالَ: «مَن هؤلاءِ لَكَ؟» فقالَ: «الأولادُ الَّذينَ أنعَمَ اللهُ بِهِم على عَبدِكَ.»

verses.he: וַ/תִּגַּ֧שְׁןָ הַ/שְּׁפָח֛וֹת הֵ֥נָּה וְ/יַלְדֵי/הֶ֖ן וַ/תִּֽשְׁתַּחֲוֶֽיןָ
verses.en: And the maidservants drew near, they and their children, and bowed down.
verses.ar: فَدَنَتِ الجارِيَتانِ هُما وأولادُهُما وسَجَدنَ.

verses.he: וַ/תִּגַּ֧שׁ גַּם לֵאָ֛ה וִ/ילָדֶ֖י/הָ וַ/יִּֽשְׁתַּחֲו֑וּ וְ/אַחַ֗ר נִגַּ֥שׁ יוֹסֵ֛ף וְ/רָחֵ֖ל וַ/יִּֽשְׁתַּחֲוֽוּ
verses.en: And Le'ah also drew near with her children, and they bowed down; and afterward Yoseph drew near and Rachel, and they bowed down.
verses.ar: ودَنَت لَيئَةُ أيضًا وأولادُها وسَجَدوا؛ ثُمَّ دَنا يوسُفُ وراحيلُ وسَجَدا.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר מִ֥י לְ/ךָ֛ כָּל הַ/מַּחֲנֶ֥ה הַ/זֶּ֖ה אֲשֶׁ֣ר פָּגָ֑שְׁתִּי וַ/יֹּ֕אמֶר לִ/מְצֹא חֵ֖ן בְּ/עֵינֵ֥י אֲדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: And he said: "What to you is all this camp that I met?" And he said: "To find favor in the eyes of my lord."
verses.ar: فقالَ: «ما لَكَ وكُلُّ هذا المُعَسكَرِ الَّذي لَقيتُ؟» فقالَ: «لِأجِدَ نِعمَةً في عَينَي سَيِّدي.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֥אמֶר עֵשָׂ֖ו יֶשׁ לִ֣/י רָ֑ב אָחִ֕/י יְהִ֥י לְ/ךָ֖ אֲשֶׁר לָֽ/ךְ
verses.en: And Esav said: "I have plenty, my brother; let what is yours be yours."
verses.ar: فقالَ عيسو: «عِندي كَثيرٌ يا أخي؛ لِيَكُن لَكَ ما هُوَ لَكَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֗ב אַל נָא֙ אִם נָ֨א מָצָ֤אתִי חֵן֙ בְּ/עֵינֶ֔י/ךָ וְ/לָקַחְתָּ֥ מִנְחָתִ֖/י מִ/יָּדִ֑/י כִּ֣י עַל כֵּ֞ן רָאִ֣יתִי פָנֶ֗י/ךָ כִּ/רְאֹ֛ת פְּנֵ֥י אֱלֹהִ֖ים וַ/תִּרְצֵֽ/נִי
verses.en: And Yaakov said: "No, I pray you, if I have found favor in your eyes, then take my gift from my hand; for I have seen your face as one sees the face of God, and you have been pleased with me.
verses.ar: فقالَ يَعقوبُ: «لا أرجوكَ، إن وَجَدتُ نِعمَةً في عَينَيكَ فَخُذ هَدِيَّتي مِن يَدي؛ فإنّي رَأيتُ وَجهَكَ كَمَن يَرى وَجهَ اللهِ، وقَد رَضيتَ عَنّي.

verses.he: קַח נָ֤א אֶת בִּרְכָתִ/י֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר הֻבָ֣את לָ֔/ךְ כִּֽי חַנַּ֥/נִי אֱלֹהִ֖ים וְ/כִ֣י יֶשׁ לִ/י כֹ֑ל וַ/יִּפְצַר בּ֖/וֹ וַ/יִּקָּֽח
verses.en: Take, I pray, my blessing that has been brought to you, for God has been gracious to me, and I have all." And he urged him, and he took it.
verses.ar: خُذْ أرجوكَ بَرَكَتي الَّتي أُتِيَت بِها إلَيكَ، فإنَّ اللهَ أنعَمَ عَلَيَّ، ولي كُلُّ شَيءٍ.» فألَحَّ عَلَيهِ فأخَذَها.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֖אמֶר נִסְעָ֣ה וְ/נֵלֵ֑כָה וְ/אֵלְכָ֖ה לְ/נֶגְדֶּֽ/ךָ
verses.en: And he said: "Let us journey and let us go, and I will go alongside you."
verses.ar: فقالَ: «لِنَرحَلْ ونَمضِ، وأنا أسيرُ بِجِوارِكَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֗י/ו אֲדֹנִ֤/י יֹדֵ֨עַ֙ כִּֽי הַ/יְלָדִ֣ים רַכִּ֔ים וְ/הַ/צֹּ֥אן וְ/הַ/בָּקָ֖ר עָל֣וֹת עָלָ֑/י וּ/דְפָקוּ/ם֙ י֣וֹם אֶחָ֔ד וָ/מֵ֖תוּ כָּל הַ/צֹּֽאן
verses.en: And he said to him: "My lord knows that the children are tender, and the flock and the cattle are nursing upon me; and if they drive them hard one day, all the flock will die.
verses.ar: فقالَ لَهُ: «سَيِّدي يَعلَمُ أنَّ الأولادَ رِقاقٌ، والغَنَمَ والبَقَرَ مُرضِعاتٌ عَلَيَّ؛ وإن أجهَدوها يَومًا واحِدًا ماتَتِ الغَنَمُ كُلُّها.

verses.he: יַעֲבָר נָ֥א אֲדֹנִ֖/י לִ/פְנֵ֣י עַבְדּ֑/וֹ וַ/אֲנִ֞י אֶֽתְנָהֲלָ֣ה לְ/אִטִּ֗/י לְ/רֶ֨גֶל הַ/מְּלָאכָ֤ה אֲשֶׁר לְ/פָנַ/י֙ וּ/לְ/רֶ֣גֶל הַ/יְלָדִ֔ים עַ֛ד אֲשֶׁר אָבֹ֥א אֶל אֲדֹנִ֖/י שֵׂעִֽירָ/ה
verses.en: Let my lord, I pray, cross over before his servant, and I will lead on gently, at the pace of the work that is before me, and at the pace of the children, until I come to my lord at Se'ir."
verses.ar: لِيَعبُرْ سَيِّدي أرجوكَ أمامَ عَبدِهِ، وأنا أسوقُ رُوَيدًا رُوَيدًا بِحَسَبِ العَمَلِ الَّذي أمامي وبِحَسَبِ الأولادِ، حَتّى آتيَ إلى سَيِّدي في سِعيرَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר עֵשָׂ֔ו אַצִּֽיגָה נָּ֣א עִמְּ/ךָ֔ מִן הָ/עָ֖ם אֲשֶׁ֣ר אִתִּ֑/י וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר֙ לָ֣/מָּה זֶּ֔ה אֶמְצָא חֵ֖ן בְּ/עֵינֵ֥י אֲדֹנִֽ/י
verses.en: And Esav said: "Let me station with you some of the people who are with me." And he said: "Why this? Let me find favor in the eyes of my lord."
verses.ar: فقالَ عيسو: «دَعني أُقيمُ مَعَكَ بَعضَ مَن مَعي.» فقالَ: «لِماذا هذا؟ لِأجِدْ نِعمَةً في عَينَي سَيِّدي.»

verses.he: וַ/יָּשָׁב֩ בַּ/יּ֨וֹם הַ/ה֥וּא עֵשָׂ֛ו לְ/דַרְכּ֖/וֹ שֵׂעִֽירָ/ה
verses.en: And Esav returned that day on his way to Se'ir.
verses.ar: فَرَجَعَ عيسو في ذلِكَ اليَومِ في طَريقِهِ إلى سِعيرَ.

verses.he: וְ/יַעֲקֹב֙ נָסַ֣ע סֻכֹּ֔תָ/ה וַ/יִּ֥בֶן ל֖/וֹ בָּ֑יִת וּ/לְ/מִקְנֵ֨/הוּ֙ עָשָׂ֣ה סֻכֹּ֔ת עַל כֵּ֛ן קָרָ֥א שֵׁם הַ/מָּק֖וֹם סֻכּֽוֹת
verses.en: And Yaakov journeyed to Sukkot, and built himself a house, and for his cattle he made shelters; therefore the name of the place was called Sukkot.
verses.ar: ورَحَلَ يَعقوبُ إلى سُكّوتَ، وبَنى لِنَفسِهِ بَيتًا، ولِماشِيَتِهِ صَنَعَ مَظالَّ؛ لِذلِكَ سُمِّيَ المَوضِعُ سُكّوتَ.

verses.he: וַ/יָּבֹא֩ יַעֲקֹ֨ב שָׁלֵ֜ם עִ֣יר שְׁכֶ֗ם אֲשֶׁר֙ בְּ/אֶ֣רֶץ כְּנַ֔עַן בְּ/בֹא֖/וֹ מִ/פַּדַּ֣ן אֲרָ֑ם וַ/יִּ֖חַן אֶת פְּנֵ֥י הָ/עִֽיר
verses.en: And Yaakov came whole to the city of Shekhem, which is in the land of Kena'an, when he came from Paddan Aram; and he encamped before the face of the city.
verses.ar: وجاءَ يَعقوبُ سالِمًا إلى مَدينَةِ شَكيمَ الَّتي في أرضِ كَنعانَ، حينَ جاءَ مِن فَدّانِ آرامَ؛ ونَزَلَ أمامَ المَدينَةِ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּ֜קֶן אֶת חֶלְקַ֣ת הַ/שָּׂדֶ֗ה אֲשֶׁ֤ר נָֽטָה שָׁם֙ אָהֳל֔/וֹ מִ/יַּ֥ד בְּנֵֽי חֲמ֖וֹר אֲבִ֣י שְׁכֶ֑ם בְּ/מֵאָ֖ה קְשִׂיטָֽה
verses.en: And he acquired the portion of the field where he had pitched his tent, from the hand of the sons of Chamor, father of Shekhem, for a hundred qesitah.
verses.ar: واشتَرى قِطعَةَ الحَقلِ الَّتي نَصَبَ فيها خَيمَتَهُ مِن يَدِ بَني حَمورَ أبي شَكيمَ بِمِئَةِ قِسطَةٍ.

verses.he: וַ/יַּצֶּב שָׁ֖ם מִזְבֵּ֑חַ וַ/יִּ֨קְרָא ל֔/וֹ אֵ֖ל אֱלֹהֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל
verses.en: And he set up there an altar, and called it El, God of Yisra'el.
verses.ar: وأقامَ هُناكَ مَذبَحًا وسَمّاهُ إيلَ إلهَ إسرائيلَ.

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Bereshit Chapter 33 — The Reconciliation with Esav

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.3: וַיִּשְׁתַּחוּ (vayyishtaḥu) — "bowed down"
Root ש-ח-ה (sh-ḥ-h): to bow down, to prostrate. Yaakov bows seven times to the ground as he approaches Esav. This is the posture of a vassal approaching a king — the man blessed as "lord over your brothers" (27:29) now prostrates himself seven times. The seven-fold bowing is complete submission. The stolen blessing has been, in effect, voluntarily surrendered in this act.

### v.4: וַיְחַבְּקֵהוּ (vayeḥabbeqehu) — "embraced him"
Root ח-ב-ק (ḥ-b-q): to embrace, to clasp. Esav runs, embraces, falls on Yaakov's neck, and kisses him. The verbs pile up: ran (וירץ), embraced (ויחבקהו), fell upon his neck (ויפל על צוארו), kissed (וישקהו), and they wept (ויבכו). The reconciliation is physical, immediate, and emotional. The masoretic text places dots above the word וישקהו ("and he kissed him") — the rabbis debated whether the dots signal that the kiss was genuine or insincere. The weight of the narrative favors sincerity: this is real reconciliation.

### v.10: כִּרְאֹת פְּנֵי אֱלֹהִים (kir'ot penei elohim) — "as one sees the face of God"
Root ר-א-ה (r-'-h): to see. Root פ-נ-ה (p-n-h): face. "I have seen your face as one sees the face of God." Yaakov has just seen the face of God at Peniel (32:31), and now he sees God's face in Esav's face. This is a staggering theological claim: the face of the estranged brother, the wronged brother, shows forth the divine countenance. Reconciliation with the human other is an encounter with the divine. The face motif reaches its climax here.

### v.11: בִּרְכָתִי (birkhati) — "my blessing"
Root ב-ר-ך (b-r-kh): to bless. "Take, I pray, my blessing that has been brought to you." Yaakov offers his מנחה (minḥah, gift) and calls it a "blessing" (berakhah). The word choice is deliberate: he who stole Esav's blessing (27:35-36) now gives a blessing back. The circle closes. The stolen ברכה is returned, transformed into a voluntary gift. Cognate with Arabic بركة (barakah, blessing).

### v.17: סֻכּוֹת (Sukkot) — "Shelters"
Root ס-כ-ך (s-k-kh): to cover, to shelter, to weave together. Yaakov builds shelters for his cattle and names the place Sukkot. The same word gives the festival of Sukkot (Tabernacles/Booths) — the annual remembrance of temporary shelters in the wilderness. The sukkah is a fragile, temporary dwelling — a reminder that all human habitation is transient.

### v.18: שָׁלֵם (shalem) — "whole"
Root ש-ל-ם (sh-l-m): whole, complete, at peace. "Yaakov came whole (שלם) to the city of Shekhem." After the wrestling and the limping, after the fear and the reconciliation, Yaakov arrives shalem — whole. The word choice is extraordinary: the man who left fragmented (fleeing, deceiving, alone) returns whole. Shalem is the root of שלום (shalom, peace) and of ירושלם (Yerushalayim, Jerusalem — "foundation of wholeness"). Cognate with Arabic سالم (sālim, whole/safe) and سلام (salām, peace).

### v.19: קְשִׂיטָה (qesitah) — "qesitah"
An ancient unit of value, mentioned only here and in Joshua 24:32 and Job 42:11. The word is so archaic that its exact value is unknown. Yaakov purchases land — the second land purchase in Genesis (after Avraham's in ch. 23). Both purchases are legal transactions with local populations, establishing legitimate claim to specific parcels.

### v.20: אֵל אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל (El Elohei Yisra'el) — "God, God of Yisra'el"
Yaakov names the altar with his new name: the God of Yisra'el, not the God of Yaakov. The new identity, received at Peniel, is now inscribed in worship. He claims his relationship with God under his covenant name.

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| שלם (shalem) | سالم/سلام (sālim/salām) | whole/peace | 10:25 — "God calls to the abode of peace (سلام)" |
| ברכה (berakhah) | بركة (barakah) | blessing | The return of blessing — the reconciliation |
| פנים (panim) | وجه (wajh) | face | 2:115 — the face of God in every direction |
| חבק (ḥavaq) | — | to embrace | The embrace of reconciliation |
| שחה (shaḥah) | سجد (sajada) | to bow/prostrate | Throughout — prostration before God and as honor |
| מנחה (minḥah) | — | gift/offering (cf. هدية, hadiyyah) | Gift as means of reconciliation |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

The reconciliation of Yaakov and Esav is one of the most moving scenes in Scripture and carries deep significance in Bahá'í thought. 'Abdu'l-Bahá teaches that reconciliation is the highest human achievement — greater than worship or sacrifice. Yaakov's recognition that Esav's face is "like the face of God" illustrates the Bahá'í principle that the divine image is present in every human face. To see God in the face of the one you have wronged, or who has wronged you, is the spiritual summit.

The fact that Yaakov arrives "whole" (shalem) to Shekhem, despite his limp, teaches that wholeness does not require perfection. He is whole AND wounded. The two are not contradictory: the wholeness includes the wound, encompasses it, integrates it. This is a profound model for spiritual maturity.

## Integrative Links

- **v.4 the embrace <> Qur'an 12:99-100**: The reunion of Yaakov (as father) with Yoseph in Egypt — the Qur'an describes a similar scene of tearful reunion, bowing, and reconciliation within this same family
- **v.10 "as one sees the face of God" <> Qur'an 2:115**: "Wherever you turn, there is the face of God" — seeing God's face in the human face
- **v.11 "take my blessing" <> Qur'an 2:177**: "Righteousness is... to give wealth out of love" — the giving back of what was taken, the voluntary offering
- **v.18 "Yaakov came whole" <> Qur'an 89:27-30**: "O soul at peace, return to your Lord, well-pleased and well-pleasing" — the arrival in wholeness/peace as the goal of the journey

chapters.title: Bereshit — פרק 34
chapters.subtitle: Genesis — Chapter 34

verses.he: וַ/תֵּצֵ֤א דִינָה֙ בַּת לֵאָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר יָלְדָ֖ה לְ/יַעֲקֹ֑ב לִ/רְא֖וֹת בִּ/בְנ֥וֹת הָ/אָֽרֶץ
verses.en: And Dinah, the daughter of Le'ah, whom she had borne to Yaakov, went out to see among the daughters of the land.
verses.ar: وخَرَجَت دينَةُ بِنتُ لَيئَةَ الَّتي وَلَدَتها لِيَعقوبَ لِتَرى بَينَ بَناتِ الأرضِ.

verses.he: וַ/יַּ֨רְא אֹתָ֜/הּ שְׁכֶ֧ם בֶּן חֲמ֛וֹר הַֽ/חִוִּ֖י נְשִׂ֣יא הָ/אָ֑רֶץ וַ/יִּקַּ֥ח אֹתָ֛/הּ וַ/יִּשְׁכַּ֥ב אֹתָ֖/הּ וַ/יְעַנֶּֽ/הָ
verses.en: And Shekhem, son of Chamor the Chivvite, prince of the land, saw her; and he took her and lay with her and humbled her.
verses.ar: فَرَآها شَكيمُ بنُ حَمورَ الحِوّيِّ رَئيسُ الأرضِ؛ فأخَذَها واضطَجَعَ مَعَها وأذَلَّها.

verses.he: וַ/תִּדְבַּ֣ק נַפְשׁ֔/וֹ בְּ/דִינָ֖ה בַּֽת יַעֲקֹ֑ב וַ/יֶּֽאֱהַב֙ אֶת הַֽ/נַּעֲרָ֔ וַ/יְדַבֵּ֖ר עַל לֵ֥ב הַֽ/נַּעֲרָֽ
verses.en: And his breath-of-life clung to Dinah, daughter of Yaakov; and he loved the young woman, and he spoke upon the heart of the young woman.
verses.ar: وتَعَلَّقَت نَفَسُهُ بِدينَةَ بِنتِ يَعقوبَ؛ وأحَبَّ الفَتاةَ وكَلَّمَ قَلبَ الفَتاةِ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֣אמֶר שְׁכֶ֔ם אֶל חֲמ֥וֹר אָבִ֖י/ו לֵ/אמֹ֑ר קַֽח לִ֛/י אֶת הַ/יַּלְדָּ֥ה הַ/זֹּ֖את לְ/אִשָּֽׁה
verses.en: And Shekhem said to Chamor his father, saying: "Take for me this girl as a wife."
verses.ar: وقالَ شَكيمُ لِحَمورَ أبيهِ: «خُذ لي هذِهِ الفَتاةَ زَوجَةً.»

verses.he: וְ/יַעֲקֹ֣ב שָׁמַ֗ע כִּ֤י טִמֵּא֙ אֶת דִּינָ֣ה בִתּ֔/וֹ וּ/בָנָ֛י/ו הָי֥וּ אֶת מִקְנֵ֖/הוּ בַּ/שָּׂדֶ֑ה וְ/הֶחֱרִ֥שׁ יַעֲקֹ֖ב עַד בֹּאָֽ/ם
verses.en: And Yaakov heard that he had defiled Dinah his daughter; and his sons were with his cattle in the field; and Yaakov held silent until they came.
verses.ar: وسَمِعَ يَعقوبُ أنَّهُ نَجَّسَ دينَةَ ابنَتَهُ؛ وبَنوهُ كانوا مَعَ ماشِيَتِهِ في الحَقلِ؛ فَسَكَتَ يَعقوبُ حَتّى جاؤوا.

verses.he: וַ/יֵּצֵ֛א חֲמ֥וֹר אֲבִֽי שְׁכֶ֖ם אֶֽל יַעֲקֹ֑ב לְ/דַבֵּ֖ר אִתּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Chamor, father of Shekhem, went out to Yaakov to speak with him.
verses.ar: وخَرَجَ حَمورُ أبو شَكيمَ إلى يَعقوبَ لِيُكَلِّمَهُ.

verses.he: וּ/בְנֵ֨י יַעֲקֹ֜ב בָּ֤אוּ מִן הַ/שָּׂדֶה֙ כְּ/שָׁמְעָ֔/ם וַ/יִּֽתְעַצְּבוּ֙ הָֽ/אֲנָשִׁ֔ים וַ/יִּ֥חַר לָ/הֶ֖ם מְאֹ֑ד כִּֽי נְבָלָ֞ה עָשָׂ֣ה בְ/יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל לִ/שְׁכַּב֙ אֶת בַּֽת יַעֲקֹ֔ב וְ/כֵ֖ן לֹ֥א יֵעָשֶֽׂה
verses.en: And the sons of Yaakov came from the field when they heard; and the men were pained, and it burned greatly in them, for a disgrace had been wrought in Yisra'el, to lie with the daughter of Yaakov — and such a thing ought not be done.
verses.ar: وجاءَ بَنو يَعقوبَ مِنَ الحَقلِ حينَ سَمِعوا؛ وتَألَّمَ الرِّجالُ واحتَدَّ غَيظُهُم جِدًّا، لِأنَّ عارًا صُنِعَ في إسرائيلَ بِالاضطِجاعِ مَعَ بِنتِ يَعقوبَ — وما كانَ يَنبَغي أن يُصنَعَ مِثلُ ذلِكَ.

verses.he: וַ/יְדַבֵּ֥ר חֲמ֖וֹר אִתָּ֣/ם לֵ/אמֹ֑ר שְׁכֶ֣ם בְּנִ֗/י חָֽשְׁקָ֤ה נַפְשׁ/וֹ֙ בְּ/בִתְּ/כֶ֔ם תְּנ֨וּ נָ֥א אֹתָ֛/הּ ל֖/וֹ לְ/אִשָּֽׁה
verses.en: And Chamor spoke with them, saying: "Shekhem my son — his breath-of-life longs for your daughter; give her, I pray, to him as a wife.
verses.ar: وكَلَّمَهُم حَمورُ قائِلًا: «شَكيمُ ابني — نَفَسُهُ تَشتاقُ إلى ابنَتِكُم؛ أعطوها لَهُ أرجوكُم زَوجَةً.

verses.he: וְ/הִֽתְחַתְּנ֖וּ אֹתָ֑/נוּ בְּנֹֽתֵי/כֶם֙ תִּתְּנוּ לָ֔/נוּ וְ/אֶת בְּנֹתֵ֖י/נוּ תִּקְח֥וּ לָ/כֶֽם
verses.en: And make marriages with us: your daughters give to us, and our daughters take for yourselves.
verses.ar: وصاهِرونا: بَناتِكُم أعطونا، وبَناتِنا خُذوا لِأنفُسِكُم.

verses.he: וְ/אִתָּ֖/נוּ תֵּשֵׁ֑בוּ וְ/הָ/אָ֨רֶץ֙ תִּהְיֶ֣ה לִ/פְנֵי/כֶ֔ם שְׁבוּ֙ וּ/סְחָר֔וּ/הָ וְ/הֵֽאָחֲז֖וּ בָּֽ/הּ
verses.en: And with us you shall dwell; and the land shall be before you — dwell and trade in it, and take possession in it."
verses.ar: ومَعَنا تَسكُنونَ؛ والأرضُ أمامَكُم — اسكُنوا وتاجِروا فيها وتَمَلَّكوا فيها.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר שְׁכֶם֙ אֶל אָבִ֣י/ה וְ/אֶל אַחֶ֔י/הָ אֶמְצָא חֵ֖ן בְּ/עֵינֵי/כֶ֑ם וַ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר תֹּאמְר֛וּ אֵלַ֖/י אֶתֵּֽן
verses.en: And Shekhem said to her father and to her brothers: "Let me find favor in your eyes, and whatever you say to me I will give.
verses.ar: وقالَ شَكيمُ لِأبيها ولِإخوَتِها: «لِأجِدْ نِعمَةً في عُيونِكُم، وما تَقولونَهُ لي أُعطي.

verses.he: הַרְבּ֨וּ עָלַ֤/י מְאֹד֙ מֹ֣הַר וּ/מַתָּ֔ן וְ/אֶ֨תְּנָ֔ה כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֥ר תֹּאמְר֖וּ אֵלָ֑/י וּ/תְנוּ לִ֥/י אֶת הַֽ/נַּעֲרָ֖ לְ/אִשָּֽׁה
verses.en: Increase upon me greatly the bride-price and the gift, and I will give as you say to me; but give me the young woman as a wife."
verses.ar: أكثِروا عَلَيَّ جِدًّا المَهرَ والهَدِيَّةَ وأنا أُعطي كَما تَقولونَ لي؛ لكِن أعطوني الفَتاةَ زَوجَةً.»

verses.he: וַ/יַּעֲנ֨וּ בְנֵֽי יַעֲקֹ֜ב אֶת שְׁכֶ֨ם וְ/אֶת חֲמ֥וֹר אָבִ֛י/ו בְּ/מִרְמָ֖ה וַ/יְדַבֵּ֑רוּ אֲשֶׁ֣ר טִמֵּ֔א אֵ֖ת דִּינָ֥ה אֲחֹתָֽ/ם
verses.en: And the sons of Yaakov answered Shekhem and Chamor his father with guile, and they spoke — because he had defiled Dinah their sister.
verses.ar: فأجابَ بَنو يَعقوبَ شَكيمَ وحَمورَ أباهُ بِمَكرٍ وتَكَلَّموا — لِأنَّهُ نَجَّسَ دينَةَ أُختَهُم.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאמְר֣וּ אֲלֵי/הֶ֗ם לֹ֤א נוּכַל֙ לַ/עֲשׂוֹת֙ הַ/דָּבָ֣ר הַ/זֶּ֔ה לָ/תֵת֙ אֶת אֲחֹתֵ֔/נוּ לְ/אִ֖ישׁ אֲשֶׁר ל֣/וֹ עָרְלָ֑ה כִּֽי חֶרְפָּ֥ה הִ֖וא לָֽ/נוּ
verses.en: And they said to them: "We cannot do this thing, to give our sister to a man who has a foreskin; for it is a disgrace to us.
verses.ar: وقالوا لَهُم: «لا نَستَطيعُ أن نَصنَعَ هذا الأمرَ، أن نُعطيَ أُختَنا لِرَجُلٍ ذي غُرلَةٍ؛ فإنَّهُ عارٌ لَنا.

verses.he: אַךְ בְּ/זֹ֖את נֵא֣וֹת לָ/כֶ֑ם אִ֚ם תִּהְי֣וּ כָמֹ֔/נוּ לְ/הִמֹּ֥ל לָ/כֶ֖ם כָּל זָכָֽר
verses.en: Only on this condition will we consent to you: if you become like us, for every male of yours to be circumcised.
verses.ar: إنَّما بِهذا نَرضى عَنكُم: إن صِرتُم مِثلَنا بِأن يُختَنَ كُلُّ ذَكَرٍ فيكُم.

verses.he: וְ/נָתַ֤נּוּ אֶת בְּנֹתֵ֨י/נוּ֙ לָ/כֶ֔ם וְ/אֶת בְּנֹתֵי/כֶ֖ם נִֽקַּֽח לָ֑/נוּ וְ/יָשַׁ֣בְנוּ אִתְּ/כֶ֔ם וְ/הָיִ֖ינוּ לְ/עַ֥ם אֶחָֽד
verses.en: Then we will give our daughters to you, and your daughters we will take for ourselves, and we will dwell with you, and we will become one people.
verses.ar: فَنُعطيَكُم بَناتِنا، وبَناتِكُم نَأخُذَ لَنا، ونَسكُنَ مَعَكُم ونَصيرَ شَعبًا واحِدًا.

verses.he: וְ/אִם לֹ֧א תִשְׁמְע֛וּ אֵלֵ֖י/נוּ לְ/הִמּ֑וֹל וְ/לָקַ֥חְנוּ אֶת בִּתֵּ֖/נוּ וְ/הָלָֽכְנוּ
verses.en: But if you will not listen to us, to be circumcised, then we will take our daughter and we will go."
verses.ar: وإن لَم تَسمَعوا لَنا أن تُختَنوا، فَنَأخُذَ ابنَتَنا ونَمضيَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּֽיטְב֥וּ דִבְרֵי/הֶ֖ם בְּ/עֵינֵ֣י חֲמ֑וֹר וּ/בְ/עֵינֵ֖י שְׁכֶ֥ם בֶּן חֲמֽוֹר
verses.en: And their words were good in the eyes of Chamor and in the eyes of Shekhem son of Chamor.
verses.ar: وحَسُنَ كَلامُهُم في عَينَي حَمورَ وفي عَينَي شَكيمَ بنِ حَمورَ.

verses.he: וְ/לֹֽא אֵחַ֤ר הַ/נַּ֨עַר֙ לַ/עֲשׂ֣וֹת הַ/דָּבָ֔ר כִּ֥י חָפֵ֖ץ בְּ/בַֽת יַעֲקֹ֑ב וְ/ה֣וּא נִכְבָּ֔ד מִ/כֹּ֖ל בֵּ֥ית אָבִֽי/ו
verses.en: And the young man did not delay to do the thing, for he delighted in the daughter of Yaakov; and he was the most honored of all his father's house.
verses.ar: ولَم يَتَأخَّرِ الفَتى في فِعلِ الأمرِ، لِأنَّهُ سُرَّ بِابنَةِ يَعقوبَ؛ وهُوَ أكرَمُ كُلِّ بَيتِ أبيهِ.

verses.he: וַ/יָּבֹ֥א חֲמ֛וֹר וּ/שְׁכֶ֥ם בְּנ֖/וֹ אֶל שַׁ֣עַר עִירָ֑/ם וַֽ/יְדַבְּר֛וּ אֶל אַנְשֵׁ֥י עִירָ֖/ם לֵ/אמֹֽר
verses.en: And Chamor and Shekhem his son came to the gate of their city, and spoke to the men of their city, saying:
verses.ar: فَجاءَ حَمورُ وشَكيمُ ابنُهُ إلى بابِ مَدينَتِهِما وكَلَّما رِجالَ مَدينَتِهِما قائِلَينِ:

verses.he: הָ/אֲנָשִׁ֨ים הָ/אֵ֜לֶּה שְֽׁלֵמִ֧ים הֵ֣ם אִתָּ֗/נוּ וְ/יֵשְׁב֤וּ בָ/אָ֨רֶץ֙ וְ/יִסְחֲר֣וּ אֹתָ֔/הּ וְ/הָ/אָ֛רֶץ הִנֵּ֥ה רַֽחֲבַת יָדַ֖יִם לִ/פְנֵי/הֶ֑ם אֶת בְּנֹתָ/ם֙ נִקַּֽח לָ֣/נוּ לְ/נָשִׁ֔ים וְ/אֶת בְּנֹתֵ֖י/נוּ נִתֵּ֥ן לָ/הֶֽם
verses.en: "These men are at wholeness with us; let them dwell in the land and trade in it, for the land — behold, it is broad on both sides before them. Their daughters we will take for ourselves as wives, and our daughters we will give to them.
verses.ar: «هؤلاءِ القَومُ مُسالِمونَ لَنا؛ فَلِيَسكُنوا في الأرضِ ولِيَتاجِروا فيها، فالأرضُ — ها هِيَ واسِعَةُ الجانِبَينِ أمامَهُم. بَناتِهِم نَأخُذُ لَنا زَوجاتٍ، وبَناتِنا نُعطيهِم.

verses.he: אַךְ בְּ֠/זֹאת יֵאֹ֨תוּ לָ֤/נוּ הָ/אֲנָשִׁים֙ לָ/שֶׁ֣בֶת אִתָּ֔/נוּ לִ/הְי֖וֹת לְ/עַ֣ם אֶחָ֑ד בְּ/הִמּ֥וֹל לָ֨/נוּ֙ כָּל זָכָ֔ר כַּ/אֲשֶׁ֖ר הֵ֥ם נִמֹּלִֽים
verses.en: Only on this condition will the men consent to us, to dwell with us, to become one people: for every male of ours to be circumcised, as they are circumcised.
verses.ar: إنَّما بِهذا يَرضى القَومُ عَنّا لِيَسكُنوا مَعَنا ولِنَصيرَ شَعبًا واحِدًا: أن يُختَنَ كُلُّ ذَكَرٍ فينا كَما هُم مُختَتِنونَ.

verses.he: מִקְנֵ/הֶ֤ם וְ/קִנְיָנָ/ם֙ וְ/כָל בְּהֶמְתָּ֔/ם הֲ/ל֥וֹא לָ֖/נוּ הֵ֑ם אַ֚ךְ נֵא֣וֹתָה לָ/הֶ֔ם וְ/יֵשְׁב֖וּ אִתָּֽ/נוּ
verses.en: Their livestock and their possessions and all their beasts — are they not ours? Only let us consent to them, and they will dwell with us."
verses.ar: ماشِيَتُهُم ومُقتَناهُم وكُلُّ بَهائِمِهِم — ألَيسَت لَنا؟ إنَّما نَرضى لَهُم فَيَسكُنوا مَعَنا.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּשְׁמְע֤וּ אֶל חֲמוֹר֙ וְ/אֶל שְׁכֶ֣ם בְּנ֔/וֹ כָּל יֹצְאֵ֖י שַׁ֣עַר עִיר֑/וֹ וַ/יִּמֹּ֨לוּ֙ כָּל זָכָ֔ר כָּל יֹצְאֵ֖י שַׁ֥עַר עִירֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And all who went out of the gate of his city listened to Chamor and to Shekhem his son; and every male was circumcised, all who went out of the gate of his city.
verses.ar: فَسَمِعَ لِحَمورَ ولِشَكيمَ ابنِهِ كُلُّ الخارِجينَ مِن بابِ مَدينَتِهِ؛ واختُتِنَ كُلُّ ذَكَرٍ، كُلُّ الخارِجينَ مِن بابِ مَدينَتِهِ.

verses.he: וַ/יְהִי֩ בַ/יּ֨וֹם הַ/שְּׁלִישִׁ֜י בִּֽ/הְיוֹתָ֣/ם כֹּֽאֲבִ֗ים וַ/יִּקְח֣וּ שְׁנֵֽי בְנֵי יַ֠עֲקֹב שִׁמְע֨וֹן וְ/לֵוִ֜י אֲחֵ֤י דִינָה֙ אִ֣ישׁ חַרְבּ֔/וֹ וַ/יָּבֹ֥אוּ עַל הָ/עִ֖יר בֶּ֑טַח וַ/יַּֽהַרְג֖וּ כָּל זָכָֽר
verses.en: And it was on the third day, when they were in pain, that two sons of Yaakov — Shim'on and Levi, brothers of Dinah — took each man his sword, and came upon the city in its security, and slew every male.
verses.ar: وكانَ في اليَومِ الثّالِثِ وهُم في الوَجَعِ أنَّ اثنَينِ مِن بَني يَعقوبَ — شِمعونَ ولاوي أخَوَي دينَةَ — أخَذَ كُلُّ واحِدٍ سَيفَهُ ودَخَلا المَدينَةَ وهِيَ آمِنَةٌ وقَتَلا كُلَّ ذَكَرٍ.

verses.he: וְ/אֶת חֲמוֹר֙ וְ/אֶת שְׁכֶ֣ם בְּנ֔/וֹ הָרְג֖וּ לְ/פִי חָ֑רֶב וַ/יִּקְח֧וּ אֶת דִּינָ֛ה מִ/בֵּ֥ית שְׁכֶ֖ם וַ/יֵּצֵֽאוּ
verses.en: And Chamor and Shekhem his son they slew by the mouth of the sword; and they took Dinah from the house of Shekhem, and went out.
verses.ar: وحَمورَ وشَكيمَ ابنَهُ قَتَلاهُما بِحَدِّ السَّيفِ؛ وأخَذا دينَةَ مِن بَيتِ شَكيمَ وخَرَجا.

verses.he: בְּנֵ֣י יַעֲקֹ֗ב בָּ֚אוּ עַל הַ֣/חֲלָלִ֔ים וַ/יָּבֹ֖זּוּ הָ/עִ֑יר אֲשֶׁ֥ר טִמְּא֖וּ אֲחוֹתָֽ/ם
verses.en: The sons of Yaakov came upon the slain, and plundered the city, because they had defiled their sister.
verses.ar: وجاءَ بَنو يَعقوبَ على القَتلى ونَهَبوا المَدينَةَ، لِأنَّهُم نَجَّسوا أُختَهُم.

verses.he: אֶת צֹאנָ֥/ם וְ/אֶת בְּקָרָ֖/ם וְ/אֶת חֲמֹרֵי/הֶּ֑ם וְ/אֵ֧ת אֲשֶׁר בָּ/עִ֛יר וְ/אֶת אֲשֶׁ֥ר בַּ/שָּׂדֶ֖ה לָקָֽחוּ
verses.en: Their flock and their cattle and their donkeys, and that which was in the city and that which was in the field, they took.
verses.ar: غَنَمَهُم وبَقَرَهُم وحَميرَهُم، وما في المَدينَةِ وما في الحَقلِ، أخَذوا.

verses.he: וְ/אֶת כָּל חֵילָ֤/ם וְ/אֶת כָּל טַפָּ/ם֙ וְ/אֶת נְשֵׁי/הֶ֔ם שָׁב֖וּ וַ/יָּבֹ֑זּוּ וְ/אֵ֖ת כָּל אֲשֶׁ֥ר בַּ/בָּֽיִת
verses.en: And all their wealth and all their little ones and their wives they took captive, and plundered all that was in the house.
verses.ar: وكُلَّ ثَروَتِهِم وكُلَّ أطفالِهِم ونِساءَهُم سَبَوا ونَهَبوا كُلَّ ما في البُيوتِ.

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֨אמֶר יַעֲקֹ֜ב אֶל שִׁמְע֣וֹן וְ/אֶל לֵוִי֮ עֲכַרְתֶּ֣ם אֹתִ/י֒ לְ/הַבְאִישֵׁ֨/נִי֙ בְּ/יֹשֵׁ֣ב הָ/אָ֔רֶץ בַּֽ/כְּנַעֲנִ֖י וּ/בַ/פְּרִזִּ֑י וַ/אֲנִי֙ מְתֵ֣י מִסְפָּ֔ר וְ/נֶאֶסְפ֤וּ עָלַ/י֙ וְ/הִכּ֔וּ/נִי וְ/נִשְׁמַדְתִּ֖י אֲנִ֥י וּ/בֵיתִֽ/י
verses.en: And Yaakov said to Shim'on and to Levi: "You have troubled me, to make me stink among the dwellers of the land, among the Kena'anite and the Perizzite; and I am few in number, and they will gather against me and strike me, and I will be destroyed, I and my house."
verses.ar: فقالَ يَعقوبُ لِشِمعونَ ولِلاوي: «كَدَّرتُماني بِأن جَعَلتُماني أنتِنُ عِندَ سُكّانِ الأرضِ، عِندَ الكَنعانيِّ والفِرِزّيِّ؛ وأنا قَليلُ العَدَدِ، فَيَجتَمِعونَ عَلَيَّ ويَضرِبوني فأُهلَكُ أنا وبَيتي.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּאמְר֑וּ הַ/כְ/זוֹנָ֕ה יַעֲשֶׂ֖ה אֶת אֲחוֹתֵֽ/נוּ
verses.en: And they said: "Shall he treat our sister as a harlot?"
verses.ar: فقالا: «أكَبَغِيٍّ يُعامِلُ أُختَنا؟»

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Bereshit Chapter 34 — Dinah and the Violence at Shekhem

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.2: וַיְעַנֶּהָ (vayeʿanneha) — "and humbled her"
Root ע-נ-ה (ʿ-n-h): to humble, to afflict, to oppress, to violate. Conventionally "defiled her" or "violated her," but the root ענה means to humble, to bring low, to cause suffering. The same root describes the affliction of Israel in Egypt (Exodus 1:11-12). The sexual violation is understood as an act of humbling — the exercise of power that degrades. This is not seduction but subjugation.

### v.3: נַפְשׁוֹ (nafsho) — "his breath-of-life"
Root נ-פ-ש (n-f-sh): breath, life, appetite, desire. "His breath-of-life clung (דבקה, daveqah) to Dinah." After the violation, Shekhem's נפש adheres to Dinah. The verb דבק (davaq, to cling) is the same used in 2:24 for a man "clinging" to his wife. The text presents a disturbing sequence: violence, then attachment, then "love" (ויאהב), then sweet words (וידבר על לב, "he spoke upon the heart"). The order is wrong — the love follows the violence. The text does not validate this as love.

### v.7: נְבָלָה (nevalah) — "a disgrace"
Root נ-ב-ל (n-b-l): folly, disgrace, outrage, senselessness. "A disgrace (nevalah) had been wrought in Yisra'el." The word carries moral weight beyond mere foolishness — it denotes an act that violates the fundamental order, an outrage against community norms. The phrase "such a thing ought not be done" (וכן לא יעשה) establishes a moral absolute: this is simply not done.

### v.7: וַיִּתְעַצְּבוּ... וַיִּחַר לָהֶם (vayyitʿatsevu... vayyiḥar lahem) — "the men were pained, and it burned greatly in them"
Two emotional responses: עצב (ʿetsev, pain/grief) — the same root as God's grief in 6:6 before the flood — and חרה (ḥarah, burning anger). The brothers experience both grief and rage simultaneously. The text validates their emotions without validating their subsequent actions.

### v.13: בְּמִרְמָה (be-mirmah) — "with guile"
Root ר-מ-ה (r-m-h): deceit, treachery. The exact same word used for Yaakov's deception of Yitschak in 27:35. The sons of Yaakov act "with guile" — like father, like sons. The pattern of deception runs through the family, but here it escalates from stealing a blessing to orchestrating a massacre.

### v.14: עָרְלָה (ʿorlah) — "foreskin"
Root ע-ר-ל (ʿ-r-l): foreskin, uncircumcised. The demand for circumcision as a condition for intermarriage is presented as a stratagem — the sons of Yaakov use the covenant sign as a weapon. This is a perversion of circumcision's purpose: the sign of the covenant becomes the means of treachery. The text implies this is wrong.

### v.25: בֶּטַח (betaḥ) — "in its security"
Root ב-ט-ח (b-ṭ-ḥ): trust, security, confidence. "They came upon the city in its security (betaḥ)." The city of Shekhem was trusting — they had entered the covenant of circumcision in good faith. The attack exploits that trust. The word betaḥ carries the weight of the betrayal: they were secure, confident in the agreement, and were slaughtered because of it. Cognate with the Arabic concept of أمان (amān, safety/security).

### v.25: חֶרֶב (ḥerev) — "sword"
Root ח-ר-ב (ḥ-r-b): sword, dryness, destruction. Shim'on and Levi take "each man his sword" — the sword is the instrument of violence that will define their characters. Yaakov's deathbed oracle (49:5-7) will condemn their violence and scatter their descendants.

### v.30: עֲכַרְתֶּם (ʿakhartem) — "you have troubled me"
Root ע-כ-ר (ʿ-k-r): to trouble, to make turbid, to bring calamity. "You have troubled me, to make me stink (להבאישני, lehav'isheni)." The root עכר is the same as the name עכן (Akhan) in Joshua 7 — the one who "troubled Israel." The verb הבאיש (hiv'ish, to make stink) is from ב-א-ש (b-'-sh, to stink) — Yaakov's reputation now reeks among the inhabitants. His reproach is practical (vulnerability) but also moral.

### v.31: הַכְזוֹנָה (hakhzonah) — "as a harlot"
Root ז-נ-ה (z-n-h): to commit harlotry, to be unfaithful. The brothers' final retort is unanswerable: "Shall he treat our sister as a harlot?" The chapter ends without resolution — no divine voice intervenes, no narrator passes judgment. Yaakov condemns the violence; the brothers defend their sister's honor. Both positions have moral weight. The text leaves the tension standing.

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| ענה (ʿanah) | — | to humble/oppress (cf. ظلم, ẓulm) | 4:75 — prayer for the oppressed |
| חמס (ḥamas) | — | violence (implicit) | 5:32 — "whoever kills a soul... it is as if he killed all of humanity" |
| נבל (naval) | — | disgrace/outrage | Acts that violate the moral order |
| רמה (ramah) | — | deceit/guile | The deception pattern in the family |
| בטח (bataḥ) | — | trust/security (cf. أمان, amān) | The betrayal of trust as a grave sin |
| שלם (shalem) | سلام (salām) | peace/wholeness | v.21 "at wholeness with us" — the false wholeness before the slaughter |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

This chapter presents one of the most morally difficult passages in Genesis. The Bahá'í perspective holds that violence, even in response to a genuine wrong, creates greater wrong. 'Abdu'l-Bahá teaches that revenge and retaliation perpetuate cycles of harm. The sons of Yaakov were right to be outraged by the violation of Dinah, but their response — the massacre of an entire city, using circumcision as a weapon of deception — compounded the original sin many times over.

The chapter is also a warning about the corruption of sacred signs: circumcision, the covenant mark, is weaponized. When religious practices are used as tools of power or deception, they lose their sacred character. Bahá'u'lláh warns: "Tread ye the path of justice, for this, verily, is the straight path."

Yaakov's silence (v.5, "Yaakov held silent until they came") is itself significant. His passivity in the face of the crisis, deferring to his sons, may be part of the problem. Leadership that is silent when it should speak fails its responsibility.

## Integrative Links

- **v.2 the violation <> Qur'an 4:19**: "It is not lawful for you to inherit women by compulsion" — the principle against forced sexual relations
- **v.7 "a disgrace in Yisra'el" <> Qur'an 5:32**: "Whoever kills a soul... it is as if he killed all of humanity; and whoever saves one, it is as if he saved all of humanity" — the individual wrong and the collective response both weighed
- **v.13 "with guile" <> Qur'an 3:54**: "They schemed, and God schemed" — human scheming within the divine perspective
- **v.25 "in its security" <> Qur'an 16:112**: "God struck it with the garment of hunger and fear for what they used to do" — the loss of security as consequence
- **v.31 "shall he treat our sister as a harlot?" <> Qur'an 4:135**: "Be upholders of justice, even against yourselves" — the question of justice that the text leaves open

chapters.title: Bereshit — פרק 35
chapters.subtitle: Genesis — Chapter 35

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר אֱלֹהִים֙ אֶֽל יַעֲקֹ֔ב ק֛וּם עֲלֵ֥ה בֵֽית אֵ֖ל וְ/שֶׁב שָׁ֑ם וַ/עֲשֵׂה שָׁ֣ם מִזְבֵּ֔חַ לָ/אֵל֙ הַ/נִּרְאֶ֣ה אֵלֶ֔י/ךָ בְּ/בָרְחֲ/ךָ֔ מִ/פְּנֵ֖י עֵשָׂ֥ו אָחִֽי/ךָ
verses.en: And God said to Yaakov: "Arise, go up to Beit-El and dwell there; and make there an altar to the God who appeared to you in your fleeing from the face of Esav your brother."
verses.ar: وقالَ اللهُ لِيَعقوبَ: «قُمِ اصعَدْ إلى بَيتِ إيلَ واسكُنْ هُناكَ؛ واصنَعْ هُناكَ مَذبَحًا لِلإلهِ الَّذي تَراءى لَكَ في فِرارِكَ مِن وَجهِ عيسو أخيكَ.»

verses.he: וַ/יֹּ֤אמֶר יַעֲקֹב֙ אֶל בֵּית֔/וֹ וְ/אֶ֖ל כָּל אֲשֶׁ֣ר עִמּ֑/וֹ הָסִ֜רוּ אֶת אֱלֹהֵ֤י הַ/נֵּכָר֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּ/תֹכְ/כֶ֔ם וְ/הִֽטַּהֲר֔וּ וְ/הַחֲלִ֖יפוּ שִׂמְלֹתֵי/כֶֽם
verses.en: And Yaakov said to his household and to all who were with him: "Put away the gods of the foreigner that are in your midst, and purify yourselves, and change your garments.
verses.ar: فقالَ يَعقوبُ لِأهلِ بَيتِهِ ولِكُلِّ مَن مَعَهُ: «أزيلوا آلِهَةَ الغُرَباءِ الَّتي في وَسَطِكُم، وتَطَهَّروا وبَدِّلوا ثِيابَكُم.

verses.he: וְ/נָק֥וּמָה וְ/נַעֲלֶ֖ה בֵּֽית אֵ֑ל וְ/אֶֽעֱשֶׂה שָּׁ֣ם מִזְבֵּ֗חַ לָ/אֵ֞ל הָ/עֹנֶ֤ה אֹתִ/י֙ בְּ/י֣וֹם צָֽרָתִ֔/י וַ/יְהִי֙ עִמָּדִ֔/י בַּ/דֶּ֖רֶךְ אֲשֶׁ֥ר הָלָֽכְתִּי
verses.en: And let us arise and go up to Beit-El, and I will make there an altar to the God who answered me in the day of my distress, and who was with me in the way that I went."
verses.ar: ولِنَقُمْ ونَصعَدْ إلى بَيتِ إيلَ، فأصنَعَ هُناكَ مَذبَحًا لِلإلهِ الَّذي أجابَني في يَومِ ضيقي، وكانَ مَعي في الطَّريقِ الَّتي سَلَكتُ.»

verses.he: וַ/יִּתְּנ֣וּ אֶֽל יַעֲקֹ֗ב אֵ֣ת כָּל אֱלֹהֵ֤י הַ/נֵּכָר֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּ/יָדָ֔/ם וְ/אֶת הַ/נְּזָמִ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּ/אָזְנֵי/הֶ֑ם וַ/יִּטְמֹ֤ן אֹתָ/ם֙ יַעֲקֹ֔ב תַּ֥חַת הָ/אֵלָ֖ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר עִם שְׁכֶֽם
verses.en: And they gave to Yaakov all the gods of the foreigner that were in their hands, and the rings that were in their ears; and Yaakov hid them under the oak that was near Shekhem.
verses.ar: فأعطَوا يَعقوبَ كُلَّ آلِهَةِ الغُرَباءِ الَّتي في أيديهِم والحِلَقِ الَّتي في آذانِهِم؛ فَطَمَرَها يَعقوبُ تَحتَ البَلّوطَةِ الَّتي عِندَ شَكيمَ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּסָּ֑עוּ וַ/יְהִ֣י חִתַּ֣ת אֱלֹהִ֗ים עַל הֶֽ/עָרִים֙ אֲשֶׁר֙ סְבִיבֹ֣תֵי/הֶ֔ם וְ/לֹ֣א רָֽדְפ֔וּ אַחֲרֵ֖י בְּנֵ֥י יַעֲקֹֽב
verses.en: And they journeyed; and a terror of God was upon the cities that were round about them, and they did not pursue after the sons of Yaakov.
verses.ar: ورَحَلوا؛ وكانَ رُعبُ اللهِ على المُدُنِ الَّتي حَولَهُم فَلَم يَلحَقوا بِبَني يَعقوبَ.

verses.he: וַ/יָּבֹ֨א יַעֲקֹ֜ב ל֗וּזָ/ה אֲשֶׁר֙ בְּ/אֶ֣רֶץ כְּנַ֔עַן הִ֖וא בֵּֽית אֵ֑ל ה֖וּא וְ/כָל הָ/עָ֥ם אֲשֶׁר עִמּֽ/וֹ
verses.en: And Yaakov came to Luz, which is in the land of Kena'an — it is Beit-El — he and all the people who were with him.
verses.ar: وجاءَ يَعقوبُ إلى لوزَ الَّتي في أرضِ كَنعانَ — هِيَ بَيتُ إيلَ — هُوَ وكُلُّ القَومِ الَّذينَ مَعَهُ.

verses.he: וַ/יִּ֤בֶן שָׁם֙ מִזְבֵּ֔חַ וַ/יִּקְרָא֙ לַ/מָּק֔וֹם אֵ֖ל בֵּֽית אֵ֑ל כִּ֣י שָׁ֗ם נִגְל֤וּ אֵלָי/ו֙ הָֽ/אֱלֹהִ֔ים בְּ/בָרְח֖/וֹ מִ/פְּנֵ֥י אָחִֽי/ו
verses.en: And he built there an altar, and he called the place El Beit-El; for there God had been revealed to him, in his fleeing from the face of his brother.
verses.ar: وبَنى هُناكَ مَذبَحًا وسَمّى المَوضِعَ إيلَ بَيتِ إيلَ؛ لِأنَّ اللهَ تَجَلّى لَهُ هُناكَ في فِرارِهِ مِن وَجهِ أخيهِ.

verses.he: וַ/תָּ֤מָת דְּבֹרָה֙ מֵינֶ֣קֶת רִבְקָ֔ה וַ/תִּקָּבֵ֛ר מִ/תַּ֥חַת לְ/בֵֽית אֵ֖ל תַּ֣חַת הָֽ/אַלּ֑וֹן וַ/יִּקְרָ֥א שְׁמ֖/וֹ אַלּ֥וֹן בָּכֽוּת
